description: Unique identifier for the DOT hazardous materials classification record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to billing.surcharge. Business justification: DOT hazmat classifications (hazard class, packing group, placard requirements) trigger specific billing surcharges (hazmat handling surcharge, placard surcharge, emergency response surcharge). Billing
Maximum net quantity per package permitted on cargo-only aircraft. May be expressed as weight (kg), volume (L), or 'Forbidden' if not permitted.
Date when this classification entry became effective in the Hazardous Materials Regulations. Used to track regulatory changes over time.
Current regulatory status of this classification entry. Active = currently in force, Superseded = replaced by newer entry, Withdrawn = no longer valid.
Timestamp when this classification record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Three-digit guide number from the DOT Emergency Response Guidebook that provides first responders with initial action guidance for incidents involving this material.
Code (E0-E5) indicating the maximum quantity per inner packaging permitted under excepted quantity provisions, which allow minimal regulatory requirements for very small quantities.
Reference to regulatory sections (e.g., 173.###) that provide exceptions or reduced requirements for limited quantities, small quantities, or specific transport modes.
Indicates whether transport of this material on cargo-only aircraft is prohibited under any circumstances.
Indicates whether transport of this material on passenger-carrying aircraft is prohibited under any circumstances.

Primary hazard class or division (1-9) assigned to the material based on its predominant hazard. Examples: 1.1 (explosives), 2.1 (flammable gas), 3 (flammable liquid), 4.1 (flammable solid), 5.1 (oxidizer), 6.1 (toxic), 7 (radioactive), 8 (corrosive), 9 (miscellaneous).
Subdivision within a hazard class that further categorizes the material by specific hazard characteristics (e.g., 1.1, 1.2, 2.1, 2.2, 6.1). May be null for classes without divisions.
For materials that are poisonous by inhalation, the hazard zone (A, B, C, or D) indicating the degree of inhalation toxicity. Zone A is the most toxic.

Code(s) identifying the required hazard warning label(s) that must be affixed to packages. Multiple labels may be required for materials with subsidiary hazards. Examples: 3 (flammable liquid), 6.1 (poison), 8 (corrosive).
Timestamp when this classification record was most recently updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Indicates whether this material is eligible for limited quantity exceptions, which allow reduced packaging, marking, and labeling requirements when transported in small quantities.
Maximum net quantity per inner receptacle or article permitted under limited quantity provisions. Expressed as weight or volume.

Indicates whether the material is designated as a marine pollutant under DOT and International Maritime Dangerous Goods (IMDG) Code regulations. Requires special marking and handling for water transport.
Additional notes, clarifications, or special instructions related to this classification entry. May include references to specific regulatory amendments or interpretations.
Codes referencing the authorized packaging sections (e.g., 173.###) that specify which container types and specifications are approved for this material.
Degree of danger presented by the material during transport. I = high danger, II = medium danger, III = low danger. Used to determine packaging requirements and quantity limits.
Maximum net quantity per package permitted on passenger-carrying aircraft. May be expressed as weight (kg), volume (L), or 'Forbidden' if not permitted.
Code identifying the specific placard design required for the transport vehicle. Examples: FLAMMABLE, CORROSIVE, POISON, OXIDIZER, DANGEROUS.
Indicates whether placards must be displayed on the transport vehicle for this material when transported in specified quantities.
The official DOT-approved name used to describe the hazardous material on shipping papers, manifests, and labels. Must match the Hazardous Materials Table entry exactly.
The threshold quantity (in pounds) at or above which a spill or release of this material must be reported to the National Response Center under CERCLA. Null if no RQ applies.
Indicates whether the material is classified as a severe marine pollutant (PP designation) requiring enhanced environmental protection measures during marine transport.
Comma-separated list of special provision codes (e.g., B1, IB2, T4, TP1) that impose additional requirements or grant exceptions for packaging, handling, or transport of this material.
Secondary hazard class(es) for materials that present more than one type of hazard. Multiple subsidiary classes may be listed, separated by commas.
If this classification has been superseded, the UN/NA number of the replacement classification entry. Null if status is active.
Indicates whether the technical or chemical name of the hazardous constituent(s) must be entered in parentheses after the proper shipping name on shipping papers.
Four-digit identification number assigned by the United Nations or North America to hazardous materials for international and domestic transport. Format: UN#### or NA####.
Code indicating where the material must be stowed on a vessel (e.g., A = on deck only, B = under deck, C = on or under deck). Applies to marine transport.
Additional stowage requirements or restrictions for marine transport, such as segregation from other materials, ventilation requirements, or temperature controls.

business_glossary_term: EPA (Environmental Protection Agency) ID Registration ID
description: Unique identifier for the EPA identification number registration record.

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulated_facility. Business justification: EPA ID registrations and regulated_facility records represent two views of the same regulated entity. Linking enables cross-referencing EPA registration data (handler_type, generator_classification) w

business_glossary_term: Authorized State RCRA (Resource Conservation and Recovery Act) Program
description: Indicates whether the state has an EPA-authorized RCRA program and administers the program in lieu of the federal EPA.

The 12-character EPA identification number assigned to the regulated site (format: 2-letter state code + 9 digits). This is the regulatory identity anchor for all RCRA (Resource Conservation and Recovery Act) compliance activities.
reference: EPA RCRA Subtitle C; 40 CFR Part 262

description: Email address for the facility contact person for regulatory correspondence.
Name of the primary contact person for regulatory correspondence and compliance matters at the registered facility.
Primary phone number for the facility contact person.

Generator size classification: LQG (Large Quantity Generator, >1000 kg/month), SQG (Small Quantity Generator, 100-1000 kg/month), VSQG (Very Small Quantity Generator, <100 kg/month), CESQG (Conditionally Exempt Small Quantity Generator, legacy term), or not_applicable for non-generators.
reference: 40 CFR Part 262.13; 40 CFR Part 262.14; 40 CFR Part 262.15

description: Current operational status of the EPA ID registration: active (currently operating), inactive (not currently handling hazardous waste), pending (application submitted), withdrawn (voluntarily terminated), or suspended (regulatory action).

business_glossary_term: RCRA (Resource Conservation and Recovery Act) Handler Type
description: Primary regulated activity type for this EPA ID: generator (produces hazardous waste), transporter (hauls hazardous waste), TSDF (Treatment Storage and Disposal Facility), or mixed (multiple activities).
reference: EPA RCRA Subtitle C; 40 CFR Part 262

description: Indicates whether this EPA ID is registered as a hazardous waste generator.
Indicates whether this EPA ID is registered as a hazardous waste transporter.

business_glossary_term: Is TSDF (Treatment Storage and Disposal Facility)
description: Indicates whether this EPA ID is registered as a Treatment, Storage, and Disposal Facility for hazardous waste.
Geographic latitude coordinate of the regulated site in decimal degrees.
Method used to verify the geographic coordinates of the site: GPS (field measurement), address_geocoding (automated), survey (professional land survey), or map_interpolation (manual map reading).
Geographic longitude coordinate of the regulated site in decimal degrees.

business_glossary_term: NAICS (North American Industry Classification System) Code
description: Six-digit NAICS code representing the primary business activity of the facility.
reference: U.S. Census Bureau NAICS; EPA Form 8700-12

description: Free-text notes or comments regarding this EPA ID registration, including special conditions, regulatory variances, or operational context.
Date when the EPA ID registration became effective and the site was authorized to conduct regulated activities.

business_glossary_term: RCRA (Resource Conservation and Recovery Act) Notification Submission Date
description: Date when the RCRA notification form (EPA Form 8700-12) was submitted to the EPA or authorized state agency.

business_glossary_term: RCRA (Resource Conservation and Recovery Act) Notification Type
description: Type of RCRA notification form submission: initial (first-time registration), subsequent (periodic update), amendment (correction to existing registration), or withdrawal (deregistration).
Legal name of the entity that owns or operates the regulated facility.
Type of entity that owns or operates the facility: private (commercial), public (publicly traded), federal (U.S. government), state (state government), or municipal (local government).
Timestamp of the last successful data synchronization with the EPA RCRAInfo system.
Status of data synchronization with the EPA RCRAInfo national database: synced (up to date), pending (sync in progress), failed (sync error), or not_synced (not yet synchronized).
Timestamp when this EPA ID registration record was first created in the system.
Timestamp when this EPA ID registration record was last updated in the system.
Date when the EPA ID registration expires and must be renewed (if applicable based on state requirements).
Four-digit SIC code representing the primary business activity of the facility (legacy classification system).
reference: U.S. Department of Labor SIC; EPA Form 8700-12

description: Primary street address of the regulated site.
Secondary address information (suite, building, floor) for the regulated site.
City where the regulated site is located.
Three-letter ISO country code for the regulated site location.
County where the regulated site is located.
The legal or trade name of the regulated facility or site registered with the EPA.
ZIP code or ZIP+4 code for the regulated site address.
Two-letter state code where the regulated site is located.
System of record where this EPA ID registration was originally captured: Enviance EHS, SAP EHS module, EPA RCRAInfo, or manual entry.
Date when the state environmental agency was notified of this EPA ID registration.
Indicates whether the state environmental quality department or local enforcement agency has been notified of this EPA ID registration.
State-issued identification number for hazardous waste handling (if different from EPA ID and required by state regulations).
Unique identifier for the hazardous waste generator record. Primary key for the entity.

Foreign key linking to service.area. Business justification: Generator-to-service-area assignment drives hazmat collection route planning, regulatory jurisdiction determination, and franchise compliance reporting. Dispatchers must know which service area a gene

Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: Each hazardous waste generator facility maps to a billing account for invoicing hazmat collection and disposal services. AR teams require this link to identify which billing account to charge for a ge

Foreign key linking to hazmat.epa_id_registration. Business justification: Hazardous waste generator is registered with EPA and has an EPA ID. The epa_id_number string is a denormalized copy of data in epa_id_registration. Adding FK to epa_id_registration normalizes this rel

Foreign key linking to asset.facility. Business justification: Generator sites ARE facilities in waste management operations. Links generator regulatory records to facility asset master for permit tracking, inspection scheduling, facility utilization reporting, a

Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Generators operate under RCRA permits (40 CFR 262). Permit governs accumulation limits, reporting, and operational requirements. Core regulatory relationship for compliance tracking and inspection pla

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulated_facility. Business justification: Every RCRA hazardous waste generator is a regulated facility. Linking enables compliance officers to access the full regulatory risk profile, active violations count, and applicable regulatory program

Maximum number of days hazardous waste may be accumulated on-site before shipment, based on generator category. LQG: 90 days; SQG: 180 days (or 270 if >200 miles from TSDF); VSQG: no time limit but quantity limits apply.
Indicates whether the generator is required to submit biennial hazardous waste reports to EPA. Required for LQGs and some SQGs.
reference: RCRA 40 CFR 262.41, EPA Biennial Report Requirements

description: Current compliance status with RCRA and state hazardous waste regulations. Reflects findings from most recent inspection or enforcement action.
reference: RCRA 40 CFR 262, EPA Enforcement and Compliance History Online (ECHO)

description: Date when the contingency plan was last reviewed and updated. Plans must be reviewed annually and updated as needed.
Reference identifier or document location for the site's hazardous waste contingency plan. Required for LQGs and SQGs.
Name of the designated emergency response contact for incidents involving hazardous waste at this site.
reference: RCRA 40 CFR 262.34, OSHA HAZWOPER 29 CFR 1910.120, DOT HMR 49 CFR 172.604

description: 24-hour emergency phone number for hazardous waste incidents. Must be staffed or monitored continuously.
reference: RCRA 40 CFR 262.34, OSHA HAZWOPER 29 CFR 1910.120, DOT HMR 49 CFR 172.604

description: Total number of formal enforcement actions (notices of violation, administrative orders, civil penalties) issued against this generator.
reference: RCRA 40 CFR 262, EPA Enforcement and Compliance History Online (ECHO)

description: Indicates whether the generator is a federal government facility (e.g., military base, federal laboratory). Federal facilities have specific RCRA compliance requirements.
reference: RCRA 40 CFR 262.10, Federal Facility Compliance Act

description: EPA classification of generator based on monthly waste generation volume. LQG (Large Quantity Generator): ≥1,000 kg/month; SQG (Small Quantity Generator): 100-1,000 kg/month; VSQG (Very Small Quantity Generator): <100 kg/month; CESQG (Conditionally Exempt Small Quantity Generator): legacy term for VSQG.
The official legal name of the hazardous waste generating entity as registered with EPA and state authorities.
Current operational and regulatory status of the hazardous waste generator registration.
reference: RCRA 40 CFR 262, State Environmental Quality Departments

description: Date when the generator first notified EPA of hazardous waste generation activities and received EPA ID number.
Expected frequency of regulatory inspections by EPA or state authorities, in months. Varies by generator category and compliance history.
reference: RCRA 40 CFR 262, State Environmental Quality Departments

description: The reporting year of the most recently submitted biennial hazardous waste report (e.g., 2022 for the 2021-2022 reporting period).
Date of the most recent regulatory inspection by EPA, state, or local enforcement agency.
reference: RCRA 40 CFR 262, State Environmental Quality Departments

description: Date of the most recent update to the generator's EPA notification information. Generators must notify EPA of changes within 30 days.
Geographic latitude coordinate of the generator site in decimal degrees. Used for spatial analysis and emergency response planning.
Geographic longitude coordinate of the generator site in decimal degrees. Used for spatial analysis and emergency response planning.
Mailing address for correspondence and regulatory notifications if different from site address.

business_glossary_term: North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) Code
description: Six-digit NAICS code identifying the primary business activity of the generator. Used for regulatory reporting and waste generation profiling.
reference: U.S. Census Bureau NAICS, RCRA Biennial Report Requirements

description: Email address of the primary contact for electronic manifest notifications and regulatory correspondence.
Full name of the primary contact person responsible for hazardous waste management at the generator site.
Primary phone number for the generator contact. Used for operational coordination and emergency response.
Job title or role of the primary contact person (e.g., Environmental Manager, EHS Coordinator).
Timestamp when this generator record was first created in the system.
Name of the source system from which this generator record originated (e.g., Enviance EHS, SAP EHS, EPA RCRAInfo).
Timestamp when this generator record was last modified.
Four-digit SIC code for the generator's primary business activity. Legacy classification system still used in some regulatory contexts.
reference: OSHA SIC Manual, EPA Reporting Requirements

description: City where the hazardous waste generation site is located.
County where the generator site is located. Required for local enforcement agency (LEA) coordination.
reference: State Environmental Quality Departments, Local Enforcement Agency Requirements

description: Two-letter state code for the generator site location. Used for jurisdictional compliance determination.
reference: RCRA 40 CFR 262, State Environmental Quality Departments

description: Physical street address of the hazardous waste generation site. Required for manifest preparation and emergency response.
ZIP code for the generator site. Supports 5-digit or 9-digit (ZIP+4) format.
State-specific generator identification number if different from EPA ID. Some states maintain parallel registration systems.
reference: State Environmental Quality Departments, State RCRA Authorization Programs

description: Indicates whether the generator has a documented waste minimization plan in place. Required for LQGs under some state programs.
reference: RCRA 40 CFR 262.41, State Hazardous Waste Reduction Programs

business_glossary_term: Resource Conservation and Recovery Act (RCRA) Biennial Report ID
description: Unique identifier for the RCRA Biennial Report submission record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to contract.disposal_agreement. Business justification: RCRA biennial reports must reconcile total waste shipped offsite against contracted disposal agreements. Direct link enables regulators and compliance officers to verify reported disposal tonnage matc

Foreign key linking to hazmat.epa_id_registration. Business justification: RCRA biennial report is filed by an EPA-registered facility. The epa_id_number string duplicates epa_id_registration data. Adding FK normalizes this relationship. Remove epa_id_number as it becomes re

Foreign key linking to hazmat.hazardous_waste_generator. Business justification: The RCRA Biennial Report is filed by and for a specific hazardous waste generator. While epa_id_registration_id exists, a direct FK to hazardous_waste_generator links the report to the generator maste

Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_code. Business justification: rcra_biennial_report.primary_waste_code is a STRING field storing the waste code value. Normalizing this to a FK to waste_code.waste_code_id replaces the denormalized string with a proper reference to

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: RCRA biennial reports fulfill specific regulatory requirements under RCRA Section 3002. Linking rcra_biennial_report to regulatory_requirement enables compliance obligation tracking — verifying the bi

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_submission. Business justification: Biennial reports (40 CFR 262.41) ARE regulatory submissions. Links hazmat-specific report content to general submission tracking for deadline management, confirmation numbers, and agency response trac

Foreign key linking to asset.facility. Business justification: Biennial reports are submitted per facility to EPA. Links regulatory reports to facility asset master for compliance tracking, facility performance benchmarking, audit trails, and facility-level waste

Foreign key linking to service.waste_stream. Business justification: RCRA biennial reports must classify waste generation by waste stream category per EPA requirements. Linking to waste_stream enables automated report population, cross-report consistency validation, an
The date on which an amended version of the biennial report was submitted. Nullable if no amendment has been filed.

Explanation for why the biennial report was amended after initial submission. Nullable if the report has not been amended. Common reasons include data corrections, additional waste codes discovered, or regulatory agency feedback.
The date on which the authorized representative signed and certified the biennial report, attesting to its accuracy under penalty of law.
Job title of the authorized facility representative who certified the biennial report (e.g., Environmental Manager, Facility Director, Compliance Officer).
Additional notes, clarifications, or context related to the biennial report submission. May include explanations of unusual waste volumes, operational changes, or regulatory correspondence.
The date and time when this biennial report record was first created in the system. Audit trail field.

The regulatory classification of the waste generator at the time of reporting. LQG = Large Quantity Generator (≥1000 kg/month), SQG = Small Quantity Generator (100-1000 kg/month), CESQG/VSQG = Conditionally Exempt/Very Small Quantity Generator (<100 kg/month).
The date and time when this biennial report record was most recently updated. Audit trail field for tracking changes.

business_glossary_term: Number of Treatment Storage and Disposal Facility (TSDF) Destinations
description: The count of distinct off-site TSDF facilities to which hazardous waste was shipped during the reporting year. Each TSDF is identified by its EPA ID number.
The count of distinct EPA hazardous waste codes (e.g., D001, F001, K001) reported in this biennial report. Each waste stream is classified by one or more waste codes.
Indicates whether the facility shipped any hazardous waste off-site to TSDFs during the reporting year. True = off-site shipments occurred, False = all waste managed on-site.

Indicates whether the facility performed any on-site treatment, storage, or disposal of hazardous waste during the reporting year. True = on-site management occurred, False = all waste shipped off-site.
The predominant treatment, storage, or disposal method used for hazardous waste during the reporting year. Examples: incineration, stabilization, landfill, fuel blending, recycling, neutralization.
Textual description of the primary waste stream corresponding to the primary waste code. Describes the waste source, composition, and hazardous characteristics.
The even-numbered calendar year for which this biennial report is submitted (e.g., 2022, 2024). Reports are required every two years per RCRA regulations.
The operational system from which this biennial report data was sourced. Typically Enviance EHS regulatory reporting module or SAP EHS module.
Two-letter abbreviation of the state environmental agency to which this biennial report was submitted. Corresponds to the state where the facility is located.
The total quantity of RCRA hazardous waste generated during the reporting year, measured in pounds. Aggregated across all waste codes managed by the facility.
The total quantity of hazardous waste treated, stored, or disposed of on-site at the generator facility during the reporting year, measured in pounds.
The total quantity of hazardous waste shipped off-site to Treatment, Storage, and Disposal Facilities (TSDFs) during the reporting year, measured in pounds.
Indicates whether the facility achieved measurable waste minimization or source reduction during the reporting year. True = minimization achieved, False = no reduction.
Narrative description of waste minimization, source reduction, and pollution prevention activities undertaken during the reporting year. Required disclosure per RCRA biennial reporting requirements.
Unique identifier for the waste code record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to hazmat.dot_hazmat_classification. Business justification: waste_code has dot_hazard_class (STRING) as a denormalized field. Adding dot_hazmat_classification_id normalizes the DOT hazard class reference to the dot_hazmat_classification master record, enabling

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Each RCRA waste code is defined by and subject to specific regulatory requirements (LDR standards, treatment standards, reporting obligations). waste_code.regulatory_citation is a denormalized referen

Indicates whether this waste code is currently active and should be used for new waste classifications. True for codes in current regulatory use. False for obsolete, superseded, or delisted codes that are retained for historical reference only.

Indicates whether this waste code designates an acutely hazardous waste under RCRA. True for P-listed wastes and certain F-listed wastes that are subject to more stringent quantity thresholds (1 kg vs 100 kg for generator status determination) and management requirements.

Specific container or packaging requirements for this waste code during storage and transportation. May specify DOT-approved containers, compatibility requirements, or prohibited container materials. Critical for safe handling and DOT Hazardous Materials Regulations compliance.
reference: 49 CFR Parts 172-173 - DOT Hazardous Materials Regulations

description: Timestamp when this waste code record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Indicates whether generators may petition EPA to delist this waste code for specific waste streams that do not exhibit hazardous characteristics. True for listed wastes (F, K, P, U codes) where site-specific delisting is possible. False for characteristic wastes (D codes) which cannot be delisted.

Date when this waste code became effective under federal or state regulations. Used to determine applicability to historical waste streams and to track regulatory changes over time. Critical for legacy waste management and compliance audits.

Maximum number of days a generator may accumulate this waste on-site before shipping to a TSDF. Typically 90 days for large quantity generators, 180 days for small quantity generators, or 270 days with approved extension. Shorter limits (e.g., 1 day) may apply to acutely hazardous wastes.

Primary hazard characteristic that qualifies this waste as hazardous under RCRA. Ignitability (D001) covers flammable liquids and oxidizers. Corrosivity (D002) covers acids and bases. Reactivity (D003) covers unstable or explosive materials. Toxicity (D004-D043) covers materials that leach toxic constituents. Listed wastes are hazardous by definition regardless of characteristics. Multiple indicates waste exhibits more than one characteristic.
Timestamp when this waste code record was last updated. Tracks regulatory changes, data corrections, and reference updates. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.

Additional operational notes, clarifications, or internal guidance for using this waste code. May include common misclassifications to avoid, industry-specific interpretations, or cross-references to related codes. Free-text field for subject matter expert annotations.

Management method codes that are prohibited for this waste code under Land Disposal Restrictions. Typically references disposal methods that are banned without prior treatment (e.g., landfill disposal, deep well injection). Comma-separated list if multiple methods are prohibited.

Indicates whether this waste code has known recycling or reclamation pathways that may qualify for reduced regulatory requirements under RCRA recycling exemptions. True if legitimate recycling options exist (e.g., solvent recovery, metal reclamation, fuel blending).

CERCLA reportable quantity in pounds. If a spill or release of this waste equals or exceeds this quantity, it must be reported to the National Response Center immediately. Values typically range from 1 lb (most hazardous) to 5,000 lbs. Null if no RQ is assigned.

Quantity threshold in kilograms below which a generator may qualify for reduced regulatory requirements. Typically 100 kg per month for hazardous waste or 1 kg per month for acutely hazardous waste. Used to determine generator category (VSQG, SQG, LQG).

Two-letter state abbreviation for state-specific waste codes. Null for federal EPA codes. Used to identify which state jurisdiction defines and enforces this code. Critical for multi-state operations to ensure compliance with local regulations.

Indicates whether this waste code is a state-specific designation beyond federal EPA requirements. True when the code is defined by state environmental regulations and may not be recognized in other states. False for federal EPA codes that apply nationwide.

Date when this waste code was or will be removed from regulatory lists. Null for active codes. Populated when EPA or state agencies delist a waste code or when state-specific codes are superseded. Historical record retention required for audit trails.
reference: Federal Register or State Regulatory Updates

description: Specific toxic constituent measured in TCLP testing for toxicity characteristic wastes. Examples include arsenic, barium, cadmium, chromium, lead, mercury, selenium, silver, benzene, trichloroethylene. Only populated for D-code toxicity characteristic wastes (D004-D043).

business_glossary_term: Toxicity Characteristic Leaching Procedure (TCLP) Regulatory Level (mg/L)
description: Maximum concentration in milligrams per liter of TCLP extract that triggers hazardous waste classification for toxicity characteristic wastes. Waste exceeding this level must be managed as hazardous. Only applicable to D-code toxicity characteristic wastes.

Indicates whether this waste code is based on Toxicity Characteristic Leaching Procedure (TCLP) test results. True for D004-D043 codes where waste is hazardous if TCLP extract exceeds regulatory levels for specific contaminants. Requires periodic testing to confirm classification.

Land Disposal Restrictions (LDR) treatment standard code that applies to this waste before land disposal. References 40 CFR Part 268 treatment standards. Specifies required treatment technology or concentration limits (e.g., DEACT for deactivation, IMERC for incineration, specific mg/L or mg/kg limits for TCLP constituents).

Subcategory designation for wastes with multiple treatment standards based on waste form or concentration. Used when a single waste code has different treatment requirements for wastewater versus non-wastewater forms, or for different concentration ranges.

Universal Treatment Standard concentration limits for underlying hazardous constituents. Specifies maximum allowable concentrations (in mg/L for TCLP or mg/kg total) for each constituent before the waste can be land disposed. Multiple constituents may be listed with their respective limits.

EPA or state hazardous waste code identifier. Format follows EPA classification: D-codes (D001-D043 for characteristic wastes), F-codes (F001-F039 for non-specific source wastes), K-codes (K001-K181 for specific source wastes), P-codes (P001-P205 for acutely hazardous commercial chemical products), U-codes (U001-U411 for toxic commercial chemical products). This is the business identifier used across manifests, profiles, and regulatory reports.
reference: 40 CFR Part 261 - Identification and Listing of Hazardous Waste

description: Classification category of the waste code. D-listed are characteristic wastes (ignitability, corrosivity, reactivity, toxicity). F-listed are wastes from non-specific sources. K-listed are wastes from specific sources. P-listed are acutely hazardous discarded commercial chemical products. U-listed are toxic discarded commercial chemical products. State-specific codes are additional classifications imposed by state environmental agencies.
reference: 40 CFR Part 261 Subparts C and D

description: Detailed narrative description of the waste stream or material covered by this code. Includes physical characteristics, chemical composition, source process, and typical examples. Used by generators and TSDFs to match waste streams to appropriate codes.

Code indicating applicable waste minimization or source reduction strategies for this waste stream. References EPA waste minimization guidance and best management practices. Used in biennial reports and pollution prevention planning.
Unique identifier for the waste characterization profile. Primary key for the waste profile entity.

business_glossary_term: Approved TSDF (Treatment Storage and Disposal Facility) Facility ID
description: Reference to the Treatment, Storage, and Disposal Facility authorized to accept this waste profile. Links to facility master data.

Foreign key linking to billing.billing_rate_schedule. Business justification: Waste profile characteristics (hazard class, treatment method, physical state) directly determine the applicable billing rate schedule for hazmat services. Billing teams use waste profile data to sele

Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Waste profiles are approved for customer accounts under master service agreements defining permitted waste streams, pricing basis, approved TSDFs. Profile approval validates contract scope, permitted

Foreign key linking to hazmat.dot_hazmat_classification. Business justification: Waste profile has un_na_number (string) but no FK to dot_hazmat_classification reference table. Adding FK provides standardized DOT classification lookup including proper shipping name, hazard class,
Reference to the customer or facility that generates this waste stream. Links to the waste generator profile in the customer domain.

Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: Waste profiles document approved waste characterizations for specific service offerings. Service order fulfillment and pricing validation require linking profiles to catalog offerings. Real business p

Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Waste profiles must comply with facility permit conditions. Approved waste streams are constrained by permit scope (waste codes, treatment methods). Required for permit compliance verification and TSD

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Waste profile approvals are governed by specific RCRA regulatory requirements (LDR treatment standards, land disposal restrictions). Linking enables compliance officers to verify that approved treatme

Foreign key linking to service.service_waste_stream. Business justification: Waste profiles characterize specific waste streams. Links regulatory/technical characterization to service catalog waste stream classification. Real business process: proper service routing, pricing d
Estimated annual quantity of waste expected to be generated under this profile, measured in tons. Used for capacity planning and permit compliance.
Current approval status of the waste profile. Waste cannot be accepted for treatment or disposal until profile is approved.
Date and time when the waste profile was approved for acceptance.
List of specific chemical constituents or contaminants present in the waste that drive its hazardous classification (e.g., 'Lead, Chromium, Toluene').
reference: 40 CFR Part 261 Appendix VIII - Hazardous Constituents

description: Approved container types for this waste stream (e.g., '55-gallon steel drum', 'Bulk tanker', 'Fiber drum', 'Supersack'). Multiple types may be listed.
Date and time when the waste profile record was first created in the system.
Department of Transportation hazard classification for transportation purposes (e.g., Class 3 Flammable Liquid, Class 6.1 Toxic, Class 8 Corrosive, Class 9 Miscellaneous).
Summary of emergency response procedures in case of spill, release, or exposure incident involving this waste material.
Lowest temperature at which vapors ignite when exposed to an ignition source. Used to determine ignitability characteristic (D001: flash point <60°C).
Indicates whether the waste exhibits the characteristic of corrosivity per EPA D002 criteria (aqueous with pH ≤2 or ≥12.5, or liquid that corrodes steel at rate >6.35mm per year at 55°C).

Indicates whether the waste exhibits the characteristic of ignitability per EPA D001 criteria (liquid with flash point <60°C, non-liquid capable of spontaneous combustion, ignitable compressed gas, or oxidizer).

Indicates whether the waste exhibits the characteristic of reactivity per EPA D003 criteria (unstable, reacts violently with water, generates toxic gases, contains cyanide or sulfide, or capable of detonation).
Indicates whether the waste exhibits the toxicity characteristic per TCLP testing, with contaminant concentrations exceeding regulatory thresholds (D004-D043).
Indicates whether the waste is subject to Land Disposal Restrictions requiring treatment to meet specific standards before landfill disposal.
Date of the most recent periodic review of this waste profile. Profiles should be reviewed annually or when waste characteristics change.
Specific treatment standards that must be met before land disposal, including concentration limits for hazardous constituents or required treatment technologies.
Date and time when the waste profile record was last modified.
Additional notes, comments, or special considerations related to this waste profile.
DOT packing group indicating degree of danger: I (great danger), II (medium danger), III (minor danger). Used for transportation classification.
Measured pH level of the waste material. Critical for determining corrosivity characteristic (D002: pH ≤2 or ≥12.5).
Physical form of the waste material at ambient temperature and pressure.
Personal protective equipment required when handling this waste (e.g., 'Level B, respirator, chemical-resistant gloves, face shield').
Description of the industrial or commercial process that generates this waste stream (e.g., 'Metal degreasing operation', 'Paint booth cleaning').
Date when the waste profile becomes effective and waste shipments under this profile may be accepted.
Date when the waste profile expires and must be renewed or re-approved. Typically profiles are valid for 1-3 years depending on facility requirements.
Descriptive name of the waste stream or material being profiled (e.g., 'Industrial Solvent Waste', 'Lead-Contaminated Soil').
Business identifier for the waste profile, used for external reference and tracking. Typically formatted as WP- followed by numeric sequence.
DOT-approved proper shipping name for the hazardous material as it must appear on shipping papers and manifests (e.g., 'Waste Flammable Liquid, N.O.S.').
Any special handling, storage, or safety requirements for this waste stream (e.g., 'Keep refrigerated', 'Incompatible with oxidizers', 'Requires secondary containment').

business_glossary_term: TCLP (Toxicity Characteristic Leaching Procedure) Results Summary
description: Summary of TCLP test results listing detected contaminants and their concentrations in mg/L. Used to determine D-code assignments for toxic characteristics.

business_glossary_term: TCLP (Toxicity Characteristic Leaching Procedure) Test Date
description: Date when the most recent Toxicity Characteristic Leaching Procedure laboratory test was performed to determine if waste exhibits toxicity characteristic.
Approved treatment or disposal method for this waste stream (e.g., 'Incineration', 'Stabilization', 'Landfill Disposal', 'Fuel Blending').
Four-digit United Nations or North America identification number assigned to the hazardous material for transportation purposes (e.g., UN1203, NA1993).
Detailed description of the waste material including source process, composition, contaminants, and any special handling considerations.

business_glossary_term: Waste Profile Code Listing - Waste Profile Code Listing Id
description: Primary key for the waste_profile_code_listing association

business_glossary_term: Waste Profile Code Listing - Waste Code Id
description: Foreign key linking to the EPA or state hazardous waste code assigned to this profile.

business_glossary_term: Waste Profile Code Listing - Waste Profile Id
description: Foreign key linking to the waste characterization profile to which this waste code applies.

The specific reason or measured property that caused this waste code to apply to this waste profile. For characteristic codes (D-codes), this captures the measured property (e.g., flash point, pH, TCLP result). For listed codes (F/K/P/U), this captures the process or source that qualifies the waste. Belongs to the profile-code pairing, not to either entity alone.
reference: 40 CFR Part 261 Subparts B, C, D

description: The measured TCLP extract concentration in mg/L for this specific waste stream that triggered a toxicity characteristic waste code (D004–D043). This value is specific to the waste profile and the code pairing — the same waste code may be triggered at different concentrations across different profiles.

Comma-separated list of EPA hazardous waste codes (D-codes for characteristic, F-codes for non-specific source, K-codes for specific source, P/U-codes for commercial chemical products) assigned to this waste stream. [Moved from waste_profile: This denormalized STRING field stores a comma-separated list of all applicable waste codes for the profile — the direct symptom of the suppressed M:N relationship. Comma-separated list of EPA hazardous waste codes (D, F, K, P, U series) assigned to the input waste stream. Determines applicable treatment standards and Land Disposal Restrictions (LDR).
reference: 40 CFR Part 261 Identification and Listing of Hazardous Waste

description: The quantity of hazardous waste fed into the treatment unit for this operation, measured in the unit specified by input_quantity_unit. Critical for mass balance calculations and regulatory reporting.
reference: 40 CFR Part 264.73 Operating Record; 40 CFR Part 268.7 LDR Recordkeeping

description: The timestamp when this treatment record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.

Indicates whether the treated waste meets EPA Land Disposal Restrictions treatment standards and is eligible for land disposal. Values: COMPLIANT=Meets LDR standards, NON_COMPLIANT=Requires additional treatment, EXEMPT=Waste category exempt from LDR, PENDING_ANALYSIS=Awaiting analytical results.

Free-text field for recording additional observations, deviations from standard procedures, equipment malfunctions, or other relevant information about the treatment operation. Supports operational troubleshooting and continuous improvement.

Certification number of the trained and certified operator who performed or supervised the treatment operation. RCRA requires personnel training and certification for hazardous waste treatment operations.
reference: 40 CFR Part 264.16 Personnel Training; HAZWOPER 29 CFR 1910.120

description: Unit of measure for the output residual quantity. Values: KG=Kilograms, LB=Pounds, GAL=Gallons, L=Liters, TON=US Tons, MT=Metric Tonnes.

The quantity of treated residual material or byproduct remaining after treatment, measured in the unit specified by output_quantity_unit. Used for mass balance verification and subsequent disposal or recycling decisions.

Description of the physical and chemical characteristics of the treated residual, including any remaining hazardous waste codes. Determines whether the residual meets Land Disposal Restrictions (LDR) treatment standards or requires further treatment.
reference: 40 CFR Part 268.7 Waste Analysis and Recordkeeping; 40 CFR Part 261.3

business_glossary_term: Treatment Pressure in Pounds per Square Inch (PSI)
description: Operating pressure in pounds per square inch (PSI) during treatment. Relevant for pressurized treatment technologies such as wet air oxidation or supercritical water oxidation.

Status of the quality assurance review for this treatment record. Values: APPROVED=Record verified and approved by QA, REJECTED=Record failed QA review, PENDING_REVIEW=Awaiting QA review, NOT_REQUIRED=QA review not required for this operation.
EPA-assigned code identifying the specific treatment unit under the facility's RCRA Part B permit. Each unit has unique permit conditions, operating parameters, and waste acceptance criteria.
reference: RCRA Part B Permit; 40 CFR Part 264.13

description: The duration in minutes that the waste remained in the treatment unit. Critical for ensuring adequate contact time for chemical reactions, thermal destruction, or physical separation processes per permit specifications.
reference: RCRA Part B Permit Operating Conditions; 40 CFR Part 264 Subpart O

description: The operational system from which this treatment record originated. Values: SAP_EHS=SAP Environment Health and Safety module, ENVIANCE=Enviance EHS platform, MANUAL_ENTRY=Manually entered by operator, SCADA=Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition system, LIMS=Laboratory Information Management System.
reference: Data Governance Framework; Data Lineage Standards

description: Result of stack emission testing performed during or after the treatment operation to verify compliance with air quality standards. Values: PASS=Emissions within permitted limits, FAIL=Emissions exceed limits, NOT_TESTED=No test performed for this operation.
reference: 40 CFR Part 60 Standards of Performance for New Stationary Sources; Clean Air Act

business_glossary_term: Toxicity Characteristic Leaching Procedure (TCLP) Test Performed
description: Boolean flag indicating whether a Toxicity Characteristic Leaching Procedure (TCLP) test was performed on the treated residual to verify that hazardous characteristics have been successfully treated. True=TCLP performed, False=TCLP not performed.
reference: 40 CFR Part 261.24 Toxicity Characteristic; EPA Method 1311

business_glossary_term: Toxicity Characteristic Leaching Procedure (TCLP) Test Result
description: Result of the TCLP test if performed. Values: PASS=Treated waste no longer exhibits toxicity characteristic, FAIL=Waste still exhibits toxicity characteristic, NOT_APPLICABLE=TCLP not required for this waste, PENDING=Test results not yet available.
Internal batch identifier for grouping multiple treatment records that were processed together or as part of a continuous treatment campaign. Enables batch-level quality control and traceability.

business_glossary_term: Treatment Cost in United States Dollars (USD)
description: The total cost in US dollars incurred for this treatment operation, including labor, energy, consumables, and equipment depreciation. Used for cost accounting and profitability analysis.
reference: GAAP Cost Accounting Standards; SAP CO Module

description: The calendar date on which the hazardous waste treatment operation was performed. Critical for regulatory reporting timelines and Land Disposal Restrictions (LDR) compliance tracking.

The calculated treatment efficiency expressed as a percentage, representing the degree of contaminant removal or destruction achieved. For incinerators, this is the Destruction and Removal Efficiency (DRE) which must meet or exceed 99.99% for most hazardous wastes.
reference: 40 CFR Part 264.343 Performance Standards for Incinerators

description: The precise date and time when the treatment process was completed. Used to calculate total treatment duration and verify process completion per permit requirements.

The pH level maintained during chemical treatment processes such as neutralization or chemical oxidation. Must be within permitted operating ranges to ensure effective treatment and regulatory compliance.
reference: 40 CFR Part 264.17 General Requirements for Ignitable, Reactive, or Incompatible Wastes

description: The precise date and time when the treatment process began. Used to calculate residence time and verify compliance with treatment technology specifications.

Current status of the treatment operation. Values: COMPLETED=Treatment successfully finished, IN_PROGRESS=Treatment underway, FAILED=Treatment did not meet specifications, ABORTED=Treatment stopped before completion, PENDING_VERIFICATION=Awaiting analytical confirmation of treatment effectiveness.

EPA-defined code representing the treatment technology applied. Values: INCIN=Incineration, NEUTR=Neutralization, STBLZ=Stabilization, FBLEN=Fuel Blending, SOLVR=Solvent Recovery, CARBN=Carbon Adsorption, WETOX=Wet Air Oxidation, CHOXD=Chemical Oxidation, CMBST=Combustion, DEACT=Deactivation, MACRO=Macroencapsulation, RORGS=Recovery of Organics, RMETL=Recovery of Metals, WTRRX=Water Reaction. reference: 40 CFR Part 268.42 Treatment Standards; EPA RCRA Waste Codes

description: Operating temperature of the treatment process in degrees Celsius. Critical parameter for thermal treatment technologies (incineration, combustion) to ensure destruction and removal efficiency (DRE) requirements are met.
