business_glossary_term: Department of Transportation (DOT) Inspection Required Flag
description: Indicates whether a Department of Transportation (DOT) inspection is required before the asset can be returned to service. True when downtime_category is dot_hold or when a safety-critical failure is identified. Supports DOT FMCSR compliance tracking.
reference: DOT Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations (FMCSR) 49 CFR Part 396; OSHA Vehicle Safety Standards

description: Indicates whether this downtime event represents a recurrence of the same failure mode on the same asset within a defined lookback period (typically 90 days). True signals a chronic reliability issue requiring escalated investigation or asset replacement consideration.
reference: ISO 14224 Reliability Analysis; RCM (Reliability-Centered Maintenance) Methodology; Infor EAM Failure History

description: Total labor cost in USD incurred for technician time associated with this downtime event, including regular and overtime rates. Sourced from Infor EAM labor records and Workday HCM payroll rates.
reference: SAP FI/CO Cost Center Accounting; Infor EAM Labor Cost Tracking; GAAP Cost Accounting Standards

description: GPS latitude coordinate (decimal degrees) of the asset location at the time of the downtime event. Sourced from Geotab telematics. Used for geographic analysis of breakdown hotspots and dispatch optimization.
reference: Geotab Fleet Telematics GPS Data; GPS (Global Positioning System) WGS84 Coordinate Standard

description: Free-text description of the geographic location where the asset breakdown or failure occurred (e.g., route name, street address, facility name, depot). Relevant for unplanned breakdowns occurring in the field.
reference: AMCS Platform Route Location; Geotab GPS Location Data; Locus Dispatch Field Location

description: GPS longitude coordinate (decimal degrees) of the asset location at the time of the downtime event. Sourced from Geotab telematics. Used for geographic analysis of breakdown hotspots and dispatch optimization.
reference: Geotab Fleet Telematics GPS Data; GPS (Global Positioning System) WGS84 Coordinate Standard

description: Estimated revenue in USD lost due to service disruptions caused by this downtime event, based on affected stops and applicable service rates. Used for operational impact financial assessment and ROI analysis of maintenance investments.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management; SAP SD Revenue Recognition; ROI (Return on Investment) Analysis

description: Odometer reading in miles recorded at the time the downtime event began. Used for mileage-based maintenance interval tracking and asset lifecycle analysis. Sourced from Geotab telematics or driver log.
reference: Geotab Fleet Telematics Odometer Data; DOT FMCSR Vehicle Inspection Records; Infor EAM Asset Meter Readings

description: Classification of the operational impact caused by the downtime event on waste collection or facility operations: route_cancellation (entire route not serviced), route_delay (route completed late), service_disruption (partial or degraded service), partial_service (some stops serviced by alternate vehicle), no_impact (downtime absorbed without service effect).
reference: AMCS Platform Service Impact Tracking; SLA (Service Level Agreement) Compliance Reporting; SWANA Operational KPIs

description: Total cost in USD of parts and materials consumed during the repair or maintenance activity associated with this downtime event. Sourced from Infor EAM parts inventory and SAP MM purchase orders.
reference: SAP MM Materials Management; Infor EAM Parts Inventory; BOM (Bill of Materials) Costing

description: Total hours the asset was idle specifically waiting for parts or materials to become available from inventory or procurement. Supports parts inventory optimization and supply chain analysis.
reference: Infor EAM Parts Management; SAP MM Materials Management; BOM (Bill of Materials) Analysis

description: Priority classification assigned to the downtime event to guide maintenance resource allocation: critical (immediate safety or regulatory risk, asset must be repaired within hours), high (significant operational impact, same-day resolution required), medium (moderate impact, next-day resolution), low (minimal impact, scheduled resolution acceptable).
reference: Infor EAM Work Order Priority; SAP PM Priority Codes; OSHA Safety Priority Standards

description: Actual hands-on repair or maintenance labor time in hours, excluding waiting time for parts or technician. Used to calculate Mean Time to Repair (MTTR) and labor efficiency metrics.
reference: ISO 14224 Active Repair Time; Infor EAM Labor Hours; SAP PM Actual Work

description: Date and time when the downtime event was first reported to the maintenance team or entered into the CMMS system. May differ from downtime_start_timestamp if there was a reporting lag.
reference: Infor EAM Event Notification; SAP PM Notification Creation Time

description: Free-text technician notes describing the repair actions taken, parts replaced, and any recommendations for follow-up maintenance or monitoring. Captured in Infor EAM upon work order completion.
reference: Infor EAM Work Order Completion Notes; SAP PM Technical Findings

description: Elapsed time in hours between the reported_timestamp and the time a technician began active work on the asset. Key KPI for maintenance responsiveness and SLA compliance measurement.
reference: ISO 14224 Maintenance Response Time; SWANA Fleet Maintenance KPIs; Infor EAM Response Metrics

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this downtime event record originated: infor_eam (Infor EAM CMMS), sap_pm (SAP Plant Maintenance module), geotab (Geotab telematics automated alert), amcs (AMCS Platform dispatch-triggered event), manual (manually entered by dispatcher or supervisor).
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage Standards; ERP (Enterprise Resource Planning) Integration Architecture

description: Number of customer service stops that were not completed or were disrupted as a direct result of this downtime event. Used to quantify customer impact and SLA compliance reporting.
reference: AMCS Platform Route Stop Tracking; SLA Compliance Reporting; Customer Service Impact Assessment

description: Total financial cost in USD attributable to this downtime event, encompassing labor cost, parts cost, and any additional costs such as rental equipment or contractor fees. Used for cost of downtime analysis and EBITDA impact assessment.
reference: SAP FI/CO Cost Center Accounting; GAAP Financial Reporting; EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization) Reporting

description: Date and time when this downtime event record was last modified. Tracks updates to status, resolution notes, cost fields, or inspection results throughout the event lifecycle.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act) Audit Trail Requirements; Databricks Delta Lake Change Data Capture

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each failure mode record in the reference catalog. Primary key for the failure_mode data product. [REFERENCE_LOOKUP role — canonical_skip_reason: This entity is a standardized reference catalog of failure codes and classifications; it is a REFERENCE_LOOKUP/MASTER_RESOURCE hybrid used to classify asset breakdowns across work orders and repair history.]
reference: Infor EAM Failure Code Catalog; ISO 14224 Reliability and Maintenance Data Standard

description: Foreign key linking to asset.class. Business justification: FMEA per asset class: failure_mode has asset_class as a plain text field — a denormalized reference to asset.class. Failure modes in waste management are defined per asset class (compactors, transfer

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Failure modes with dot_reportable_flag or epa_reportable_flag must reference the specific regulatory requirement mandating reporting. This drives automated regulatory notification workflows when repor

Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle_class. Business justification: failure_mode.applicable_vehicle_types is a denormalized plain-text field. Replacing it with a proper FK to vehicle_class normalizes failure mode applicability, enabling accurate FMEA analysis per vehi

The primary vehicle or facility component system affected by this failure mode (e.g., 'Engine', 'Transmission', 'Hydraulic Lift System', 'Braking System', 'Electrical System', 'Compactor Body', 'Chassis'). Aligns with VMRS system codes for fleet assets and EAM component hierarchy for facility assets.
reference: VMRS (Vehicle Maintenance Reporting Standards) Code 31-34; Infor EAM Component Hierarchy; SAP PM Functional Location

description: Timestamp when this failure mode record was first created in the enterprise catalog. Supports audit trail requirements and catalog version management. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Section 404 Audit Trail Requirements; ISO 14224 Data Management

description: Standard method by which this failure mode is typically detected. Informs PM schedule design and sensor/telematics alert configuration. Sensor alerts integrate with Geotab telematics; diagnostic codes align with OBD-II/J1939 fault code systems for fleet assets. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: visual_inspection|sensor_alert|driver_report|pm_inspection|diagnostic_code|operator_complaint|third_party_inspection — promote to reference product]
reference: SAE J1939 Diagnostic Fault Codes; Geotab Telematics Platform; DOT DVIR Requirements 49 CFR 396.11

description: Indicates whether this failure mode, when identified on a commercial motor vehicle, constitutes a DOT-reportable defect requiring out-of-service designation or driver vehicle inspection report (DVIR) notation. Drives compliance workflows in fleet maintenance and DOT inspection management.
reference: DOT FMCSA 49 CFR Part 396.11 (DVIR); 49 CFR Part 396.17 (Periodic Inspection); NWRA Fleet Safety Standards

description: Date from which this failure mode code is valid for use on work orders and maintenance records. Supports catalog versioning and ensures that historical work orders retain the failure classification that was active at the time of the repair.
reference: Infor EAM Catalog Effectivity; SAP PM Catalog Profile Validity; ISO 14224

description: Indicates whether this failure mode may result in an EPA-reportable environmental incident (e.g., hydraulic fluid spill, leachate containment breach, LFG emission exceedance). When true, triggers environmental incident notification workflows in Enviance EHS and CERCLA/RCRA compliance reporting.
reference: EPA RCRA 40 CFR Part 264; CERCLA Section 103; Clean Water Act 33 USC 1321; Enviance EHS Incident Module

description: Standard estimated number of hours an asset is expected to be out of service when this failure mode occurs, including diagnosis, repair, and return-to-service time. Used for fleet availability modeling, route coverage planning, and KPI reporting on asset utilization.
reference: Infor EAM Downtime Tracking; AMCS Fleet Availability Reporting; ISO 14224

description: Standard estimated cost of parts and materials required to repair this failure mode, expressed in US dollars. Used for work order cost estimation, maintenance budget planning, and repair-vs-replace decision support. Sourced from SAP MM BOM pricing and Infor EAM parts catalog.
reference: SAP MM Material Master Pricing; Infor EAM Parts Catalog; GAAP Cost Accounting Standards

description: Date after which this failure mode code is no longer valid for assignment to new work orders. Null for codes with no planned expiration. Supports catalog lifecycle management and ensures deprecated codes are not used in active maintenance workflows.
reference: Infor EAM Catalog Effectivity; SAP PM Catalog Profile Validity

description: High-level classification grouping failure modes by the nature of the failure mechanism. Drives routing of work orders to the appropriate technician skill set and supports trend analysis by failure type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mechanical|electrical|hydraulic|structural|operator_induced|pneumatic|software — promote to reference product if additional categories are needed]
reference: Infor EAM Failure Category; ISO 14224 Section 8; FMEA Methodology

description: Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the failure mode within the enterprise catalog. Used as the externally-known business identifier on work orders, repair records, and FMEA documentation. Sourced from Infor EAM failure code configuration.
reference: Infor EAM Failure Code Catalog; SAP PM Catalog Profile; ISO 14224

description: Detailed narrative describing the nature of the failure, observable symptoms, and conditions under which it typically occurs. Provides technicians and engineers with sufficient context to identify and classify the failure accurately in the field.
reference: Infor EAM; ISO 14224; FMEA Methodology (SAE J1739)

description: Logical grouping of related failure modes for reporting and analysis purposes (e.g., 'Powertrain Failures', 'Body and Compactor Failures', 'Hydraulic System Failures', 'Containment Failures'). Supports roll-up reporting in maintenance dashboards and FMEA analysis by system group.
reference: Infor EAM Failure Group Configuration; SAP PM Catalog Group; ISO 14224 Failure Classification

description: Current lifecycle status of the failure mode record in the enterprise catalog. Active records are available for selection on work orders and PM schedules. Inactive or deprecated records are retained for historical reporting but cannot be assigned to new work orders.
reference: Infor EAM Catalog Management; SAP PM Catalog Profile Lifecycle

description: Short, human-readable name for the failure mode (e.g., 'Hydraulic Hose Burst', 'Brake Pad Wear', 'Electrical Short Circuit'). Used as the identity label in work order dropdowns, dashboards, and maintenance reports.
reference: Infor EAM; SAP PM Catalog; VMRS Code Council

business_glossary_term: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) Detectability Score
description: FMEA detectability rating on a 1-10 scale representing the ability of current inspection and monitoring controls to detect the failure before it causes asset breakdown. Score of 10 indicates the failure is virtually undetectable. Component of the RPN calculation.

business_glossary_term: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) Occurrence Score
description: FMEA occurrence rating on a 1-10 scale representing the likelihood of this failure mode occurring within a given operating period. Score of 10 indicates near-certain occurrence. Derived from historical work order frequency data and OEM reliability data. Component of the RPN calculation.
reference: FMEA Methodology (SAE J1739); ISO 14224; Infor EAM Failure History

business_glossary_term: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) Risk Priority Number (RPN)
description: Composite risk score calculated as the product of Severity, Occurrence, and Detectability ratings (each 1-10) per FMEA methodology. Range 1-1000. Higher RPN values indicate higher priority for preventive maintenance investment and engineering redesign. Maintained by reliability engineering and reviewed during PM optimization cycles.
reference: FMEA Methodology (SAE J1739); ISO 14224; Reliability-Centered Maintenance (RCM) Standards

business_glossary_term: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) Severity Score
description: FMEA severity rating on a 1-10 scale representing the seriousness of the failure effect on asset function, safety, or regulatory compliance. Score of 10 indicates catastrophic safety or environmental impact. Component of the RPN calculation.

Indicates whether repair of this failure mode involves handling, containment, or disposal of hazardous materials (e.g., hydraulic fluid, refrigerants, battery acid, leachate). When true, triggers hazardous waste handling procedures and DOT/EPA hazmat compliance workflows.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Part 171-180 (Hazardous Materials Regulations); EPA RCRA 40 CFR Part 262; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 (HazCom)

description: External industry standard code that maps this failure mode to a recognized classification system beyond VMRS (e.g., ISO 14224 failure mechanism code, SWANA equipment failure taxonomy, or OEM-specific fault code). Enables cross-system benchmarking and regulatory reporting alignment.
reference: ISO 14224 Reliability and Maintenance Data; SWANA Technical Standards; SAE J1939

description: Timestamp when this failure mode record was most recently updated. Tracks catalog maintenance activity and supports change management auditing for regulated maintenance programs. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX Section 404 Audit Trail Requirements; ISO 14224 Data Management

description: Indicates whether Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) energy isolation procedures are required when performing corrective maintenance for this failure mode. Drives automatic inclusion of LOTO safety instructions on generated work orders. Mandatory for compliance with OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 (Control of Hazardous Energy); ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety

description: Average operating hours between occurrences of this failure mode across the asset population, expressed in hours. Derived from historical work order data and used to calibrate PM interval recommendations and reliability forecasting models. Null for newly cataloged failure modes with insufficient history.
reference: ISO 14224 Reliability Data; MIL-HDBK-217F Reliability Prediction; Infor EAM Reliability Module

description: Average labor hours required to diagnose and repair this failure mode, expressed in hours. Used for maintenance scheduling, technician capacity planning, and downtime impact estimation on work orders. Derived from actual labor hours recorded in Infor EAM and SAP PM work order history.
reference: ISO 14224; Infor EAM Work Order History; SAP PM Actual Labor Reporting

description: SAE J1939 or OBD-II diagnostic trouble code (DTC) associated with this failure mode for fleet assets. Enables automatic failure mode classification from telematics fault code alerts received via Geotab. Null for non-fleet or non-electronic failure modes.
reference: SAE J1939 Standard; SAE J2012 DTC Format; Geotab Fleet Telematics Integration

description: Indicates whether detection of this failure mode should automatically trigger creation of a preventive maintenance work order or PM schedule review. Supports proactive maintenance strategy by escalating recurring failure patterns to PM program adjustments.
reference: Infor EAM PM Module; SAP PM Maintenance Strategy; RCM Standards

description: Comma-delimited list of required PPE items for technicians performing repairs associated with this failure mode (e.g., 'Safety glasses, Chemical-resistant gloves, Steel-toed boots, Face shield'). Populated on work orders to ensure OSHA PPE compliance during maintenance activities.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.132 (PPE General Requirements); ISO 45001; NWRA Safety Standards

description: Standardized guidance on the preferred corrective maintenance action to resolve this failure mode (e.g., 'Replace hydraulic hose assembly', 'Inspect and replace brake pads', 'Perform electrical continuity test and rewire'). Used to pre-populate work order task instructions and support technician decision-making.
reference: Infor EAM Standard Task Library; SAP PM Task List; OEM Service Manuals

description: Assessed likelihood that this failure mode will recur on the same asset within 90 days of repair if only corrective action is taken without addressing the root cause. High recurrence risk failure modes are flagged for root cause analysis and PM program review.
reference: RCM (Reliability-Centered Maintenance) Standards; ISO 14224; Infor EAM Failure History Analytics

description: Date on which this failure mode record was last reviewed by reliability engineering for accuracy, relevance, and FMEA score currency. Supports periodic catalog maintenance governance and ensures failure mode data quality for analytics and AI/ML applications.
reference: ISO 14224 Data Quality; RCM Review Cycle Standards; Infor EAM Catalog Management

description: Name or employee identifier of the reliability engineer or maintenance manager who last reviewed and approved this failure mode record for catalog inclusion. Supports quality assurance and change management governance for the failure mode catalog.
reference: ISO 14224 Data Quality Management; SOX Internal Controls; Infor EAM Catalog Approval Workflow

description: Indicates whether this failure mode presents a direct safety risk to operators, technicians, or the public. When true, triggers mandatory safety review, LOTO (Lockout/Tagout) procedures, and potential DOT out-of-service designation on associated work orders. Supports OSHA and DOT compliance workflows.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 (LOTO); DOT FMCSA 49 CFR Part 396.9; ISO 45001

description: Standardized severity rating indicating the potential impact of this failure mode on asset operability, safety, regulatory compliance, or service delivery. Critical failures trigger immediate out-of-service actions; high failures require same-day response; medium and low allow scheduled repair. Aligns with FMEA severity rankings.
reference: FMEA Methodology (SAE J1739) Severity Scale; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; DOT 49 CFR Part 396

description: Identifies the originating system from which this failure mode record was created or imported. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer and reconciliation between Infor EAM, SAP PM, and VMRS standard code imports.
reference: Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage Standards; Infor EAM Integration; SAP PM Integration

description: The native failure code identifier as it exists in the originating source system (Infor EAM or SAP PM). Enables bidirectional reconciliation between the enterprise catalog and source systems during ETL processing and audit reviews.
reference: Infor EAM Failure Code; SAP PM Catalog Code; Databricks Silver Layer Lineage

description: Narrative description of the most common underlying root cause(s) associated with this failure mode (e.g., 'Lack of lubrication', 'Fatigue cracking due to overloading', 'Corrosion from leachate exposure'). Supports root cause analysis (RCA) and FMEA documentation. Populated by maintenance engineering based on historical repair data.
reference: FMEA Methodology (SAE J1739); ISO 14224; RCM (Reliability-Centered Maintenance) Standards

description: Industry-standard VMRS code mapping this failure mode to the American Trucking Associations Vehicle Maintenance Reporting Standards taxonomy. Enables benchmarking against industry fleet maintenance data, DOT compliance reporting, and interoperability with fleet telematics systems such as Geotab. Null for non-fleet (facility) failure modes.
reference: American Trucking Associations (ATA) VMRS Code Manual; DOT FMCSA Maintenance Regulations 49 CFR Part 396

description: Indicates whether this failure mode may be covered under OEM or vendor warranty, triggering warranty claim initiation in SAP MM. When true, work orders associated with this failure mode are flagged for warranty review before parts are charged to the maintenance cost center.
reference: SAP MM Warranty Management; Infor EAM Warranty Module; OEM Warranty Terms

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each closed/archived repair or maintenance event record in the repair history. Primary key for this data product.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle Management; SAP PM Work Order Management

description: Foreign key linking to landfill.cell. Business justification: Cell-level repair history for liner systems, gas collection infrastructure, and leachate systems is required for closure plan documentation, financial assurance calculations, and regulatory compliance

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_inspection. Business justification: Repair history records document repairs performed in response to compliance inspection findings. This linkage supports regulatory audit trails showing that inspection-identified deficiencies were corr

Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Repair history must reference the governing contract for warranty claim processing, subcontractor cost recovery, and contract performance reporting. In waste management, repairs on contracted fleet an

Foreign key linking to maintenance.downtime_event. Business justification: repair_history and downtime_event are both children of work_order, but a direct link from repair_history to downtime_event establishes the causal chain: a downtime event triggers a repair, and the rep

Reference to the maintenance facility, shop, or service location where the repair was performed (e.g., fleet maintenance yard, landfill workshop, transfer station). Enables geographic and facility-level maintenance cost and performance analysis.
reference: Infor EAM Location Master; SAP PM Functional Location

description: Foreign key linking to maintenance.failure_mode. Business justification: Repair history records should link to standardized failure mode catalog for consistent failure tracking and trend analysis. Currently has failure_mode (STRING) and failure_code (STRING) which duplicat
Reference to the fleet vehicle, facility equipment, or infrastructure asset on which the repair or maintenance event was performed. Enables longitudinal asset health and lifecycle cost analysis.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle Management; SAP PM Equipment Master

description: Foreign key linking to landfill.groundwater_monitoring_well. Business justification: Repair and rehabilitation history for groundwater monitoring wells (casing repair, pump replacement, redevelopment) is required for RCRA compliance documentation and well integrity reporting to regula

Foreign key linking to landfill.site. Business justification: Repair history for landfill equipment must be maintained by site for regulatory inspections, environmental compliance audits, and demonstrating proper maintenance of pollution control equipment. Requi

Foreign key linking to landfill.lfg_extraction_well. Business justification: Well-level repair history for LFG extraction wells is required for EPA NSPS Subpart WWW/XXX compliance documentation, well performance trending, warranty tracking, and operational life analysis. A lan

Reference to the Preventive Maintenance (PM) schedule or maintenance plan that triggered this work order, if applicable. Populated for planned/preventive repairs; null for corrective or emergency repairs. Enables PM compliance rate analysis.
reference: Infor EAM PM Schedule; SAP PM Maintenance Plan

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_corrective_action. Business justification: Repair history records provide the physical evidence of corrective action completion. Linking repair history to regulatory_corrective_action enables automated closure verification, supports regulatory

Foreign key linking to service.sla_definition. Business justification: Repair history is used for retrospective SLA compliance audits — did the repair meet contractual response and resolution time commitments? Linking to sla_definition enables SLA performance trending, c

Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: Repair history tracks vehicle-specific failure patterns, warranty claims, and cost trends. Fleet managers analyze repair history by vehicle for replacement decisions and reliability analysis. Asset_id

Foreign key linking to compliance.violation_notice. Business justification: Repair history records document maintenance performed to remediate violation notices. Regulators require audit trail evidence linking specific repairs to cited violations during enforcement proceeding

Foreign key linking to asset.warranty. Business justification: Warranty claim traceability: repair_history has is_warranty_claim (boolean) and warranty_claim_number (denormalized text). A proper FK to asset.warranty enables warranty reimbursement tracking, claim

Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_profile. Business justification: Repair history is an independent audit record used for RCRA waste generation audits and regulatory inspections. Direct waste_profile_id on repair_history enables hazmat-generating repair traceability
Reference to the originating Work Order (WO) that was executed and subsequently closed to produce this repair history record. Links the archived record back to the active WO lifecycle.
reference: Infor EAM Work Order Module; SAP PM Module

description: Externally-known asset tag or equipment number (e.g., fleet unit number, facility equipment tag) as assigned in Infor EAM or SAP PM. Enables direct lookup without requiring a join to the asset master.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Master; SAP PM Equipment Master

description: Date and time when the Work Order (WO) was formally closed and archived in the CMMS, creating this repair history record. May differ from completion_timestamp if administrative review or cost settlement occurs after physical completion.
reference: Infor EAM Work Order Lifecycle; SAP PM Order Settlement; SOX Financial Close Controls

description: Date and time when the repair or maintenance event was completed and the asset was returned to service. Used to calculate actual repair duration and Mean Time To Repair (MTTR). Distinct from the WO close date which may occur later during administrative closure.
reference: Infor EAM Work Order Completion; SAP PM Order Confirmation; ISO 14224

description: Name or description of the specific component, sub-assembly, or system on the asset that was repaired or replaced (e.g., rear axle, hydraulic pump, compactor blade, engine air filter, brake caliper). Supports Bill of Materials (BOM) analysis and component-level MTBF tracking.
reference: Infor EAM BOM Module; SAP PM Bill of Materials (BOM); VMRS Component Code Standard

description: SAP General Ledger (GL) cost center code to which the repair costs are allocated. Enables financial reporting of maintenance expenditure by business unit, region, or operational division for EBITDA and COGS analysis.
reference: SAP FI/CO Cost Center Accounting; GAAP Cost Allocation; SOX Section 404

description: Timestamp when this repair history record was first created in the source system (Infor EAM or SAP PM). Serves as the audit trail creation marker for data lineage and SOX compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404 Internal Controls; Infor EAM Audit Trail

description: Official DOT inspection report number assigned by the inspecting authority for DOT-mandated inspections. Required for regulatory compliance documentation and FMCSA audit response. Populated only when is_dot_inspection is True.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Part 396.21; FMCSA Inspection Records

description: Total hours the asset was out of service (unavailable for operations) due to this repair event, from the time it was taken out of service to the time it was returned to active duty. Key input for fleet availability rate and operational impact assessment.
reference: ISO 14224 Availability Metrics; Infor EAM Downtime Tracking; SWANA Fleet Benchmarking

description: Engine hour meter reading recorded at the time of repair for hour-metered assets (compactors, generators, heavy equipment, Waste-to-Energy (WTE) plant equipment). Used for hour-based PM interval tracking and MTBF analysis where mileage is not applicable.
reference: Infor EAM Meter Reading; SAP PM Measurement Document; ISO 14224

description: SAP General Ledger (GL) account code used to post the repair costs in the financial system. Distinguishes between capital expenditure (CapEx) and operating expenditure (OpEx) maintenance costs for GAAP/IFRS financial reporting.
reference: SAP FI/CO General Ledger; GAAP ASC 360 Property Plant and Equipment; IFRS 16; SOX Section 404

description: Indicates whether this repair event was associated with or triggered by a Department of Transportation (DOT) mandated vehicle inspection (e.g., annual inspection per 49 CFR Part 396.17, roadside inspection, or out-of-service order). Critical for DOT compliance audit trail.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Part 396.17 Annual Inspection; FMCSA Compliance; DOT Out-of-Service Criteria

description: Indicates whether the repair costs are recoverable under an OEM or vendor warranty. When True, triggers the warranty recovery process and cost reclassification from maintenance expense to warranty receivable.
reference: SAP MM Warranty Management; Infor EAM Warranty Module; GAAP Revenue Recognition

description: Total labor cost in US dollars for this repair event, calculated from technician hours and applicable labor rates. Component of total repair cost and lifecycle cost analysis. Confidential as it reflects internal labor rate structures.
reference: SAP FI/CO Cost Center Accounting; Infor EAM Cost Module; GAAP Cost Accounting

description: Total actual labor hours expended by all technicians on this repair event, as recorded in the CMMS time entry. Used for Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) calculation, technician productivity analysis, and labor cost allocation.
reference: Infor EAM Labor Module; SAP PM Confirmation; ISO 14224

description: Odometer reading in miles recorded at the time of repair for wheeled fleet assets (trucks, ASL, FEL, REL vehicles). Used to calculate mileage-based Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF), establish PM trigger intervals, and support DOT inspection compliance records.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Part 396; Geotab Fleet Telematics; Infor EAM Meter Reading

description: Total cost in US dollars of all parts and materials consumed during this repair event, sourced from the Bill of Materials (BOM) and parts inventory. Component of total repair cost and lifecycle cost analysis.
reference: SAP MM Materials Management; Infor EAM Parts Inventory; GAAP Cost Accounting

description: Priority level assigned to the work order at the time of execution, indicating urgency and impact on fleet availability or regulatory compliance (e.g., critical for DOT out-of-service violations, routine for scheduled Preventive Maintenance (PM)).
reference: Infor EAM Work Order Priority; SAP PM Priority; DOT 49 CFR Part 396.9

description: SAP Purchase Order (PO) number associated with external parts procurement or subcontractor services for this repair. Links the maintenance record to the Accounts Payable (AP) process for cost reconciliation.
reference: SAP MM Purchase Order (PO); SAP AP Accounts Payable; GAAP Accrual Accounting

description: Free-text narrative describing the work performed, findings, corrective actions taken, and any relevant observations by the technician. Provides the detailed maintenance record for audit, warranty claims, and future diagnostic reference.
reference: Infor EAM Work Order Notes; SAP PM Long Text; DOT Inspection Records

description: Lifecycle status of the repair history record. Completed indicates work was finished and verified; closed indicates the WO was formally archived; cancelled indicates the WO was voided; deferred indicates work was postponed and re-scheduled.
reference: Infor EAM Work Order Lifecycle; SAP PM Order Status Management

description: Classification of the maintenance event: corrective (reactive repair after failure), preventive (scheduled Preventive Maintenance (PM) cycle), predictive (condition-based), emergency (unplanned critical failure), or inspection (DOT/regulatory inspection). Drives MTBF and MTTR segmentation.
reference: ISO 14224 Reliability and Maintenance Data; DOT 49 CFR Part 396; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147

description: Date the failure, defect, or maintenance need was first reported or identified, either by a driver vehicle inspection report (DVIR), operator, or automated telematics alert. Establishes the start of the repair lifecycle for Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) calculation.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Part 396.11 Driver Vehicle Inspection Report (DVIR); Geotab Fleet Telematics

description: Technician or engineer analysis of the underlying root cause of the failure or maintenance need (e.g., deferred maintenance, operator misuse, manufacturing defect, environmental exposure). Supports root cause analysis (RCA) and continuous improvement programs.
reference: ISO 14224 Reliability and Maintenance Data; OSHA Incident Investigation Standards

description: Date the maintenance or repair event was scheduled to be performed, as planned in the Preventive Maintenance (PM) schedule or work order dispatch. Used to measure schedule adherence and PM compliance rate.
reference: Infor EAM PM Scheduling; SAP PM Maintenance Plan

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this repair history record was sourced (Infor EAM for asset-centric maintenance, SAP PM for work order execution, or Manual for paper-based records). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Enterprise Data Governance; Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage Standards

description: Date and time when the technician physically began work on the asset. Used as the primary business event timestamp for Mean Time To Repair (MTTR) calculation and technician utilization analysis.
reference: Infor EAM Labor Time Entry; SAP PM Confirmation; ISO 14224

description: Cost in US dollars paid to external vendors or subcontractors for work performed on this repair event (e.g., specialized engine rebuilds, body shop work, OEM warranty repairs). Tracked separately from internal labor cost for make-vs-buy analysis.
reference: SAP MM Purchase Order (PO); Infor EAM Subcontractor Module; GAAP Cost Accounting

description: Name or employee ID of the maintenance supervisor or fleet manager who reviewed and approved the completed repair before work order closure. Required for DOT annual inspection certification and internal quality control.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Part 396.17 Inspection Certification; Infor EAM Work Order Approval Workflow

description: Full name of the primary technician who performed the repair, as recorded at the time of work order closure. Retained in the historical record for audit and accountability purposes even if the technician record changes. Classified confidential as it is employee data.
reference: Workday HCM Employee Records; DOT 49 CFR Part 396.21 Inspection Certification

description: Total cost in US dollars for the complete repair event, encompassing labor, parts, subcontractor, and any other direct costs. Primary financial metric for asset lifecycle cost analysis, EBITDA impact assessment, and repair vs. replace decision support.
reference: SAP FI/CO Cost Settlement; Infor EAM Cost Module; GAAP Cost Accounting; IFRS 16

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this repair history record in the source system. Supports incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail requirements.
reference: SOX Section 404 Internal Controls; Infor EAM Audit Trail

business_glossary_term: Vehicle Maintenance Reporting Standards (VMRS) System Code
description: Three-digit Vehicle Maintenance Reporting Standards (VMRS) system code identifying the major vehicle system affected by the repair (e.g., 011 = Air Brakes, 013 = Cooling System, 032 = Engine). Industry-standard classification for fleet maintenance benchmarking and DOT compliance reporting.
reference: American Trucking Associations (ATA) VMRS Code Standard; DOT 49 CFR Part 396

description: Human-readable, externally-known Work Order (WO) number assigned by the CMMS (Infor EAM or SAP PM) at the time the work order was created. Serves as the primary business identifier for cross-system traceability and audit.
reference: Infor EAM Work Order Module; SAP PM Module; DOT Inspection Records

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each maintenance type record in the reference classification table. Primary key. [_canonical_skip_reason: Entity role is REFERENCE_LOOKUP — a small enumeration/code list providing standardized taxonomy for maintenance activity classification across fleet and facility assets; per-role minimums are exempt.]
reference: Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle; SAP PM Module

description: Foreign key linking to asset.asset_class. Business justification: Maintenance planning requires defining which asset classes require specific maintenance types (PM schedules, DOT inspections, EPA compliance checks). The text field 'asset_class_applicability' should

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Maintenance types (DOT inspection, EPA-required equipment check, OSHA-mandated inspection) are defined by specific regulatory requirements. regulatory_authority is a denormalized field that should be

Indicates whether work orders of this maintenance type require supervisory or management approval before execution (True) or can be executed without prior approval (False). Drives approval workflow routing in SAP PM and Infor EAM for cost control and compliance.
reference: SAP PM Module; Infor EAM; SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act; Internal Controls

description: Minimum estimated cost in US dollars above which a work order of this maintenance type requires management approval. Supports tiered authorization controls for maintenance spend in compliance with SOX internal control requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Module; SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act; Internal Audit Controls

description: Standard average labor cost for a maintenance activity of this type, expressed in US dollars. Derived from technician labor rates and estimated duration. Used for work order cost estimation, maintenance budget planning, and COGS reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Module; Infor EAM; GAAP Cost Accounting

description: Standard average cost of parts and materials consumed for a maintenance activity of this type, expressed in US dollars. Used for maintenance budget planning, work order cost estimation, and BOM cost benchmarking in SAP MM and Infor EAM.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; Infor EAM; GAAP Cost Accounting

description: Maps this maintenance type to a cost accounting category in SAP FI/CO for GL posting and COGS allocation (e.g., PREV_MAINT, CORR_MAINT, EMER_MAINT, DOT_INSP, WARRANTY). Enables financial reporting by maintenance type and supports EBITDA analysis for fleet operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Module; GAAP/IFRS Financial Reporting Standards; SOX Compliance

description: Timestamp when this maintenance type record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports data lineage, audit trail, and change management tracking per SOX and internal data governance requirements.
reference: SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act; ISO 8601; Internal Data Governance

description: Default work order priority assigned when a work order is created under this maintenance type. Drives scheduling urgency, technician dispatch sequencing, and SLA compliance tracking. Can be overridden at the individual work order level.
reference: Infor EAM; SAP PM Module; NWRA Fleet Maintenance Standards

description: Indicates whether this maintenance type constitutes or includes a DOT-mandated vehicle inspection (True) or not (False). When True, triggers DOT inspection form completion, driver vehicle inspection report (DVIR) requirements, and out-of-service defect tracking per 49 CFR Part 396.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Part 396; FMCSA Regulations; SAP EHS Module

description: Native maintenance type code as defined in Infor EAM, used for system integration and data lineage tracing. Enables bidirectional synchronization between the lakehouse silver layer and the Infor EAM CMMS source system.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle and Maintenance Scheduling

description: Date from which this maintenance type classification becomes valid and available for use in work order and PM schedule creation. Supports versioning and phased rollout of new maintenance type definitions aligned with regulatory or operational changes.
reference: Infor EAM; SAP PM Module; ISO 55001; DOT Regulatory Updates

description: Standard estimated labor hours required to complete a maintenance activity of this type. Used for technician scheduling, shift planning, and asset downtime forecasting. Serves as the default value populated on new work orders of this type in Infor EAM.
reference: Infor EAM; SAP PM Module; NWRA Fleet Maintenance Standards

description: Date after which this maintenance type classification is no longer valid for new work order or PM schedule creation. Null indicates no expiration. Supports retirement of obsolete maintenance types while preserving historical records.
reference: Infor EAM; SAP PM Module; ISO 55001; Data Governance Standards

description: Indicates whether maintenance activities of this type are typically performed by an external vendor or contractor (True) rather than internal technicians (False). When True, triggers purchase order (PO) creation workflow in SAP MM for external service procurement.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module; SAP PM Module; Infor EAM; DOT Contractor Compliance

description: Indicates whether failure mode and root cause analysis is required for work orders of this maintenance type (True) or not (False). When True, technicians must document failure codes, cause codes, and remediation actions in Infor EAM for reliability-centered maintenance (RCM) analysis.
reference: Infor EAM; SAP PM Module; ISO 55001 Asset Management; RCM Standards

description: Indicates whether maintenance activities of this type generate reportable GHG emissions data (True) or not (False). Applicable for maintenance involving CNG/RNG fuel systems, LFG equipment, or WTE facility assets where EPA GHG reporting under the Clean Air Act is required.
reference: EPA Clean Air Act; EPA GHG Reporting Rule 40 CFR Part 98; ISO 14001; Enviance EHS

description: SAP General Ledger account code to which maintenance costs of this type are posted. Ensures accurate financial reporting, cost center allocation, and compliance with GAAP/IFRS standards for maintenance expense recognition.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Module; GAAP; IFRS; SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act

description: Indicates whether Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) energy isolation procedures are required for maintenance activities of this type (True) or not (False). Mandatory safety control per OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 for hazardous energy control during maintenance on fleet and facility equipment.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147; SAP EHS Module; Enviance EHS

description: Maximum number of hours an asset is permitted to remain out of service for maintenance of this type before escalation is triggered. Used to enforce SLA compliance for fleet availability and to drive escalation workflows in Infor EAM and AMCS dispatch.
reference: Infor EAM; AMCS Platform; NWRA Fleet Availability Standards; SLA Management

description: Unit of measure for the recurrence_interval_meter field, defining the measurement basis for meter-triggered PM schedules (e.g., miles for vehicle odometer, hours for engine runtime, cycles for lift/compaction cycles, tons for throughput-based equipment).
reference: Infor EAM PM Scheduling; SAP PM Module; DOT 49 CFR

description: Standard number of calendar days required to plan and prepare for a maintenance activity of this type before execution. Used by maintenance planners to schedule parts procurement, technician availability, and asset downtime windows. Sourced from Infor EAM PM cycle configuration.
reference: Infor EAM; SAP PM Module; ISO 55001 Asset Management

description: Defines the basis on which PM schedules of this maintenance type are triggered: time-based (fixed calendar intervals), meter-based (mileage, engine hours, cycles), condition-based (sensor thresholds), event-based (after specific operational events), or calendar-based (fixed dates). Drives PM cycle configuration in Infor EAM.
reference: Infor EAM PM Scheduling; SAP PM Module; ISO 55001 Asset Management

description: Comma-separated list of mandatory PPE items required for maintenance activities of this type (e.g., safety_glasses, gloves, hard_hat, hi_vis_vest, respirator). Populated on work orders to ensure technician safety compliance per OSHA PPE standards.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.132; SAP EHS Module; Enviance EHS

description: Default calendar-based recurrence interval in days for PM schedules of this maintenance type. Used when pm_trigger_basis is 'time' or 'calendar'. Drives automatic PM work order generation in Infor EAM and SAP PM scheduling cycles.
reference: Infor EAM PM Scheduling; SAP PM Module; ISO 55001 Asset Management

description: Default meter-based recurrence interval for PM schedules of this maintenance type (e.g., miles, engine hours, lift cycles). Used when pm_trigger_basis is 'meter'. The unit of measure is defined by meter_unit. Drives meter-based PM work order generation in Infor EAM.
reference: Infor EAM PM Scheduling; SAP PM Module; DOT 49 CFR Part 396

description: Indicates whether maintenance activities of this type must be reported to a regulatory authority (True) or not (False). Triggers mandatory documentation workflows in Enviance EHS and SAP EHS for DOT inspections, EPA compliance events, and OSHA-required maintenance records.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Part 396; EPA Regulations; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; RCRA; Enviance EHS

description: Code identifying the specific technician certification required to perform maintenance of this type (e.g., CDL_CLASS_A, ASE_MASTER, DOT_INSPECTOR, HAZMAT_HANDLER). Enforced during work order technician assignment in Infor EAM and Workday HCM. Applicable when requires_certified_technician is True.
reference: DOT 49 CFR; OSHA; ASE Certification Standards; Workday HCM

description: Indicates whether maintenance of this type requires the asset (vehicle, equipment, or facility) to be taken out of service (True) or can be performed while the asset remains operational (False). Drives downtime planning and route coverage adjustments in AMCS and Locus Dispatch.
reference: Infor EAM; AMCS Platform; Locus Dispatch; ISO 55001

description: Indicates whether maintenance activities of this type must be performed by a technician holding a specific certification (True) or can be performed by any qualified technician (False). When True, the required_certification_code field specifies the certification. Supports CDL, ASE, and DOT certification enforcement.
reference: DOT 49 CFR; OSHA; Workday HCM Certification Tracking

business_glossary_term: SAP Plant Maintenance (PM) Order Type Code
description: SAP PM order type code corresponding to this maintenance type (e.g., PM01, PM02, PM03). Used for GL posting, cost settlement, and integration with SAP FI/CO for maintenance cost reporting and EBITDA analysis.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module; SAP FI/CO Module; GAAP; SOX

description: Numeric value controlling the display sequence of maintenance types in dropdown lists, reports, and scheduling interfaces. Lower values appear first. Enables business users to prioritize frequently used types for operational efficiency.
reference: Infor EAM; SAP PM Module; UI/UX Standards

description: High-level grouping of the maintenance type indicating whether the activity is planned (scheduled in advance), unplanned (reactive/emergency), regulatory (DOT/EPA/OSHA mandated), or warranty (covered under manufacturer or service warranty). Supports cost allocation and KPI segmentation.
reference: SAP PM Module; DOT 49 CFR; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; ISO 14001

description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the maintenance type, used as a business key across work orders, PM schedules, and reporting systems (e.g., PREV, CORR, PRED, EMER, CBM, DOT, WARR, RBLD). Sourced from Infor EAM and SAP PM type classification.
reference: Infor EAM; SAP PM Module; SWANA Maintenance Classification Standards

description: Detailed narrative explaining the purpose, scope, and application of this maintenance type. Provides guidance to planners and technicians on when and how to apply this classification across fleet vehicles, containers, and facility assets.

Full descriptive name of the maintenance type as recognized across fleet and facility operations (e.g., Preventive, Corrective, Predictive, Emergency, Condition-Based, DOT Inspection, Warranty, Rebuild). Used in work order displays, PM schedule labels, and management reporting.
reference: Infor EAM; SAP PM Module; NWRA Fleet Maintenance Standards

description: Current lifecycle status of the maintenance type record indicating whether it is actively used in work order and PM schedule creation (active), temporarily disabled (inactive), or retired from use (deprecated). Controls availability in downstream scheduling and dispatch systems.

Timestamp of the most recent modification to this maintenance type record, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change tracking, data synchronization between Infor EAM and the lakehouse silver layer, and audit compliance.
reference: SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act; ISO 8601; Internal Data Governance

description: Indicates whether maintenance activities of this type may be covered under an OEM or service warranty (True) or are not warranty-eligible (False). When True, triggers warranty claim validation workflow in Infor EAM before cost posting to internal cost centers.
reference: Infor EAM Warranty Management; SAP PM Module; SAP MM Module

description: SAP PM and Infor EAM work order class associated with this maintenance type. Determines the work order processing workflow, approval routing, and cost settlement rules. Maps directly to the order type configuration in SAP PM (e.g., PM01 Corrective, PM02 Preventive, PM03 Inspection).
reference: SAP PM Module; Infor EAM; NWRA Fleet Maintenance Standards

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each part record in the master parts catalog. Primary key for the parts_catalog data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE.
reference: Infor EAM Parts Master; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master

description: Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_code. Business justification: Parts containing hazardous materials (batteries, mercury switches, PCB capacitors) must link to EPA waste codes for disposal planning, waste characterization, and regulatory compliance. Essential for

Foreign key linking to hazmat.dot_hazmat_classification. Business justification: Parts catalog entries for hazardous materials (fluids, batteries, aerosols) must carry DOT classification for 49 CFR shipping and storage compliance. Normalizes denormalized hazmat_class and un_number

Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Certain parts are permit-specific (emissions control components, hazardous waste containment parts, EPA-certified equipment). Parts catalogs must track permit applicability to ensure only approved com

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Hazardous parts catalog entries (is_hazardous_material=true) must reference governing regulatory requirements (RCRA, DOT hazmat regulations) for handling, storage, and disposal. This drives compliant

Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle_class. Business justification: Parts catalog entries are scoped to vehicle classes (e.g., hydraulic seals only applicable to rear-loaders). Linking parts_catalog to vehicle_class enables class-specific parts lookup during work orde
Date on which this part record became effective and available for use in work orders and purchasing. Supports catalog version management and historical BOM accuracy.
reference: Infor EAM Parts Master; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master Validity

description: Refundable deposit amount charged by the supplier for a remanufactured part's core. Credited back upon return of the used core. Expressed in USD. Applicable only when is_core_returnable is True.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Core Charge; Infor EAM Core Return; GAAP Inventory Accounting

description: Default SAP cost center code associated with the procurement and consumption of this part. Used for departmental cost allocation and maintenance budget tracking in SAP CO.
reference: SAP S/4HANA CO Cost Center Accounting; GAAP Cost Allocation; SOX Compliance

description: Timestamp when this part record was first created in the parts catalog. Provides audit trail for record provenance and supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Infor EAM Audit Trail; SAP S/4HANA Change Documents; SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act Audit Requirements

business_glossary_term: Department of Transportation (DOT) Inspection Part Flag
description: Indicates whether this part is a safety-critical component subject to Department of Transportation (DOT) vehicle inspection requirements (True). Examples include brake components, tires, lights, and steering parts. Ensures compliance tracking for DOT roadworthiness inspections.
reference: DOT FMCSA 49 CFR Part 396 Inspection, Repair, and Maintenance; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.178

description: Manufacturer or engineering-specified expected useful service life of the part in calendar days under normal operating conditions. Used for PM interval planning, lifecycle cost analysis, and asset reliability modeling.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle; SAP S/4HANA PM Maintenance Strategy; SWANA Fleet Lifecycle Management

description: General Ledger (GL) account code to which the cost of this part is posted upon consumption in a work order. Enables accurate cost allocation to maintenance expense accounts in SAP FI/CO. Examples: parts expense, consumables, capital spares.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO General Ledger; GAAP Financial Reporting; SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act Compliance

description: Indicates whether a core (used/rebuildable) part must be returned to the supplier upon installation of a replacement (True). Common for remanufactured parts such as alternators, starters, and hydraulic pumps. Drives core charge tracking in procurement.
reference: Infor EAM Core Return Management; SAP S/4HANA MM Returnable Packaging; NWRA Fleet Parts Standards

description: Indicates whether this part or material is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, storage, and disposal procedures under DOT, EPA, and OSHA regulations (e.g., certain fluids, batteries, refrigerants). True = hazardous; False = non-hazardous.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Hazardous Materials Regulations; EPA RCRA; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 Hazard Communication; CERCLA

description: Indicates whether individual units of this part are tracked by unique serial number (True) or managed as bulk/lot inventory (False). Serialized parts (e.g., engines, transmissions, compactors) require individual asset tracking in Infor EAM.
reference: Infor EAM Serialized Parts Tracking; SAP S/4HANA MM Serial Number Management

description: Date on which the standard cost for this part was last reviewed and updated. Supports annual cost roll-up processes and ensures standard costs remain aligned with market pricing.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Standard Cost Estimate; GAAP Inventory Valuation; IFRS IAS 2

description: Timestamp when this part record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking, incremental data loads, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: Infor EAM Audit Trail; SAP S/4HANA Change Documents; SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act Audit Requirements

description: Expected number of calendar days from purchase order placement to receipt of the part from the preferred supplier. Used for reorder point calculation and maintenance scheduling to ensure parts availability.
reference: Infor EAM Parts Lead Time; SAP S/4HANA MM Purchasing Info Record; SWANA Fleet Parts Management

description: Name of the original equipment manufacturer or parts manufacturer (e.g., Cummins, Parker Hannifin, Donaldson, Heil). Used for warranty tracking, sourcing decisions, and quality management.
reference: Infor EAM Parts Master; SAP S/4HANA MM Vendor Master

description: The part number assigned by the manufacturer (distinct from OEM number when the manufacturer is a third-party supplier rather than the equipment OEM). Enables direct ordering from manufacturer catalogs.
reference: Infor EAM Parts Master; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master

description: Maximum allowable on-hand inventory quantity to prevent overstocking and excess carrying costs. Used in min-max inventory planning. Expressed in the part's unit of measure.
reference: Infor EAM Inventory Management; SAP S/4HANA MM Max Stock Level

description: Safety stock level — the absolute minimum quantity that must be maintained on hand to prevent stockouts during lead time. Distinct from reorder_point which is the trigger level. Expressed in the part's unit of measure.
reference: Infor EAM Safety Stock; SAP S/4HANA MM Safety Stock; SWANA Fleet Parts Management

description: Date on which this part was designated as obsolete or discontinued. After this date, the part is no longer available for new work orders or purchase orders. Null if the part is still active.
reference: Infor EAM Parts Master; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Discontinuation

description: The Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) part number as assigned by the vehicle or equipment manufacturer (e.g., Mack Trucks, Heil Environmental, McNeilus). Used for cross-referencing with manufacturer catalogs and sourcing OEM replacements.
reference: Infor EAM Parts Master; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; DOT FMCSA Equipment Standards

description: High-level classification of the part by functional system or component group. Drives Bill of Materials (BOM) organization, work order planning, and inventory segmentation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: engine|hydraulic|electrical|body|tire|filter|fluid|brake|transmission|exhaust|pneumatic|safety|chassis|cooling|fuel|waste_body|compaction|lift_system|cab — promote to reference product]
reference: Infor EAM Parts Classification; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Group; NWRA Fleet Maintenance Standards

description: Detailed technical description of the part including specifications, dimensions, material composition, and application context. Supports technician identification and procurement accuracy.
reference: Infor EAM Parts Master; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master

description: Human-readable short name or title of the part (e.g., 'Hydraulic Pump Assembly', 'Air Filter Element', 'Brake Caliper'). Used as the primary identity label for display in work orders, purchase orders, and inventory reports.
reference: Infor EAM Parts Master; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master

description: Internal Waste Management part number used as the primary business identifier for the part across procurement, maintenance work orders, and inventory systems. Corresponds to the Material Number in SAP MM and the Part Number in Infor EAM.
reference: Infor EAM Parts Master; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master

description: Current lifecycle status of the part in the catalog. Active parts are available for use in work orders and purchasing. Obsolete parts are no longer procured. Superseded parts have been replaced by a newer part number. Pending_approval parts are under review before activation.
reference: Infor EAM Parts Master Status; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Status; SWANA Fleet Maintenance Standards

description: Classification of the part by its maintenance disposition: consumable (single-use, e.g., filters, fluids), repairable (can be rebuilt), rotable (tracked serialized item returned to stock after repair), expendable (low-cost, not tracked), or capital (high-value tracked asset component).
reference: Infor EAM Parts Master; SAP S/4HANA PM Maintenance Item Types; SWANA Fleet Maintenance Best Practices

description: Indicates whether this part is used in scheduled Preventive Maintenance (PM) cycles (True) versus only in corrective/unplanned repairs (False). Drives PM parts kit planning and stocking strategies.
reference: Infor EAM PM Scheduling; SAP S/4HANA PM Maintenance Plan; SWANA Fleet Maintenance Best Practices

description: Minimum on-hand inventory quantity threshold that triggers a replenishment purchase order. When stock falls to or below this level, procurement is initiated. Expressed in the part's unit of measure.
reference: Infor EAM Inventory Management; SAP S/4HANA MM MRP Reorder Point Planning; SWANA Fleet Parts Management

description: Standard quantity to order when a replenishment is triggered. Represents the economic order quantity (EOQ) or fixed lot size for the part. Expressed in the part's unit of measure.
reference: Infor EAM Inventory Management; SAP S/4HANA MM MRP Lot Size; SWANA Fleet Parts Management

description: Reference number for the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) associated with this part or material. Required by OSHA Hazard Communication Standard for hazardous chemicals. Enables technicians to access handling, storage, and emergency response information.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 Hazard Communication Standard; ISO 11014 Safety Data Sheet

description: Identifies the operational system of record from which this part record originated (e.g., INFOR_EAM for Infor EAM parts master, SAP_MM for SAP S/4HANA Material Master, MANUAL for manually entered records). Supports data lineage and reconciliation in the Silver Layer.
reference: Infor EAM; SAP S/4HANA MM; Databricks Lakehouse Data Lineage Standards

description: Standard cost per unit of measure for the part as maintained in the cost accounting system. Used for work order cost estimation, budget planning, and variance analysis. Expressed in USD. Corresponds to the standard price in SAP MM Material Master costing view.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Standard Cost; GAAP Inventory Valuation; IFRS IAS 2 Inventories

description: Default warehouse or parts room storage location code where this part is stocked (e.g., bin, shelf, or zone identifier). Used for inventory picking, cycle counting, and parts room organization in Infor EAM.
reference: Infor EAM Warehouse Management; SAP S/4HANA MM Storage Location

description: Standard unit of measure for the part used in inventory management, purchasing, and work order consumption (e.g., EA=Each, QT=Quart, GL=Gallon, LT=Liter, KG=Kilogram, SET=Set, PAIR=Pair). Aligns with SAP MM base unit of measure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|QT|GL|LT|KG|LB|FT|M|SET|BOX|CASE|ROLL|PAIR — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Unit of Measure; Infor EAM Stock UOM; ISO 80000 Quantities and Units

description: Standard warranty period in calendar days provided by the manufacturer or supplier for this part. Used to track warranty claims and determine eligibility for no-cost replacements on failed parts.
reference: Infor EAM Warranty Management; SAP S/4HANA PM Warranty; NWRA Fleet Parts Standards

description: Physical weight of the part in pounds. Used for shipping cost estimation, DOT weight compliance on service vehicles, and ergonomic safety assessments for technician handling under OSHA guidelines.
reference: DOT FMCSA Vehicle Weight Regulations; OSHA 29 CFR 1910 General Industry Safety; Infor EAM Parts Specifications

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each parts inventory record representing the on-hand stock position of a specific part at a specific storeroom and bin location. Primary key for the parts_inventory data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.

Reference to the part master record in the parts catalog. Links this inventory position to the part definition including description, specifications, and classification. Sourced from Infor EAM parts catalog.
reference: Infor EAM Parts Catalog; SAP S/4HANA MM Module

description: Reference to the maintenance storeroom or warehouse location where this part inventory is physically held. Each storeroom is associated with a facility or depot serving fleet and facility maintenance operations.
reference: Infor EAM Storeroom Management; SAP S/4HANA MM Storage Location

description: ABC analysis classification of the part based on annual consumption value. 'A' items represent high-value/high-usage parts requiring tight inventory control; 'B' items are moderate; 'C' items are low-value/low-usage parts. Used to prioritize cycle count frequency and inventory management effort.
reference: Infor EAM ABC Analysis; SAP S/4HANA MM ABC Indicator; SWANA Maintenance Best Practices

description: Total quantity of this part issued from this storeroom over the trailing 12-month period. Used for ABC classification, reorder point calculation, safety stock sizing, and inventory carrying cost analysis. Updated periodically from goods issue history.
reference: Infor EAM Consumption History; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Consumption

description: Specific bin, shelf, rack, or aisle location within the storeroom where the part is physically stored (e.g., 'A-12-3', 'RACK-B-SHELF-4'). Enables efficient parts picking during work order execution and physical inventory counts.
reference: Infor EAM Bin Location Management; SAP S/4HANA WM Bin

description: Difference between last_count_quantity and the system quantity_on_hand at the time of the physical count. Positive values indicate more stock found than recorded; negative values indicate shrinkage or unrecorded usage. Used to trigger inventory adjustments and investigate discrepancies.
reference: Infor EAM Inventory Adjustment; SAP S/4HANA MM Inventory Difference; SOX Section 404

description: Timestamp when this parts inventory record was first created in the source system (Infor EAM or SAP MM). Represents the date the part was first stocked at this storeroom location. Used for inventory aging analysis and audit trail.
reference: Infor EAM Record Creation; SAP S/4HANA MM Material Document

description: Classification of the part's operational criticality based on the impact of a stockout on fleet or facility operations. 'critical' parts (e.g., DOT-required safety components, CNG system parts) cause immediate vehicle out-of-service if unavailable. Used to prioritize reorder actions and safety stock levels.
reference: Infor EAM Criticality Ranking; DOT FMCSA Vehicle Inspection Requirements; OSHA Equipment Safety Standards

description: Required frequency for physical cycle counting of this part at this storeroom. Typically driven by ABC classification (A=monthly, B=quarterly, C=annual) and criticality level. Used to schedule cycle count tasks and ensure inventory accuracy for SOX compliance.
reference: Infor EAM Cycle Count Schedule; SAP S/4HANA MM Cycle Count; SOX Section 404 Internal Controls

description: DOT hazardous materials classification code for the part when is_hazardous is True (e.g., '3' for flammable liquids, '8' for corrosives, '9' for miscellaneous). Required for compliant storage, handling, and disposal documentation. Null when is_hazardous is False.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Part 172 Hazardous Materials Table; EPA RCRA Waste Classification

description: Current lifecycle status of this inventory record. 'active' indicates the part is available for use; 'quarantine' indicates the part is held pending quality inspection; 'obsolete' indicates the part is no longer used but stock remains; 'pending_disposal' indicates stock is being removed from service.
reference: Infor EAM Inventory Status; SAP S/4HANA MM Stock Type

description: Total monetary value of the on-hand inventory for this part at this storeroom, calculated as quantity_on_hand multiplied by unit_cost. Expressed in USD. Used for balance sheet inventory asset reporting, carrying cost analysis, and financial close processes per GAAP/IFRS.
reference: Infor EAM Inventory Valuation; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO Inventory Account; GAAP ASC 330; IFRS IAS 2

description: Indicates whether this part or material is classified as hazardous under DOT, EPA, or OSHA regulations (e.g., solvents, lubricants, refrigerants, battery acid). Triggers special handling, storage, and disposal requirements. True = hazardous material requiring regulatory compliance controls.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Hazardous Materials Regulations; EPA RCRA; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200 Hazard Communication

description: Indicates whether this part is managed by lot or batch number for traceability purposes (True). Lot-controlled parts (e.g., lubricants, chemicals, safety-critical components) require lot tracking for quality control, recall management, and regulatory compliance.
reference: Infor EAM Lot Management; SAP S/4HANA MM Batch Management; ISO 9001 Traceability

description: Indicates whether individual units of this part are tracked by unique serial number (True) or managed as bulk/lot quantity (False). Serialized parts (e.g., major assemblies, electronic control units) require individual tracking through installation and removal from assets.
reference: Infor EAM Serial Number Tracking; SAP S/4HANA MM Serial Number Profile

description: Actual quantity recorded during the most recent physical inventory count for this part at this storeroom. Compared against the system quantity_on_hand to identify and reconcile inventory discrepancies.
reference: Infor EAM Physical Inventory Count; SAP S/4HANA MM Physical Inventory Document

description: Specific regulatory or industry certification required to perform this task (e.g., CDL for vehicle brake inspection, ASE for engine diagnostics, DOT_INSPECTOR for federal vehicle inspection). Enforces compliance with DOT 49 CFR Part 396 and OSHA standards. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CDL|ASE|DOT_INSPECTOR|HAZMAT|OSHA_30|FORKLIFT|NONE|EPA_608|RCRA_HAZWASTE — promote to reference product]
reference: DOT 49 CFR Part 396; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; RCRA 40 CFR Part 262; ASE Certification Standards

description: Code identifying the minimum skill or trade qualification required to perform this task (e.g., 'MECH-DIESEL', 'ELEC-HV', 'HVAC-CERT'). Used for technician assignment validation and workforce capability gap analysis.
reference: Infor EAM Skill Requirement; Workday HCM Skill Profile; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

description: Mandatory safety procedures, lockout/tagout (LOTO) requirements, Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) specifications, and hazard warnings that must be followed before and during task execution. Sourced from EHS system and linked to OSHA compliance requirements.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 (LOTO); OSHA 29 CFR 1910.132 (PPE); Enviance EHS Safety Procedure Library

description: Planned date and time when the technician is scheduled to begin this task. Used for maintenance scheduling, shift planning, and asset downtime window management.
reference: Infor EAM Scheduled Start; SAP PM Scheduled Start Date/Time

description: Reason code explaining why a task was skipped or bypassed in the work order execution. Required when task_status is 'skipped' to maintain audit trail integrity and support PM compliance reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_applicable|deferred|parts_unavailable|asset_unavailable|safety_hold|supervisor_override|weather|other — promote to reference product]
reference: Infor EAM Skip Reason; SAP PM Operation Skip; NWRA PM Compliance Standards

description: Reference code linking this task to a standardized maintenance task template in the CMMS library (e.g., 'HYD-INSP-001' for hydraulic inspection). Enables consistent task definition, labor estimation benchmarking, and cross-fleet maintenance analytics.
reference: Infor EAM Standard Task; SAP PM Standard Text Key; SWANA Maintenance Best Practices

description: Detailed narrative description of the maintenance task to be performed, including the specific action, component, and expected outcome (e.g., 'Inspect and replace hydraulic hose on compactor lift arm'). Sourced from standard task library or entered by planner.
reference: Infor EAM Task Description; SAP PM Operation Short Text

description: Externally visible alphanumeric identifier for the task, typically formatted as the parent WO number suffixed with a task sequence (e.g., WO-2024-00123-T01). Used in technician communications, printed work packets, and audit trails.
reference: Infor EAM Task Code; SAP PM Operation Number

description: Numeric ordering of this task within the parent work order, defining the execution sequence of multi-step maintenance procedures (e.g., 10, 20, 30 in SAP PM operation numbering convention). Enables dependency management and step-by-step workflow tracking.
reference: Infor EAM Operation Sequence; SAP PM Operation Activity Number

description: Current lifecycle state of the maintenance task within the work order execution workflow. Drives technician dashboard visibility, WO completion logic, and downtime reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|in_progress|complete|skipped|on_hold|cancelled — promote to reference product]
reference: Infor EAM Task Status; SAP PM Operation Status; NWRA Fleet Maintenance Standards

description: Classification of the maintenance task by the nature of the work performed. Used for labor planning, parts forecasting, and maintenance cost categorization. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: inspection|repair|replacement|lubrication|calibration|cleaning|adjustment|testing|documentation — promote to reference product]
reference: Infor EAM Activity Type; SAP PM Activity Type; ISO 14001 Maintenance Classification

description: Free-text field for the assigned technician to record observations, findings, deviations from standard procedure, or recommendations during task execution. Captured via Infor EAM mobile app and retained for maintenance history and knowledge management.
reference: Infor EAM Technician Remarks; SAP PM Long Text; NWRA Maintenance Documentation Standards

description: Date and time when the supervisor or quality inspector verified and approved the completed task. Establishes the regulatory sign-off timestamp for DOT inspection records and OSHA compliance documentation.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Part 396.21; OSHA Recordkeeping Requirements; Infor EAM Approval Timestamp

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the maintenance work order record in the Databricks Silver layer. Primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.
reference: Infor EAM Work Order Entity; SAP PM Order Object

Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_inspection. Business justification: Post-inspection corrective work orders reference the triggering compliance inspection for audit trail and regulatory response tracking. Regulators require documented evidence that inspection-identifie

Foreign key linking to service.container_type. Business justification: Work orders for container repair, inspection, or replacement must reference the container type to apply type-specific maintenance procedures, warranty terms, and DOT inspection requirements. Container

Reference to the facility or depot where the maintenance work is performed (e.g., vehicle maintenance shop, landfill facility, transfer station). Used for geographic and operational reporting.
reference: Infor EAM Location Master; SAP PM Functional Location (IFLOT)

Foreign key linking to landfill.site. Business justification: Maintenance work orders for landfill equipment (compactors, gas collection systems, leachate pumps) must be tracked by site for environmental compliance reporting, regulatory audits, and site-specific

Foreign key linking to hazmat.manifest. Business justification: Hazmat cleanup and remediation work orders directly generate RCRA hazardous waste manifests. Linking work_order to manifest provides cradle-to-grave regulatory traceability from the maintenance author

Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Work orders executed to satisfy operating permit requirements (e.g., landfill gas system maintenance, leachate collection upkeep) must reference the governing permit for permit compliance tracking, au
