Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Certain maintenance activities are mandated by regulations (DOT inspections, EPA equipment standards, OSHA machinery safeguards). Work orders must reference the driving regulatory requirement for comp

Foreign key linking to customer.service_address. Business justification: Work orders for container delivery, swap, repair, or removal are dispatched to a specific customer service address. Technicians require the site location to execute the work order. This is a fundament

Foreign key linking to customer.service_enrollment. Business justification: Enrollment-driven work orders (initial container delivery, scheduled bulk pickups, compactor service, enrollment termination removal) must reference the governing service enrollment. This supports SLA

Foreign key linking to service.offering. Business justification: Work orders for service equipment (compactors, balers, containers) are tracked against service offerings for cost allocation, warranty claims, and SLA compliance. Real WM operations require linking ma

Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Work order dispatch requires a site contact (e.g., facility manager, service contact) for technician coordination, access instructions, and completion sign-off. Role-prefixed 'site_contact_id' disting

Foreign key linking to service.sla_definition. Business justification: Work orders for missed pickups or service failures must reference the governing SLA definition to calculate breach status, trigger auto-credits, and meet contractual resolution windows. SLA compliance

Foreign key linking to contract.sla_term. Business justification: Work orders are executed to fulfill specific SLA commitments (response time, completion time, quality). Linking work_order to the governing sla_term enables SLA compliance dashboards, penalty calculat

Foreign key linking to maintenance.maintenance_type. Business justification: Work orders should be classified by standardized maintenance type for consistent categorization, cost allocation, and regulatory reporting. Currently has wo_type (STRING) which should reference mainte

Foreign key linking to fleet.vehicle. Business justification: Every vehicle repair work order must directly reference the vehicle being serviced. This is the most fundamental CMMS link — vehicle maintenance cost tracking, downtime reporting, and warranty claims

Foreign key linking to compliance.violation_notice. Business justification: Work orders generated to remediate cited violations must reference the violation notice to demonstrate regulatory response. EPA and state agencies require documented corrective maintenance tied to spe

Foreign key linking to asset.warranty. Business justification: Warranty claim management: work_order has warranty_claim_flag but no FK to the specific warranty record. When a WO triggers a warranty claim, maintenance teams must reference the warranty to validate

Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_profile. Business justification: Work orders generating hazardous waste (parts cleaning, fluid changes, contaminated materials) must reference approved waste profiles for proper characterization, manifest preparation, and disposal co

Total actual cost incurred for the work order upon completion, including actual labor, parts consumed, and external contractor charges in USD. Posted to the SAP FI/CO cost center. Used for EBITDA reporting, maintenance budget variance, and asset lifecycle cost analysis.
reference: SAP FI/CO Actual Cost Settlement; Infor EAM Actual Cost; GAAP ASC 360; SOX Section 404

description: Actual date and time when the maintenance work was completed and the asset was returned to service. Used to calculate actual labor duration, downtime, and mean time to repair (MTTR) metrics.
reference: SAP PM Actual Finish (IEDD/IETD); Infor EAM Actual End; ISO 55001

description: Actual number of labor hours expended by technicians to complete the work order. Captured from time confirmations in SAP PM or Infor EAM. Used for labor cost calculation, productivity analysis, and maintenance KPI reporting.
reference: SAP PM Actual Work (ISMNW); Infor EAM Actual Hours; GAAP Labor Cost Capitalization

description: Actual date and time when the technician began executing the work order. Used to calculate schedule adherence, labor hours, and downtime duration. Compared against scheduled_start_date for KPI reporting.
reference: SAP PM Actual Start (ISDD/ISTD); Infor EAM Actual Start; ISO 55001 Performance Monitoring

business_glossary_term: Asset Meter Reading at Work Order
description: Odometer (miles) or engine hours reading of the asset at the time the work order was executed. Used to track PM intervals (e.g., oil change every 5,000 miles), calculate mean time between failures (MTBF), and validate DOT inspection compliance.
reference: SAP PM Counter Reading (ZAEHL); Infor EAM Meter Reading; DOT 49 CFR Part 396.3

description: Date and time when the work order was formally closed in the originating system, confirming all costs have been settled and the asset has been returned to service. Used for cycle time analysis, cost settlement confirmation, and regulatory record completion.
reference: SAP PM Order Close (TECO); Infor EAM WO Close Date; SOX Section 404; DOT 49 CFR Part 396.21

description: Free-text description of the repair, adjustment, or corrective action performed by the technician to resolve the failure. Captured at work order completion. Required for DOT inspection records and OSHA incident documentation.
reference: SAP PM Activity Text (LTXT); Infor EAM Corrective Action; DOT 49 CFR Part 396.11; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147

description: Date and time when the work order was first created in the originating system (SAP PM or Infor EAM). Represents the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED canonical category. Used for aging analysis, SLA measurement, and audit trails.
reference: SAP PM ERDAT/ERUHR; Infor EAM Creation Date; SOX Section 404 Audit Trail

description: Indicates whether this work order is associated with or fulfills a DOT vehicle inspection requirement under 49 CFR Part 396. When True, the work order record serves as part of the DOT inspection documentation and must be retained per regulatory retention schedules.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Part 396.11 (Driver Vehicle Inspection Reports); DOT 49 CFR Part 396.21 (Periodic Inspection)

description: Type of DOT inspection associated with this work order, if applicable. Annual = federally mandated annual vehicle inspection; Pre_Trip = driver pre-trip inspection defect repair; Post_Trip = post-trip defect repair; Roadside = repair following roadside enforcement inspection; None = not a DOT inspection work order.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Part 396.11; DOT 49 CFR Part 396.17 (Periodic Inspection Standards)

description: Timestamp when the asset was returned to operational service following maintenance completion. Used with downtime_start_timestamp to calculate total asset downtime duration for fleet availability reporting.
reference: Infor EAM Downtime End; SAP PM Breakdown End; ISO 55001 Availability Metrics

description: Timestamp when the asset was taken out of service due to the maintenance event. May precede actual_start_timestamp if the asset was grounded before a technician was assigned. Critical for fleet availability and route impact analysis.
reference: Infor EAM Downtime Start; SAP PM Breakdown Start; NWRA Fleet Availability KPI

description: Total estimated cost of the work order at time of creation, including planned labor, parts, and external services in USD. Used for budget planning, purchase order approval thresholds, and maintenance cost forecasting.
reference: SAP PM Planned Cost (KOSTO); Infor EAM Estimated Cost; GAAP ASC 360; SOX Cost Controls

description: Planned number of labor hours required to complete the work order, as estimated at time of creation. Used for workforce scheduling, capacity planning, and cost estimation. Compared against actual_labor_hours for variance analysis.
reference: SAP PM Planned Work (ARBEI); Infor EAM Estimated Hours; SWANA Maintenance Cost Benchmarks

description: Cost of external contractor or vendor services engaged to complete or support the work order in USD. Linked to purchase orders (PO) in SAP MM. Relevant for outsourced repairs, specialized inspections, and warranty work.
reference: SAP MM External Service (ML81N); Infor EAM Contractor Cost; GAAP Service Expense Recognition

description: Indicates whether the required LOTO energy isolation procedure has been completed and verified prior to work commencement. Must be True before work begins on LOTO-required work orders. Supports OSHA compliance audit trails.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147; SAP EHS Permit-to-Work; Infor EAM Safety Clearance

description: Indicates whether Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) energy isolation procedures are required before executing this work order. Mandatory safety clearance flag per OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 for work on equipment with hazardous energy sources (hydraulics, electrical, pneumatic).
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 (Control of Hazardous Energy); SAP EHS Safety Permit; Infor EAM Safety Plan

description: Unit of measure for the asset_meter_reading field. Miles or kilometers for vehicle odometers; hours for engine hour meters; cycles for equipment with cycle-based PM intervals.
reference: SAP PM Counter Unit (ZAEHL); Infor EAM Meter UOM; DOT 49 CFR Part 396

description: Source system that created and owns this work order record. Determines the authoritative system of record for reconciliation. SAP_PM indicates SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance module; INFOR_EAM indicates Infor Enterprise Asset Management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PM Module; Infor EAM CMMS

description: Actual cost of spare parts and materials consumed during work order execution in USD. Sourced from SAP MM goods issue or Infor EAM parts transactions. Enables parts vs. labor cost split analysis for maintenance optimization.
reference: SAP MM Material Cost (MATNR/DMBTR); Infor EAM Parts Cost; GAAP Inventory Expensing

description: Purchase order number from SAP MM associated with external services or parts procurement for this work order. Links maintenance execution to the procurement process for AP matching and spend management.
reference: SAP MM Purchase Order (EBELN); Infor EAM PO Reference; GAAP AP Matching

description: Business priority level assigned to the work order, driving scheduling urgency and resource allocation. Critical = immediate safety or regulatory risk (e.g., brake failure, DOT out-of-service condition); High = significant operational impact; Medium = moderate impact; Low = minor issue; Routine = standard PM cycle.
reference: SAP PM Priority (PRIOK); Infor EAM Priority; OSHA 29 CFR 1910 (Safety Priority)

description: Indicates whether this work order is required to satisfy a regulatory compliance obligation beyond DOT (e.g., EPA equipment inspection, OSHA mandated maintenance, RCRA hazardous waste equipment certification, Clean Air Act emission control maintenance). Triggers enhanced documentation and retention requirements.
reference: EPA 40 CFR; OSHA 29 CFR 1910; RCRA 40 CFR Part 264; Clean Air Act Section 112

description: The business event date on which the maintenance need was identified and the work order was formally requested. Represents the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP canonical category. Distinct from created_timestamp (system entry time) — captures the real-world trigger date for SLA and compliance measurement.
reference: SAP PM Basic Start Date (GSTRP); Infor EAM Required Date; ISO 55001

description: Technician-documented root cause of the failure or maintenance need, captured upon work order completion. Supports reliability-centered maintenance (RCM) analysis, repeat failure prevention, and regulatory reporting for DOT and OSHA incidents.
reference: SAP PM Root Cause (URSAC); Infor EAM Root Cause; ISO 55001 Failure Analysis; RCRA Corrective Action

description: Planned date on which the maintenance work is expected to be completed. Used for fleet availability commitments, route planning coordination, and maintenance window management.
reference: SAP PM Scheduled Finish (GLTRP); Infor EAM Scheduled End; SWANA Fleet Management Guidelines

description: Planned date on which the maintenance work is scheduled to begin. Used for workforce scheduling, parts pre-staging, and fleet availability planning. May differ from actual_start_date if work is rescheduled.
reference: SAP PM Scheduled Start (GSTRP); Infor EAM Scheduled Start; NWRA Fleet Maintenance Best Practices

description: Native primary key or internal identifier of this work order in the originating system (SAP PM order number or Infor EAM internal WO ID). Enables bidirectional traceability between the lakehouse Silver layer and the operational system of record.
reference: SAP PM AUFNR Field; Infor EAM WO_ID

description: Date and time of the most recent modification to the work order record in the originating system. Represents the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED canonical category. Used for change detection in ETL pipelines and audit compliance.
reference: SAP PM AEDAT; Infor EAM Last Modified Date; SOX Section 404

description: Indicates whether the repair or parts replacement on this work order is eligible for or has been submitted as a warranty claim to the OEM or parts supplier. When True, actual_cost may be partially or fully recovered. Supports warranty recovery tracking and vendor management.
reference: Infor EAM Warranty Management; SAP PM Warranty Claim; GAAP Warranty Accounting

description: Externally-known, human-readable work order number assigned by the originating system (SAP PM or Infor EAM). Used for cross-system reference, technician communication, and regulatory documentation such as DOT inspection records.
reference: SAP PM PM01 Order Number; Infor EAM WO Number; DOT 49 CFR Part 396

description: Current lifecycle state of the work order. Open = created but not started; In_Progress = technician actively working; On_Hold = paused pending approval or resources; Pending_Parts = awaiting parts from inventory; Completed = work finished, pending closure review; Closed = fully closed and costed; Cancelled = voided without execution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|in_progress|on_hold|pending_parts|completed|closed|cancelled — promote to reference product]
reference: SAP PM System Status (STAT); Infor EAM WO Status; ISO 55001 Asset Management

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the MRF commodity offtake agreement or sales contract record in the Databricks Silver Layer.
Reference to the commodity buyer (counterparty) master record — the organization purchasing recovered materials from the MRF under this contract.
reference: SAP SD Customer Master; Salesforce CRM Account

description: Reference to the recovered commodity master record (e.g., OCC, HDPE, aluminum, glass) that is the subject of this offtake agreement.
reference: SAP MM Material Master; ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular

description: Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Buyer contracts for commodity sales are specialized service agreements governed by the master contract framework. Operations teams track contract lifecycle, amendments, renewals, terminations, and com

Foreign key linking to compliance.permit. Business justification: Export contracts for recyclable commodities require EPA export permits and international waste shipment permits (Basel Convention compliance). Enables verification of export authorization and tracking
Reference to the MRF facility originating this commodity sales contract. Identifies which processing facility is the selling party.
reference: Waste Management Facility Master; EPA SWIS Facility Registry

business_glossary_term: Minimum Quality Commodity Grade Id (Foreign Key)
description: Foreign key linking to recycling.commodity_grade. Business justification: A recycling buyer contract specifies a minimum quality grade requirement for accepted commodity. recycling_buyer_contract currently stores minimum_quality_grade as a denormalized string. Adding minimu

Foreign key linking to recycling.mrf_facility. Business justification: A recycling buyer contract (offtake agreement) is between a specific MRF facility and a commodity buyer. recycling_buyer_contract already has facility_id → asset.facility (cross-domain) but lacks the

business_glossary_term: Salesforce Customer Relationship Management (CRM) Contract ID
description: The Salesforce CRM contract record identifier linked to this commodity offtake agreement. Used for cross-system traceability between the MRF data product and the CRM contract management module.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contract Management; Waste Management CRM Integration Standards

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Recycling buyer contracts for export or hazardous-adjacent materials are governed by specific regulatory requirements (Basel Convention, EPA export rules, state recycling broker regulations). Complian
Indicates whether this contract automatically renews at expiration unless either party provides written notice of non-renewal within the renewal_notice_days period.
The contractually specified target bale weight in pounds for commodity shipments under this agreement. Deviations from this specification may trigger price adjustments or load rejections.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; Waste Management MRF Baling Standards

description: The contractually agreed base price per short ton for the commodity in US dollars. For fixed-price contracts, this is the locked price. For index-linked contracts, this may represent the base or floor price. Used in revenue recognition and commodity sales reporting.
reference: GAAP ASC 606 Revenue Recognition; SAP SD Pricing Condition; Waste Management MRF Revenue Policy

description: The maximum price per short ton the buyer will pay, applicable when price_mechanism is 'floor_and_ceiling'. Caps the MRF's upside in exchange for floor price protection. Null for non-floor-and-ceiling contracts.
reference: Waste Management MRF Commercial Policy; GAAP ASC 606

description: The contractually committed volume of recovered commodity in short tons per month that Waste Management agrees to supply to the buyer. Used for production planning, route optimization, and SLA compliance tracking.
reference: Waste Management MRF Operations Standard; NWRA Commodity Sales Guidelines

description: The method by which contamination penalties are assessed: per_ton (rate applied to excess contamination weight), flat_fee (fixed charge per non-conforming load), price_deduction_pct (percentage reduction from invoice price), or load_rejection (load refused and returned at seller's cost).
reference: Waste Management MRF Commercial Policy; ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular

description: The contractual penalty rate in US dollars per short ton applied to shipments that exceed the maximum allowable contamination percentage. Used to calculate contamination surcharge deductions from commodity revenue.
reference: Waste Management MRF Commercial Policy; GAAP ASC 606

description: Free-text field for capturing additional contract terms, special conditions, buyer-specific requirements, or operational notes not captured in structured fields.

Current lifecycle state of the commodity offtake agreement. Draft: under negotiation; Active: binding and in force; Suspended: temporarily paused; Expired: reached end date without renewal; Terminated: cancelled before end date.
reference: Waste Management Contract Lifecycle Policy; SAP SD Contract Status

description: Classification of the commodity sales agreement by duration and commitment structure. Spot contracts are single-transaction; short-term are typically under 12 months; long-term offtake agreements are multi-year committed volume arrangements; evergreen contracts auto-renew; trial contracts are provisional.
reference: Waste Management MRF Commercial Policy; NWRA Commodity Sales Guidelines

description: The timestamp when this buyer contract record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SOX compliance reporting.
reference: SOX Section 404; Waste Management Data Governance Policy; GAAP Audit Trail Requirements

description: The agreed delivery terms defining when title and risk of loss transfer from Waste Management to the buyer. FOB Origin: buyer takes title at MRF loading dock; FOB Destination: title transfers at buyer's facility; Ex Works: buyer arranges all transport.
reference: ICC Incoterms 2020; SAP SD Shipping Conditions; DOT Freight Regulations

description: The contractually agreed method for resolving disputes between Waste Management and the commodity buyer regarding contract terms, quality, or payment.
reference: Waste Management Legal Contract Policy; NWRA Dispute Resolution Guidelines

description: Indicates whether this contract includes provisions for diversion rate credits or incentives tied to the MRF's overall diversion rate performance. Supports EPA recycling reporting and circular economy goal tracking.
reference: EPA Recycling Program Guidelines; Waste Management Sustainability Policy; ISO 14001

description: The date on which the commodity offtake agreement expires or is scheduled to terminate. Null for open-ended evergreen contracts.
reference: GAAP ASC 606 Revenue Recognition; SAP SD Contract Validity

description: Indicates whether this contract grants the buyer exclusive rights to purchase a specific commodity from this MRF facility, preventing Waste Management from selling that commodity to competing buyers during the contract term.
reference: Waste Management MRF Commercial Policy; Antitrust Compliance Guidelines

description: The date on which the contract was formally signed and executed by both parties. May differ from the effective start date for contracts with retroactive or future effective dates.
reference: GAAP ASC 606; Waste Management Legal Contract Policy

description: The minimum guaranteed price per short ton below which the buyer will not pay, applicable when price_mechanism is 'floor_and_ceiling'. Protects the MRF from extreme market downturns. Null for non-floor-and-ceiling contracts.
reference: Waste Management MRF Commercial Policy; GAAP ASC 606

description: Indicates whether the contract contains a force majeure clause that suspends obligations in the event of extraordinary circumstances (natural disasters, regulatory shutdowns, pandemics). Relevant for EPA/OSHA emergency compliance scenarios.
reference: Waste Management Legal Contract Policy; CERCLA Emergency Provisions

description: The U.S. state whose laws govern the interpretation and enforcement of this commodity sales contract (e.g., 'TX', 'CA', 'FL'). Relevant for dispute resolution and regulatory compliance.
reference: Waste Management Legal Policy; State Environmental Quality Departments

description: The frequency at which invoices are generated and submitted to the buyer for commodity shipments under this contract. Drives Oracle Revenue Management billing cycle configuration.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management; SAP SD Billing Plan; Waste Management Billing Policy

description: The maximum percentage of contaminants (by weight) permitted in each shipment under this contract before contamination penalty clauses are triggered. Aligns with buyer's incoming quality acceptance criteria.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; Waste Management MRF Quality Policy; EPA Recycling Guidelines

description: The agreed payment terms for commodity invoices under this contract (e.g., 'Net 30', 'Net 15', '2/10 Net 30'). Governs accounts receivable aging and cash flow forecasting for MRF commodity revenue.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management; SAP SD Payment Terms; GAAP ASC 606

description: The published market index or benchmark used to determine the commodity price when price_mechanism is 'index_linked' or 'floor_and_ceiling' (e.g., 'RISI Yellow Sheet OCC #11', 'LME Aluminum', 'ISRI Zorba'). Null for fixed-price contracts.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; RISI Recovered Fiber Index; LME Metal Prices

description: The pricing methodology governing how the commodity sale price is determined under this contract. Fixed: set price per ton; Index-linked: tied to a published market index; Floor-and-ceiling: bounded index pricing; Negotiated: periodic bilateral negotiation; Revenue-share: buyer and seller split net proceeds.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; RISI/RISI Fiber Index; Waste Management MRF Pricing Policy

description: Number of calendar days prior to the contract end date by which either party must provide written notice of intent to renew or not renew the agreement.
Indicates whether the contract contains a renewal option clause allowing either party to extend the agreement beyond the original end date.
reference: Waste Management MRF Commercial Policy; GAAP ASC 842

business_glossary_term: SAP Sales and Distribution (SD) Contract Number
description: The SAP S/4HANA SD module contract document number (scheduling agreement or quantity contract) linked to this MRF commodity sales agreement. Used for cross-system reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Module; SAP Contract Management

description: The date on which the commodity offtake agreement becomes legally binding and commodity deliveries may commence under its terms.
reference: GAAP ASC 606 Revenue Recognition; SAP SD Contract Validity

description: The actual date on which the contract was terminated early, if applicable. Null for contracts that expire naturally at end_date or remain active.
reference: Waste Management Contract Termination Policy; SAP SD

description: Reason code explaining why the contract was terminated before its scheduled end date. Populated only when contract_status is 'terminated'. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: buyer_default|seller_default|mutual_agreement|market_exit|regulatory|force_majeure|non_renewal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Identifies which party is responsible for arranging and paying for transportation of commodity bales from the MRF to the buyer's facility. Impacts logistics cost allocation and fleet planning.
reference: Waste Management MRF Logistics Policy; DOT Freight Regulations

description: The timestamp when this buyer contract record was most recently modified. Used for change tracking, data synchronization, and audit compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404; Waste Management Data Governance Policy

description: The allowable percentage variance (plus or minus) from the committed monthly volume before penalty or renegotiation clauses are triggered. For example, 10% means the MRF may deliver between 90% and 110% of committed volume without penalty.
reference: Waste Management MRF Commercial Policy; NWRA Commodity Sales Guidelines

description: Unique identifier for the commodity buyer record. Primary key for the commodity buyer entity within the recycling domain.
Indicates whether the buyer is currently active and eligible for commodity sales. True means active and available for transactions, False means inactive and excluded from sales processes.
First line of the buyer's billing address including street number and street name. Used for invoicing and accounts receivable processing.
Second line of the buyer's billing address for suite, floor, or building information. Optional field for additional address details.
City name for the buyer's billing address.
Three-letter ISO country code for the buyer's billing address.
Postal or ZIP code for the buyer's billing address.
State or province code for the buyer's billing address. Uses standard postal abbreviations.
Date when the business relationship with this commodity buyer was first established. Used for buyer tenure analysis and relationship management.
Legal or trade name of the commodity buyer organization that purchases recovered materials from MRF operations.

Classification of the commodity buyer based on their role in the recovered materials supply chain. Domestic mills process materials domestically, export brokers facilitate international sales, reprocessors transform materials into new feedstock, direct end-users consume materials in manufacturing, aggregators consolidate materials from multiple sources, and traders buy and resell commodities.
Primary email address for the buyer contact. Used for purchase orders, invoices, shipping notifications, and quality documentation.
Primary telephone number for the buyer contact. Used for order coordination, quality issues, and logistics communication.
Timestamp when this commodity buyer record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.
Maximum outstanding accounts receivable balance allowed for this buyer in USD. Used for credit risk management and sales order approval workflows.

Reference to the Preventive Maintenance (PM) schedule in Infor EAM that governs routine maintenance intervals, inspection tasks, and lubrication schedules for this sort line.

Identifier used to reference this sort line within the AMCS Platform for recycling route management and material flow tracking integration. Enables cross-system reconciliation between MRF operations and collection route data.

description: Degree of automation on the sort line. Manual lines rely entirely on human sorters; semi-automated lines combine optical sorters or mechanical separators with manual quality control; fully automated lines use robotics and optical sorting with minimal human intervention.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; NWRA Best Practices for MRF Automation

description: Timestamp when this sort line master record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management in the Databricks Silver Layer.

description: Date on which the sort line was permanently taken out of service. Null for active lines. Used for asset lifecycle reporting, capital planning, and EPA facility capacity reporting.

description: Engineered target material recovery rate expressed as a percentage of inbound material weight that is successfully recovered as saleable commodity. Used as the baseline for diversion rate reporting and performance benchmarking against EPA recycling goals.
reference: EPA Recycling Program Guidelines; SWANA MRF Operations Standards; ISO 14001

description: Engineered design capacity of the sort line expressed in tons per day (TPD), calculated based on standard operating shift hours. Used for daily production planning, EPA recycling reporting, and diversion rate calculations.
reference: EPA Recycling Program Guidelines; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Engineered design capacity of the sort line expressed in tons per hour (TPH). Represents the maximum rated processing rate under optimal conditions as specified by the equipment manufacturer or system integrator. Used for capacity planning and performance benchmarking.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; EPA Recycling Measurement Guidelines

description: Indicates whether a dust suppression or dust collection system is installed on this sort line. Required for air quality compliance under the Clean Air Act and OSHA particulate matter exposure limits, particularly on glass and shredder lines.

description: Number of eddy current separator units installed on this sort line. Eddy current separators use magnetic induction to eject non-ferrous metals (aluminum, copper) from the material stream. Critical for aluminum recovery rates.

description: Total number of individual equipment assets installed on this sort line (e.g., balers, optical sorters, conveyors, eddy current separators, magnetic separators, shredders, glass breakers). Used for maintenance planning and asset inventory management.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle Management; SAP PM Plant Maintenance

description: Indicates whether an automated fire suppression system (e.g., sprinkler, spark detection and suppression) is installed on this sort line. MRF sort lines processing paper and plastics carry elevated fire risk; fire suppression is required by NFPA 13 and local fire codes.

description: Total floor area occupied by the sort line and its associated equipment in square meters. Used for facility layout planning, fire safety compliance, and OSHA egress requirement calculations.

description: Number of glass breaking/crushing units installed on this sort line. Glass breakers reduce glass to cullet for downstream processing or disposal. Relevant for container sort lines processing single-stream material.

description: Date on which the sort line was physically installed and commissioned at the facility. Used to calculate asset age, depreciation schedules, and to trigger lifecycle replacement planning.

description: Date of the most recent major overhaul or capital refurbishment of the sort line. Used to assess remaining useful life, schedule next overhaul, and support capital expenditure planning.

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the sort line within the facility (e.g., MRF-SL-01, OCC-LINE-A). Used in operational communications, work orders, and reporting.

description: Human-readable descriptive name for the sort line (e.g., 'OCC Pre-Sort Line 1', 'Fiber Sorting Line B'). Used in operational dashboards, maintenance scheduling, and facility floor plans.

description: Current operational lifecycle status of the sort line. description: Classification of the sort line by the material stream it processes. description: Number of magnetic separator (overhead magnet or drum magnet) units installed on this sort line for ferrous metal recovery. Ferrous metal recovery rate is directly dependent on magnetic separator performance.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle Management

description: Name of the original equipment manufacturer (OEM) who designed and built the sort line system (e.g., Machinex, Van Dyk, CP Group, Bollegraaf). Used for warranty management, spare parts sourcing, and technical support.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle Management; SAP MM Materials Management

description: Maximum acceptable contamination rate (percentage of non-target material in the output stream) for this sort line before commodity quality is rejected by buyers. Exceeding this threshold triggers quality control interventions and may affect commodity sale prices.

description: Manufacturer's model designation for the sort line system. Used for spare parts identification, technical documentation retrieval, and warranty claims.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle Management; SAP MM Materials Management

description: Rated operational noise level of the sort line in decibels (dB). Used for OSHA hearing conservation program compliance, PPE (Personal Protective Equipment) requirements determination, and facility noise permit compliance.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.95 Occupational Noise Exposure; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety

description: Number of production shifts per day this sort line is designed to operate (typically 1, 2, or 3 shifts). Used for daily throughput capacity calculations, workforce scheduling, and operational cost modeling.

description: Number of optical sorting units installed on this sort line. Optical sorters use near-infrared (NIR) or visible spectrum technology to identify and eject specific material types. Drives automation level classification and throughput capacity.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle Management

description: Rated electrical power consumption of the sort line in kilowatts (KW) at full operating capacity. Used for energy cost modeling, utility budgeting, and greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions reporting under EPA and ISO 14001 requirements.
reference: EPA GHG Reporting Program; ISO 14001 Environmental Management Systems; Clean Air Act

description: Target percentage of inbound material that is expected to be classified as residue (non-recoverable waste sent to landfill disposal). Lower residue rates indicate higher diversion performance. Used in EPA diversion rate reporting and sustainability KPI tracking.
reference: EPA Recycling Program Guidelines; SWANA Diversion Rate Standards; ISO 14001

description: Indicates whether RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) technology is installed on this sort line for automated container or material tracking. Supports integration with AMCS Platform for container management and material flow tracking.

description: SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance (PM) functional location code assigned to this sort line. Used to link maintenance work orders, preventive maintenance plans, and cost center allocations in SAP PM and FI/CO modules.
