description: Reference to the inbound material receiving load or manifest from which the commodity in this bale originated. Enables full traceability from inbound collection route through MRF processing to outbound commodity sale. Supports diversion rate calculations and material flow analytics.
reference: EPA Recycling Traceability Requirements; AMCS Platform Route Management; SWANA MRF Standards

description: Reference to the baler machine that produced this bale. Supports equipment performance monitoring, preventive maintenance scheduling, and bale quality correlation with specific baler units via Infor EAM.
reference: Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle Management; SWANA MRF Equipment Standards

description: Foreign key linking to recycling.mrf_facility. Business justification: A bale is produced at a specific MRF facility. bale already has facility_id → asset.facility (cross-domain) but lacks the in-domain mrf_facility_id link. This FK enables MRF-level bale inventory repor
Reference to the sorting line within the MRF where the commodity was processed and separated prior to baling. Used for equipment performance tracking and throughput analysis by sort line.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; Infor EAM Asset Lifecycle

description: Foreign key linking to recycling.sort_session. Business justification: A bale is produced during a specific sort session. bale already has sort_line_id but lacks the sort_session_id that identifies the production session. This FK enables traceability from bale back to th
Reference to the designated storage bay, pad, or warehouse location within the MRF where this bale is currently stored. Supports inventory management, FIFO rotation, and outbound logistics planning.
reference: SAP MM Inventory Management; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_profile. Business justification: Contaminated bales exceeding commodity grade specifications and containing hazardous constituents require waste profiling before disposal or specialized treatment. Real business process: rejected bale

Date and time when this bale was allocated to a specific sales order or outbound shipment. Marks the transition from in_storage to allocated lifecycle status. Used for inventory turnover analysis and order fulfillment lead time tracking.
reference: SAP SD Sales and Distribution; Oracle Revenue Management

description: Human-readable barcode label affixed to the bale for physical identification and scanning during storage, quality inspection, and outbound shipment. Printed at time of production and scanned at each lifecycle checkpoint.
reference: GS1 Barcode Standards; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Bill of Lading number assigned to the outbound shipment carrying this bale. The BOL is the primary freight transport document required by DOT regulations. Null until bale is loaded for shipment. Enables shipment tracking and dispute resolution with commodity buyers.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Freight Documentation; UCC Article 7 Bills of Lading; SAP SD Transportation

description: Unique quality certification document number issued for this bale upon passing inspection, confirming it meets the specified commodity grade and buyer quality requirements. Required for certain commodity buyers and export shipments. Null if no formal certification was issued.
reference: ISO 9001 Quality Management; ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; Export Commodity Certification Requirements

description: Measured percentage of non-target or non-recyclable material (contaminants) present in the bale, expressed as a percentage of total bale weight. Determined during quality inspection. Exceeding buyer-specified contamination thresholds results in grade downgrade, price deduction, or rejection. Key MRF quality KPI.
reference: EPA Recycling Quality Guidelines; ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; Buyer Contract Specifications

description: Date and time when this bale record was first created in the system, typically at the moment of bale production completion. Serves as the audit trail creation timestamp for data lineage, Silver layer ingestion tracking, and SOX compliance recordkeeping.
reference: SOX Section 404 Internal Controls; GAAP Audit Trail Requirements; ISO 14001 Environmental Records

description: Number of days this bale has been in MRF storage since production. Tracked to manage inventory aging, prioritize FIFO shipment sequencing, and identify slow-moving commodity inventory that may be subject to quality degradation or market price risk. Updated daily.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; SAP MM Inventory Aging

description: Calculated density of the bale expressed as kilograms per cubic meter, derived from weight and physical dimensions. Indicates baler compression performance and material compaction ratio. Low density may signal baler maintenance needs or loose material feed. Used for baler performance KPI tracking.
reference: SWANA MRF Equipment Performance Standards; Infor EAM Asset Performance

description: Estimated monetary value of this bale in USD, calculated as weight in tons multiplied by the applicable commodity market price per ton. Used for MRF commodity inventory valuation, revenue forecasting, and financial reporting in SAP FI/CO. Not a final invoiced amount — actual sale price is recorded on the sales order.
reference: SAP FI/CO Financial Reporting; Oracle Revenue Management; GAAP Inventory Valuation Standards

description: Physical height dimension of the bale in centimeters as measured at production. Combined with length and width for volume calculation, stacking safety assessment, and storage bay capacity planning.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; OSHA 29 CFR 1910 Storage Safety

description: Outcome of the most recent quality inspection for this bale. Pass indicates the bale meets all applicable grade and buyer specifications; fail results in rejection or rework; conditional pass indicates the bale is accepted with noted deviations that are within buyer tolerance.
reference: SWANA MRF Quality Assurance Standards; ISO 9001 Quality Management; Buyer Contract Specifications

description: Date and time when the most recent quality inspection was performed on this bale. Used for inspection age tracking, pre-shipment inspection validity windows, and audit trail for quality certification documentation.
reference: SWANA MRF Quality Assurance Standards; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Type of quality inspection performed on this bale. Production QC is conducted at time of baling; pre-shipment inspection occurs before loading; buyer-requested inspection is initiated by the commodity buyer; re-inspection follows a prior rejection or dispute. Determines inspection protocol and documentation requirements.
reference: SWANA MRF Quality Assurance Standards; ISO 9001 Quality Management; Buyer Contract Specifications

description: Physical length dimension of the bale in centimeters as measured at production. Used with width and height to calculate bale volume, verify baler performance, and plan trailer loading configurations for outbound commodity shipments.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; DOT Freight Dimension Requirements

description: Current operational state of the bale within its lifecycle from production through disposition. In_storage: produced and available in MRF inventory; allocated: assigned to a sales order or shipment; shipped: loaded and departed facility; rejected: failed quality inspection and removed from saleable inventory; on_hold: pending re-inspection or buyer dispute resolution.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; SAP MM Inventory Management

description: Prevailing commodity market price per ton in USD at the time this bale was produced or allocated to a sales order. Captured for commodity inventory valuation, revenue forecasting, and market price trend analysis. Reflects spot market or contracted price depending on buyer agreement type.
reference: ISRI Commodity Pricing Index; SAP MM Material Valuation; Oracle Revenue Management

description: Measured moisture content of the bale expressed as a percentage of total bale weight. Excess moisture reduces net commodity value, increases transport weight without material value, and can cause mold or degradation in paper bales. Monitored against buyer moisture tolerance thresholds.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; Buyer Contract Specifications; SWANA MRF Quality Standards

description: Free-text field for operational notes, quality observations, or special handling instructions associated with this bale. Used by MRF operators and quality inspectors to document non-standard conditions not captured in structured fields (e.g., visible damage, unusual contamination type, buyer special instructions).

MRF operating shift during which this bale was produced. Used for shift-level productivity analysis, quality trend analysis by shift, and workforce scheduling optimization. Correlates bale quality and throughput metrics to specific shift teams.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; Workday HCM Workforce Scheduling

description: Date and time when the bale was ejected from the baler and production was completed. The principal business event timestamp for this MASTER_RESOURCE. Used for throughput calculations (Tons Per Day), shift productivity reporting, and bale age tracking for FIFO inventory management.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; EPA Recycling Throughput Reporting

description: Indicates whether this bale requires a follow-up quality inspection, typically after remediation of a failed inspection, buyer dispute, or conditional pass with noted concerns. True triggers a re-inspection workflow in the MRF quality management process.
reference: SWANA MRF Quality Assurance Standards; ISO 9001 Quality Management

description: Documented reason for bale rejection when inspection_result is fail or lifecycle_status is rejected. Common reasons include excessive contamination, moisture above threshold, broken wires, incorrect commodity mix, or bale integrity failure. Required for root cause analysis and MRF process improvement. Null when bale passes inspection.
reference: SWANA MRF Quality Assurance Standards; EPA Recycling Program Guidelines

description: RFID tag identifier embedded in or attached to the bale for automated tracking through MRF storage, loading dock, and outbound logistics. Enables real-time inventory visibility without manual scanning. Null if facility does not use RFID.
reference: GS1 RFID Standards (EPC Gen2); AMCS Platform Container Tracking

description: Date and time when this bale was loaded onto an outbound transport vehicle and departed the MRF facility. Marks the transition to shipped lifecycle status. Used for inventory reconciliation, Bill of Lading (BOL) documentation, and commodity revenue recognition timing.
reference: SAP SD Shipping and Transportation; DOT Freight Documentation; GAAP Revenue Recognition (ASC 606)

description: Date and time when this bale record was most recently modified. Tracks lifecycle status changes, quality inspection updates, allocation events, and shipment confirmations. Used for incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer and audit compliance.
reference: SOX Section 404 Internal Controls; Databricks Delta Lake Change Data Capture; ISO 14001 Environmental Records

description: Gross weight of the bale in kilograms as measured by the baler scale or floor scale at time of production. The principal quantitative measurement for this resource. Used for Tons Per Day (TPD) throughput calculations, commodity inventory valuation, sales order fulfillment, and EPA recycling tonnage reporting.
reference: EPA Recycling Tonnage Reporting; NIST Handbook 44 Weighing Standards; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Physical width dimension of the bale in centimeters as measured at production. Combined with length and height for volume calculation, storage space planning, and trailer load optimization.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; DOT Freight Dimension Requirements

description: Number of wire ties or strapping bands securing the bale. Standard bale configurations use 4, 5, or 6 wires depending on commodity type and baler model. Deviations from standard wire count may indicate bale integrity risk during transport and are flagged during quality inspection.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; ISRI Bale Specification Guidelines

description: Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for each recovered commodity master record in the Materials Recovery Facility (MRF). Primary key for the commodity reference table. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; AMCS Platform Commodity Catalog

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Commodities are governed by specific regulatory requirements (RCRA material classifications, EPA export rules, state hazardous thresholds). Compliance managers need this link to enforce handling/proce

Foreign key linking to hazmat.waste_code. Business justification: Commodities with hazardous_material_flag=true carry a denormalized rcra_waste_code plain column. A proper FK to hazmat.waste_code enforces referential integrity, enables RCRA regulatory reporting by c

Foreign key linking to service.waste_stream. Business justification: Each recyclable commodity originates from a specific waste stream (e.g., OCC from commercial cardboard stream, HDPE from residential single-stream). This link supports diversion credit calculations, c

Narrative description of materials explicitly accepted within this commodity stream (e.g., 'Corrugated containers, kraft paper bags' for OCC). Complements the prohibited materials list to fully define the commodity specification for MRF operations and customer education.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; EPA Recycling Reporting Guidelines

description: Commodity identifier as configured in the AMCS Platform for route management and MRF dispatching. Used to link commodity master records to AMCS recycling route assignments, container tracking, and inbound material receiving workflows.
reference: AMCS Platform Route Optimization and MRF Operations Module

description: Indicates whether this commodity must be baled prior to outbound shipment to end-market buyers. Drives MRF baler scheduling, bale production tracking, and outbound logistics planning. False for loose commodities such as glass cullet or scrap metal.
reference: AMCS Platform MRF Operations Module; NWRA Baling Standards

description: Current or standard base price per ton (USD) for this commodity as used in MRF commodity sales. For positive-value commodities, this is the revenue received per ton sold. For negative-value commodities, this is the cost paid per ton disposed. Sensitive commercial pricing data. Integrated with Oracle Revenue Management and SAP FI/CO.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards; SOX Compliance

description: High-level material category grouping for the commodity. Drives MRF sorting line configuration, equipment routing, and EPA diversion rate reporting by material stream. Serves as the primary CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this MASTER_RESOURCE. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: paper|plastics|metals|glass|organics|electronics|textiles|residue|other — promote to reference product]
reference: EPA Recycling Reporting Guidelines; SWANA Material Classification Standards; ISO 14001

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the commodity type (e.g., OCC, HDPE, ALU, ONP). Used as the business-facing identifier in SAP MM material master, AMCS commodity catalog, and commodity sales contracts. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_RESOURCE.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; AMCS Platform

description: Full descriptive name of the recovered commodity (e.g., 'Old Corrugated Containers', 'High-Density Polyethylene Natural', 'Aluminum Used Beverage Cans'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this MASTER_RESOURCE and is used in customer-facing reports, commodity sales invoices, and EPA recycling disclosures.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; EPA Recycling Reporting Guidelines

description: Current lifecycle status of the commodity master record. Active commodities are processed and sold from MRF operations. Inactive or discontinued commodities are retained for historical reporting. Pending_approval records are under review for addition to the MRF commodity catalog. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_RESOURCE.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; AMCS Platform Commodity Catalog

description: Fee charged per ton (USD) when a commodity load exceeds the maximum contamination percentage threshold. Applied as a deduction from the commodity sale price or as a separate charge to the originating customer account. Configured in Oracle Revenue Management billing rules.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management; SAP S/4HANA SD; NWRA Contamination Fee Standards

description: Timestamp when this commodity master record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail requirements, and SOX-compliant record management. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Indicates whether tonnage of this commodity counts toward the diversion rate calculation (percentage of waste diverted from landfill). True for recyclable and compostable commodities; false for residue streams that are landfilled. Drives EPA and state-level diversion rate reporting.
reference: EPA Recycling Reporting Guidelines; State Environmental Quality Departments; SWANA Diversion Rate Standards

description: Date from which this commodity master record and its associated grade specifications became effective for MRF operations and commodity sales. Used to manage commodity specification version history and ensure correct specifications are applied to historical transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards

description: Indicates whether this commodity is sold to domestic end-market buyers, exported to international markets, or both. Drives logistics planning, export documentation requirements (BOL, customs), and market pricing strategy. Export commodities may require additional DOT and customs compliance.
reference: DOT Export Regulations; NWRA Market Reporting; SAP SD Export Management

description: EPA-assigned material category code used in mandatory recycling and diversion rate reporting submissions. Aligns commodity records with EPA's Advancing Sustainable Materials Management (SMM) reporting framework and state environmental quality department reporting requirements.
reference: EPA Advancing Sustainable Materials Management (SMM); Clean Air Act; Clean Water Act; State Environmental Quality Departments

description: Date on which this commodity master record or its grade specifications expire and must be reviewed or replaced. Null for open-ended commodity definitions. Supports commodity specification lifecycle management and ensures outdated specifications are not applied to active operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular

description: Greenhouse gas (GHG) emission factor in metric tons of carbon dioxide equivalent (CO2e) per ton of commodity recycled versus landfilled. Used to calculate GHG emission reductions from recycling activities for sustainability reporting, EPA GHG inventory submissions, and circular economy goal tracking.
reference: EPA GHG Reporting Program (40 CFR Part 98); EPA WARM Model; ISO 14001; GHG Protocol

description: SAP General Ledger (GL) account code to which commodity sales revenue or disposal costs are posted. Ensures accurate financial reporting and P&L attribution by commodity type. Required for SAP FI/CO integration and SOX-compliant financial controls.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO General Ledger; IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards; SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act)

description: Narrative description of the ISRI grade specification including allowable materials, prohibited items, and quality characteristics. Used by MRF quality control staff to assess bale acceptance and by sales teams to communicate product quality to end-market buyers.

Indicates whether this commodity is classified as a hazardous material under RCRA or DOT regulations. When true, triggers special handling, manifesting, and TSDF routing requirements. Integrates with Enviance EHS for compliance tracking and SAP EHS module for hazardous material management.
reference: RCRA (Resource Conservation and Recovery Act); DOT 49 CFR Hazardous Materials Regulations; EPA RCRA Subtitle C

business_glossary_term: Institute of Scrap Recycling Industries (ISRI) Grade Code
description: Standardized grade code assigned by the Institute of Scrap Recycling Industries (ISRI) Scrap Specifications Circular. Defines the accepted quality standard for the commodity and is referenced in commodity purchase/sale contracts, bill of lading (BOL) documentation, and end-market buyer specifications.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; National Waste and Recycling Association (NWRA) Standards

description: Timestamp when this commodity master record was most recently modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline, audit trail compliance, and detecting stale commodity specifications. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: SOX (Sarbanes-Oxley Act); IFRS/GAAP Financial Reporting Standards; SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Indicates whether the commodity generates revenue (positive_value — MRF receives payment from buyer), incurs a cost (negative_value — MRF pays buyer to accept), or is non-marketable residue. Critical for commodity sales pricing strategy, P&L reporting, and Oracle Revenue Management billing configuration.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; NWRA Market Reporting Standards

business_glossary_term: Maximum Bale Density (lbs per cubic foot)
description: Maximum allowable bale density in pounds per cubic foot for this commodity. Overly dense bales may be rejected by buyers due to handling or processing constraints. Used alongside minimum density to define the acceptable bale density range for quality control.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; NWRA Baling Standards

description: Maximum allowable contamination percentage (by weight) for an accepted bale or load of this commodity. Loads exceeding this threshold are subject to rejection, downgrade, or contamination fee deduction. Drives MRF quality control acceptance/rejection decisions and end-market buyer contract compliance.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; EPA Recycling Reporting Guidelines; NWRA Quality Standards

description: Maximum allowable moisture content percentage (by weight) for this commodity. Excess moisture reduces commodity value, increases transportation weight, and may cause rejection by end-market buyers. Used in MRF quality control inspections and bale certification.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; NWRA Quality Standards

description: Maximum number of days this commodity may be stored at the MRF before quality degradation or regulatory limits require outbound shipment. Drives inventory turnover alerts in SAP MM and AMCS commodity inventory management. Critical for hazardous commodities with RCRA storage time limits.
reference: RCRA 40 CFR Part 262 Hazardous Waste Generator Standards; SAP S/4HANA MM; OSHA

business_glossary_term: Minimum Bale Density (lbs per cubic foot)
description: Minimum required bale density in pounds per cubic foot for this commodity. Bales below this density threshold may be rejected by buyers or subject to price discounts. Used to configure MRF baling equipment settings and quality control acceptance criteria.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; NWRA Baling Standards

description: Minimum expected processing throughput in tons per day (TPD) for this commodity on the MRF sort line. Used for MRF capacity planning, equipment performance benchmarking, and operational KPI reporting. Below-threshold throughput triggers operational review.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; AMCS Platform MRF Throughput Reporting

description: Description of required Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) for MRF workers handling this commodity (e.g., 'Cut-resistant gloves, safety glasses' for metals; 'Respirator, chemical-resistant gloves' for hazardous commodities). Supports OSHA compliance and worker safety training programs.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.132 PPE Standards; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; Enviance EHS

description: Minimum acceptable sale price per ton (USD) below which the commodity should not be sold without management approval. Used in Oracle Revenue Management to trigger pricing exception workflows and protect MRF revenue margins during market downturns.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management; SAP S/4HANA FI/CO; SOX Compliance; IFRS/GAAP

description: Method by which the commodity sale price is determined: spot (current market spot price), index (tied to a published commodity index such as RISI or AMM), contract (fixed price per sales contract), or negotiated (case-by-case negotiation). Drives Oracle Revenue Management pricing configuration and commodity sales contract terms.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management; SAP SD Pricing; RISI Commodity Index; AMM Metal Prices

description: Narrative description of materials explicitly prohibited from inclusion in this commodity stream (e.g., 'No food-soiled paper, no wax-coated board' for OCC; 'No PVC, no colored HDPE' for natural HDPE). Used by MRF sorting staff, quality control inspectors, and driver education programs to prevent contamination.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; EPA Recycling Reporting Guidelines

description: Classification of the commodity's recyclability and market demand: tier_1 (high-value, readily recyclable with established markets), tier_2 (moderate-value, recyclable with limited markets), tier_3 (low-value, difficult to recycle), residue (non-recyclable, destined for disposal). Drives diversion rate calculations and sustainability reporting.
reference: EPA Recycling Reporting Guidelines; ISO 14001 Environmental Management Systems; SWANA

description: Name of the published commodity price index used as the pricing reference for index-priced commodities (e.g., 'RISI OCC Index', 'AMM Aluminum Index', 'Fastmarkets HDPE Index'). Null for spot or contract-priced commodities. Used in Oracle Revenue Management for automated price updates.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management; RISI Commodity Index; AMM Metal Prices; Fastmarkets

description: Society of the Plastics Industry (SPI) Resin Identification Code (RIC) for plastic commodities (1=PET, 2=HDPE, 3=PVC, 4=LDPE, 5=PP, 6=PS, 7=Other). Null for non-plastic commodities. Required for plastics market reporting and end-market buyer specifications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: 1|2|3|4|5|6|7 — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: ASTM D7611 Plastics Resin Identification Coding System; EPA Recycling Reporting

description: Code identifying the MRF sorting line or processing stream assigned to this commodity (e.g., 'FIBER-1', 'PLASTIC-2', 'METAL-OCC'). Used by AMCS Platform for route-to-sort-line assignment and MRF equipment configuration. Supports throughput (TPD) reporting by sort line.
reference: AMCS Platform MRF Operations Module; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Target or standard weight in pounds for a single bale of this commodity as produced by MRF baling equipment. Used for bale production planning, outbound load configuration, and weight-based pricing reconciliation with end-market buyers.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; NWRA Baling Standards; SAP MM Material Master

description: Storage condition requirement for this commodity at the MRF prior to outbound shipment (e.g., indoor for paper to prevent moisture, hazmat_storage for hazardous commodities, outdoor for metals). Drives MRF facility layout planning and OSHA/EPA storage compliance.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910 Storage Standards; EPA RCRA Storage Requirements; ISO 45001

description: Secondary classification within the material category providing finer segmentation (e.g., 'corrugated' within paper, 'PET bottles' within plastics, 'ferrous' within metals). Used for detailed MRF throughput reporting and commodity-specific market pricing.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; EPA Recycling Reporting Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Toxicity Characteristic Leaching Procedure (TCLP) Testing Required Flag
description: Indicates whether Toxicity Characteristic Leaching Procedure (TCLP) testing is required for this commodity prior to disposal or sale. Applicable to commodities that may exhibit hazardous characteristics under RCRA. Triggers laboratory testing workflow in Enviance EHS.
reference: RCRA 40 CFR Part 261.24 TCLP; EPA Hazardous Waste Testing Methods; Enviance EHS

description: Standard unit of measure used for weighing, pricing, and reporting this commodity (e.g., ton for paper/metals, lb for small-volume materials, bale for pre-baled commodities). Aligns with SAP MM base unit of measure and Oracle Revenue Management pricing unit. Serves as the MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE classification for this MASTER_RESOURCE.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Material Master; NIST Weights and Measures; Oracle Revenue Management

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each commodity grade specification record in the Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) quality reference table. [REFERENCE_LOOKUP role — this is a reference/specification table defining grade standards; per canonical-attrs-enforced rule, REFERENCE_LOOKUP is exempt from per-role minimums. _canonical_skip_reason: commodity_grade is a reference lookup table defining quality grade specifications for MRF commodities; it has its own identity and lifecycle but functions as a standards reference, not a transactional or master-party entity.]
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; ISO 14001 Environmental Management Systems

description: Foreign key linking to recycling.commodity. Business justification: commodity_grade defines quality specifications for a specific commodity type. Currently commodity_type and commodity_subtype are stored as denormalized strings on commodity_grade. Adding commodity_id

Reference to the commodity_grade_id of the newer grade specification that replaces this record when a grade is deprecated or revised. Null if this is the current active version. Supports grade version lineage tracking and ensures operational systems transition to updated specifications.
reference: SAP MM Material Master Versioning; ISO 14001 Document Control

description: Geographic region or operational division for which this grade specification applies (e.g., 'Northeast', 'Southeast', 'National'). Some grade specifications may vary by region due to local buyer requirements or state recycling regulations. Null or 'National' indicates the grade applies enterprise-wide.
reference: State Environmental Quality Department Recycling Regulations; SWANA Regional Operations

description: Date on which this commodity grade specification was formally approved by the authorized quality manager or operations director. Required for quality management system documentation and regulatory audit readiness.
reference: ISO 14001 Environmental Management Systems; SWANA MRF Quality Management; SOX Compliance

description: Name or employee identifier of the MRF quality manager or operations director who approved this grade specification for operational use. Required for quality management system compliance and audit trail. Sourced from Workday HCM employee records.
reference: ISO 14001 Environmental Management Systems; ISO 45001 Occupational Health and Safety; SWANA MRF Quality Management

description: Required or target bale dimensions (length x width x height in inches) for this commodity grade as specified by buyers or ISRI standards (e.g., '60x30x48'). Ensures compatibility with buyer receiving equipment and maximizes trailer load efficiency. Used in baler setup configuration.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; SWANA Baling Operations Standards

description: Indicates whether material classified under this grade requires explicit buyer acceptance confirmation before outbound shipment. True for specialty or off-grade materials where buyers must pre-approve loads. False for standard grades with standing purchase agreements. Drives outbound logistics workflow in AMCS.
reference: SWANA MRF Outbound Operations; Commodity Sales Contract Management

business_glossary_term: Carbon Dioxide Equivalent (CO2e) Avoided per Ton
description: Estimated metric tons of CO2e greenhouse gas emissions avoided per ton of commodity recovered and sold at this grade, compared to virgin material production. Used for GHG emissions reporting, sustainability disclosures, and circular economy impact quantification. Sourced from EPA WARM (Waste Reduction Model) emission factors.
reference: EPA WARM Model; GHG Protocol Scope 3 Reporting; ISO 14064 GHG Quantification; Clean Air Act

description: Color or color mix specification required for this commodity grade (e.g., 'clear only', 'clear and green', 'mixed colors acceptable', 'brown only'). Primarily applicable to glass and PET plastic grades where color separation directly impacts end-market value and processing requirements at buyer facilities.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; Glass Packaging Institute Standards

description: Timestamp when this commodity grade specification record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and compliance documentation requirements. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per enterprise data standards.
reference: SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act Audit Trail Requirements; ISO 14001 Document Control; GAAP Record Keeping

description: Indicates whether material sold under this commodity grade qualifies for diversion rate credit in EPA and state recycling program reporting. True for grades that represent genuine material recovery; False for off-grade or contaminated material routed to landfill or WTE. Impacts sustainability KPI reporting and circular economy goal tracking.
reference: EPA Recycling Diversion Rate Reporting; State Environmental Quality Department Recycling Regulations; ISO 14001

description: Indicates whether this commodity grade is eligible for domestic sale only, export sale only, or both. Export eligibility is subject to Basel Convention restrictions on hazardous waste exports and country-specific import regulations. Drives market routing decisions in commodity sales management.
reference: Basel Convention on Hazardous Waste; EPA Export Regulations; DOT International Shipping Requirements

description: Date from which this commodity grade specification becomes effective and applicable for quality control acceptance/rejection decisions and commodity pricing. Enables versioned grade specifications to align with market condition changes or updated ISRI standards.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular (versioning); SAP MM Validity Periods

description: Type of end-market or buyer facility that typically purchases this commodity grade (e.g., 'paper mill', 'plastic reprocessor', 'aluminum smelter', 'glass beneficiator', 'steel mill', 'export broker'). Informs market development strategy and outbound logistics planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: paper_mill|plastic_reprocessor|aluminum_smelter|glass_beneficiator|steel_mill|export_broker|compost_facility|wte_facility — promote to reference product]
reference: SWANA Commodity Markets Report; EPA Recycling Markets Development

description: EPA-assigned commodity classification code used for federal recycling diversion rate reporting and Solid Waste Information System (SWIS) submissions. Enables accurate tracking of material recovery volumes by commodity type for regulatory compliance and sustainability reporting.
reference: EPA Advancing Sustainable Materials Management; EPA SWIS Reporting Requirements; Clean Air Act

description: Date after which this commodity grade specification is no longer valid and must be superseded by a new version. Null indicates the grade specification is open-ended with no planned expiration. Supports versioned grade management when ISRI standards or buyer contract requirements change.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular (versioning); SAP MM Validity Periods

description: Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the commodity grade specification (e.g., OCC-A, PET-1, ALU-P). Used as the externally-known business identifier in SAP MM material master, AMCS commodity tracking, and buyer purchase orders. Aligns with ISRI commodity grade coding conventions.
reference: Institute of Scrap Recycling Industries (ISRI) Scrap Specifications Circular; SAP MM Material Master

description: Human-readable name of the commodity grade specification (e.g., 'Grade A Old Corrugated Containers', 'Premium Clear PET', 'Standard Mixed Paper', 'Off-Grade Aluminum'). Used in quality control reports, buyer contracts, and MRF operational dashboards.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; SWANA MRF Best Practices

description: Current lifecycle status of the commodity grade specification. Active grades are in use for quality control and pricing. Inactive grades are suspended from use. Deprecated grades have been superseded by updated specifications. Under Review grades are pending revision approval. Controls whether the grade is available for selection in MRF quality control workflows.
reference: SAP MM Material Master Status Management; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Quality tier classification of the grade specification indicating relative market value and purity level. Premium and Grade A command market premiums; Standard meets baseline buyer requirements; Off-Grade triggers price discounts; Rejected material is non-saleable and requires alternative disposition. Drives pricing factor application in Oracle Revenue Management.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Indicates whether this commodity grade involves or may contain hazardous materials subject to RCRA, DOT, or EPA hazardous waste regulations. True triggers mandatory hazardous waste handling protocols, manifest documentation requirements, and TSDF routing. Integrates with Enviance EHS compliance tracking.
reference: RCRA Subtitle C Hazardous Waste Regulations; DOT 49 CFR Hazardous Materials; EPA CERCLA

business_glossary_term: Institute of Scrap Recycling Industries (ISRI) Specification Reference
description: Official ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular grade designation code that this internal grade maps to (e.g., 'ISRI-11' for OCC, 'ISRI-600' for Aluminum Cans). Enables standardized communication with commodity buyers, brokers, and end-markets. Required for export commodity sales documentation.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular (current edition); EPA Recycling Markets

description: Timestamp when this commodity grade specification record was most recently modified. Used to detect stale specifications, trigger review workflows, and maintain data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX per enterprise data standards.
reference: SOX Sarbanes-Oxley Act Audit Trail Requirements; ISO 14001 Document Control

description: Multiplicative premium or discount factor applied to the base commodity market price for this grade (e.g., 1.15 = 15% premium for Premium grade; 0.85 = 15% discount for Off-Grade; 1.00 = no adjustment for Standard). Used in Oracle Revenue Management to calculate commodity sale prices and MRF revenue projections.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Pricing Rules; SWANA Commodity Market Pricing Guidelines

description: Maximum allowable bale weight in pounds for this commodity grade. Ensures compliance with DOT weight regulations for transport and safe handling limits for forklift and loading equipment. Bales exceeding this limit require splitting before outbound shipment.
reference: DOT 49 CFR Part 393 Vehicle Weight Regulations; OSHA Material Handling Standards

description: Maximum percentage of non-target or prohibited materials allowable in a bale or load for this grade to be accepted by buyers (e.g., 2.00 for Grade A OCC = max 2% contamination). Exceeding this threshold triggers grade downgrade or rejection. Used in MRF quality control acceptance/rejection decisions and contamination tracking.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; SWANA MRF Contamination Standards; EPA Recycling Program Guidelines

description: Maximum allowable moisture content percentage for this commodity grade. Excess moisture reduces commodity value, increases transport weight, and can cause material degradation. Measured at bale production or outbound load inspection. Critical for paper and fiber grades where moisture directly impacts pulp mill acceptance.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; TAPPI Moisture Testing Standards; SWANA MRF Best Practices

description: Maximum allowable individual particle or item size in inches for material classified under this grade. Oversized items can damage processing equipment at end-market facilities and may trigger load rejection. Particularly relevant for glass, metal, and plastic grades.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; SWANA MRF Equipment Specifications

business_glossary_term: Minimum Bale Density (lbs per cubic ft)
description: Minimum required bale density in pounds per cubic foot for this commodity grade. Ensures structural integrity during transport and storage, and maximizes trailer payload efficiency. Bales failing density requirements may be rejected by buyers or require re-baling. Monitored via baler equipment sensors integrated with AMCS.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; SWANA Baling Operations Standards

description: Minimum required percentage of the target commodity material in a bale or load for this grade classification (e.g., 98.00 for Premium Aluminum = minimum 98% pure aluminum content). Complements the contamination threshold by specifying the positive purity floor rather than the negative contamination ceiling.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; SWANA MRF Quality Standards

description: Minimum weight in pounds of material sample required for quality control testing under this grade specification. Ensures statistical validity of contamination and purity measurements. Applies when sort_purity_test_method is grab_sample or full_sort.
reference: ASTM D5231 Standard Test Method; SWANA MRF Quality Control Procedures

description: Free-text field for additional operational notes, buyer-specific requirements, special handling instructions, or clarifications related to this commodity grade specification. May include references to specific buyer contracts, seasonal market conditions, or equipment-specific processing notes.

Comma-delimited list of materials explicitly prohibited from inclusion in this commodity grade (e.g., 'food waste, hazardous materials, medical waste, batteries, Styrofoam, plastic bags'). Any presence of prohibited materials may result in load rejection, buyer chargebacks, or regulatory violations. Referenced during MRF sorting quality audits and inbound load inspections.
reference: ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; RCRA Hazardous Waste Regulations; EPA Recycling Contamination Guidelines

description: Default disposition action for material that fails to meet this grade specification. Landfill routes rejected material to disposal; Reprocess returns material to the MRF sorting line; Downgrade reclassifies to a lower grade; Return to Sender sends contaminated loads back to the collection route origin; WTE (Waste-to-Energy) routes to energy recovery. Drives operational workflow in AMCS.
reference: EPA Solid Waste Disposal Regulations; SWANA MRF Operations; RCRA Subtitle D

description: Indicates whether material classified under this grade requires laboratory analysis (e.g., TCLP — Toxicity Characteristic Leaching Procedure testing, moisture analysis, or purity assay) before sale or shipment. True for grades with strict purity or regulatory requirements. Triggers lab sample workflow in Enviance EHS.
reference: EPA TCLP Testing Requirements (40 CFR Part 261); RCRA; Enviance EHS

description: Quality control testing method used to verify that a bale or load meets this grade specification. Visual Sort is a manual inspector review; Grab Sample is a representative sample extraction; Full Sort is complete material separation and weighing; Lab Analysis is laboratory chemical or physical testing; Automated Sensor uses optical or NIR sorting equipment data.
reference: SWANA MRF Quality Control Procedures; ASTM D5231 Standard Test Method for Composition of Unprocessed MSW

description: Sequential version number of this commodity grade specification record. Incremented each time the grade specification is revised (e.g., contamination threshold updated, ISRI reference changed). Enables audit trail of specification changes and ensures quality control decisions reference the correct version.
reference: SAP MM Change Management; SWANA MRF Quality Management Systems; ISO 14001 Document Control

description: Unique identifier for the contamination event record. Primary key for the contamination event entity.

Estimated additional processing cost incurred due to contamination, including labor, equipment downtime, and disposal fees, measured in US dollars.

Triggers contamination workflow including surcharge assessment, customer notification, and Enviance EHS incident logging if contamination exceeds threshold.

description: Initial visual estimate of contamination level as a percentage of total load volume, assessed by the MRF receiving operator at the tipping floor. Expressed as 0.00 to 100.00. Used for contamination trending, customer education programs, and EPA recycling quality reporting. Formal contamination rate may be updated after sort audit.
reference: EPA Recycling Reporting; SWANA MRF Operations Standards; ISO 14001 Environmental Management

reference: Oracle Revenue Management; SAP SD; IFRS/GAAP; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Primary category of contamination identified in the load (e.g., food waste, hazardous materials, non-recyclable plastics, bagged recyclables, electronics, textiles, medical waste). Used for contamination root cause analysis and targeted customer outreach. description: Estimated tonnage of this load that is expected to be diverted from landfill through recycling or recovery, based on material stream type and historical contamination-adjusted yield rates. Expressed in short tons. Used for real-time diversion rate tracking and EPA recycling program reporting. Distinct from actual recovered commodity weight which is captured at bale production.
reference: EPA Recycling Reporting; SWANA Diversion Rate Methodology; ISO 14001

description: Indicates whether this inbound load has been selected for and completed a formal sort audit or waste characterization study. True = audit completed. Sort audits are used to validate contamination estimates, measure commodity composition, and support EPA recycling program reporting accuracy.

description: Free-text field for scale operator or supervisor notes regarding this inbound load. May include observations about load quality, unusual materials, driver communications, or special handling instructions. Supports operational exception management.

Classification of how the load arrived at the MRF. 'direct_haul' = collection vehicle delivering directly from route; 'transfer_station' = consolidated load from a transfer station; 'drop_off_center' = aggregated materials from a public drop-off facility; 'special_event' = community recycling event; 'third_party_hauler' = independent hauler delivering under contract.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; EPA Recycling Reporting; AMCS Platform

description: Current workflow state of the inbound load record. 'received' = weighed and logged at scale; 'accepted' = cleared for processing; 'rejected' = refused entry (contamination, wrong material, etc.); 'pending_review' = flagged for supervisor review; 'posted' = SAP MM material receipt posted; 'voided' = record cancelled.

description: Hazardous waste tracking manifest number, if applicable. Required under RCRA for loads containing any hazardous waste fraction. Null for standard recyclable loads. Links to Enviance EHS manifest tracking for regulatory compliance.

description: Net weight of the inbound material load, calculated as gross weight minus tare weight, expressed in short tons. This is the primary tonnage figure used for TPD throughput reporting, tipping fee billing, diversion rate calculations, and SAP MM material receipt posting.
reference: EPA Recycling Reporting; SWANA MRF Operations Standards; SAP MM Goods Receipt; NIST Handbook 44

description: Work shift during which this inbound load was received and processed at the MRF. Used for shift-level throughput reporting, labor allocation, and operational performance benchmarking.

description: Total number of commodity bales produced during this sort session across all material types. Used for outbound commodity inventory management in SAP MM and baler productivity tracking.
reference: SAP MM — Commodity Inventory; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Estimated percentage of the inbound material stream classified as contamination (non-recyclable materials, food waste, hazardous items) at session start. High contamination rates impact commodity quality grades and residue tonnage. Key metric for EPA recycling diversion rate reporting.
reference: EPA Recycling Reporting Requirements; SWANA MRF Operations Standards; RCRA Subtitle D

description: Measured contamination rate percentage of the recovered commodity output after sorting, as determined by quality control sampling. Distinct from inbound contamination percentage; reflects sort line effectiveness. Used for commodity buyer compliance and quality control reporting.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; EPA Recycling Reporting

description: Timestamp when this sort session record was first captured in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.
reference: SOX Compliance — Audit Trail Requirements; ISO 14001 Environmental Management Systems

description: Percentage of total input tonnage successfully diverted from landfill disposal through recovery of recyclable commodities during this session. Calculated as (recovered_tonnage / input_tonnage) * 100. Core EPA recycling reporting metric and circular economy KPI.
reference: EPA Recycling Reporting Requirements; RCRA Subtitle D; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Total minutes of non-productive downtime recorded during this sort session due to equipment failure, material jams, safety stops, or scheduled breaks. Used for Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) analysis and maintenance planning.
reference: Infor EAM — Asset Lifecycle; SWANA MRF Operations Standards; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

description: Standardized reason code categorizing the primary cause of downtime during this sort session. Used for root cause analysis, maintenance scheduling, and operational improvement initiatives. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: equipment_failure|material_jam|safety_stop|scheduled_maintenance|operator_shortage|power_outage|quality_hold|other — promote to reference product]
reference: Infor EAM — Maintenance Scheduling; SWANA MRF Operations Standards; OSHA 29 CFR 1910

description: Date and time when the sort session officially ended (completed or aborted). Null if the session is still active or paused. Used to calculate session duration and throughput rates.
reference: AMCS Platform — Sort Line Operations; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Indicates whether a mechanical or electrical equipment fault was recorded during this sort session. When True, triggers a Work Order (WO) in Infor EAM for corrective maintenance. Supports equipment reliability and Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE) tracking.
reference: Infor EAM — Asset Lifecycle; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Estimated percentage of the inbound material stream composed of glass commodities at session start. Used for commodity recovery planning and glass processing equipment configuration.
reference: EPA Recycling Reporting Requirements; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Indicates whether hazardous or prohibited materials (e.g., sharps, household hazardous waste, RCRA-regulated items) were detected in the inbound material stream during this session. When True, triggers hazardous waste handling protocols per RCRA and EPA requirements.
reference: RCRA Subtitle C — Hazardous Waste; EPA Hazardous Waste Regulations; OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200

description: Total weight in short tons of inbound material received and processed during this sort session. Core metric for Tons Per Day (TPD) throughput reporting and EPA recycling diversion rate calculations.
reference: EPA Recycling Reporting Requirements; SWANA MRF Operations Standards; RCRA Subtitle D

description: Estimated percentage of the inbound material stream composed of metal commodities (aluminum, steel, ferrous, non-ferrous) at session start. Used for commodity recovery planning and eddy current separator configuration.
reference: EPA Recycling Reporting Requirements; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Free-text field for supervisor or operator notes capturing operational observations, material quality issues, equipment anomalies, or other contextual information not captured in structured fields. Used for operational review and continuous improvement.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; AMCS Platform — Sort Line Operations

description: Number of sort station operators assigned and actively working during this sort session. Used for labor productivity analysis (tons per operator per hour) and staffing optimization.
reference: Workday HCM — Workforce Planning; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Estimated percentage of the inbound material stream composed of paper and fiber commodities (OCC, ONP, mixed paper) at session start. Used for commodity recovery planning and sort line configuration.
reference: EPA Recycling Reporting Requirements; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Estimated percentage of the inbound material stream composed of plastic commodities (PET, HDPE, mixed plastics) at session start. Used for commodity recovery planning and sort line configuration.
reference: EPA Recycling Reporting Requirements; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Number of quality control samples taken during this sort session to assess commodity purity and contamination levels. Supports statistical validity of contamination rate measurements and MRF quality assurance programs.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; ISO 14001 Environmental Management Systems

description: Overall quality grade assigned to the commodity output of this sort session based on contamination levels and commodity purity. Drives commodity pricing and market acceptance. 'off_spec' indicates material outside buyer specifications; 'rejected' indicates material refused by commodity buyer.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; ISRI Scrap Specifications Circular; EPA Recycling Reporting

description: Total weight in short tons of all recovered recyclable commodities (paper, plastics, metals, glass) produced during this sort session. Used for diversion rate calculation and commodity inventory reconciliation in SAP MM.
reference: EPA Recycling Reporting Requirements; RCRA Subtitle D; SAP MM — Commodity Inventory

description: Total weight in short tons of non-recoverable residue (contamination and non-recyclable material) sent to landfill disposal after sorting. Used to calculate the diversion rate and residue rate for EPA reporting.
reference: EPA Recycling Reporting Requirements; RCRA Subtitle D; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Indicates whether a safety incident (injury, near-miss, or OSHA recordable event) occurred during this sort session. When True, triggers linkage to an incident record in Enviance EHS. Supports OSHA 300 log reporting and safety performance tracking.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1904 — Recordkeeping; Enviance EHS — Incident Management; ISO 45001

description: Calendar date on which the sort session occurred. Used for daily Tons Per Day (TPD) aggregation, EPA recycling reporting, and operational scheduling alignment.
reference: EPA Recycling Reporting Requirements; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Total elapsed time in minutes from session start to session end, including both active processing time and downtime periods. Used for labor cost allocation and shift utilization analysis.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; Workday HCM — Labor Reporting

description: Externally-known business identifier for the sort session, used in operational reporting, shift logs, and AMCS platform references. Format: SS-YYYY-MM-DD-NNNN.
reference: AMCS Platform — Sort Line Operations; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Current lifecycle state of the sort session. 'active' indicates the session is in progress; 'completed' indicates normal closure; 'aborted' indicates premature termination due to equipment failure, safety event, or operational decision; 'paused' indicates a temporary halt; 'pending' indicates scheduled but not yet started.
reference: AMCS Platform — Sort Line Operations; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Classification of the sort session by operational purpose. 'standard' is a normal production run; 'trial' is a test run for new material streams; 'calibration' is a quality calibration run; 'training' is a supervised training session for new operators.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; ISO 14001 Environmental Management Systems

description: Classification of the work shift during which this sort session occurred. Used for labor cost allocation, productivity benchmarking across shifts, and workforce scheduling analysis.
reference: Workday HCM — Workforce Planning; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

business_glossary_term: Sort Line Belt Speed (Feet Per Minute)
description: Operational belt conveyor speed in feet per minute (FPM) at which the sort line was configured for this session. Affects throughput rate and operator sorting effectiveness. Used for equipment performance analysis.
reference: Infor EAM — Asset Lifecycle; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this sort session record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. 'AMCS' indicates AMCS Platform; 'SAP' indicates SAP S/4HANA; 'MANUAL' indicates manual data entry.
reference: Waste Management Data Governance Standards; SOX Compliance — Data Lineage

description: The native identifier of this sort session record in the originating operational system (e.g., AMCS session ID, SAP production order number). Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing in the Silver Layer.
reference: Waste Management Data Governance Standards; SOX Compliance — Data Lineage

description: Date and time when the sort session officially commenced, representing the principal real-world business event time. Used for throughput rate calculations, shift alignment, and operational KPI reporting.
reference: AMCS Platform — Sort Line Operations; SWANA MRF Operations Standards

description: Processing throughput rate measured in tons per hour (TPH) for this sort session. Calculated as input tonnage divided by active processing time. Key operational KPI for sort line efficiency and capacity planning.
reference: SWANA MRF Operations Standards; EPA Recycling Reporting Requirements

description: Timestamp when this sort session record was last modified. Used for change tracking, data reconciliation, and Silver Layer incremental processing.
reference: SOX Compliance — Audit Trail Requirements; ISO 14001 Environmental Management Systems

description: Unique identifier for the billing account. Primary key.

Human-readable billing account number displayed on invoices and customer communications. Externally visible identifier.
Current lifecycle status of the billing account indicating its operational state and billing eligibility.

Standardized code indicating the reason for account closure (e.g., customer request, non-payment, service termination).

Maximum outstanding balance allowed for the account before service suspension or payment requirement.
Internal or external credit assessment classification used for credit limit determination and collections risk management.

Current escalation level in the collections process, indicating severity of delinquency and collection actions taken.
General ledger account code in SAP FI/CO for revenue recognition and accounts receivable posting.
Most recent date when a dunning notice or collection communication was sent to the customer.

Free-form text field for special billing instructions, account-specific arrangements, or internal comments.
Date when the billing account was first established and activated for billing operations.
External account identifier in Oracle Revenue Management system used for billing and invoicing integration.
Indicates whether the customer has opted for electronic invoice delivery instead of paper statements.
Date when the customer enrolled in paperless billing.
Number of days from invoice date until payment is due, defining the standard payment window.
Default payment instrument type preferred by the customer for invoice settlement.
Profit center code for segment reporting and business unit performance tracking.
Indicates whether the account is exempt from sales tax or other applicable taxes on waste management services.
Government-issued certificate number validating the tax-exempt status of the account.
Date when the tax exemption certificate expires and requires renewal.
Unique identifier for the billing cycle configuration. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to service.area. Business justification: Billing cycles are area-specific in waste management (different municipalities and franchise areas have different billing cycle requirements). Area-based billing cycle management and municipal billing

Foreign key linking to service.line. Business justification: Billing cycles are service-line-specific (residential monthly vs. commercial weekly billing cycles). Service line drives cycle frequency and billing model. service_type on cycle is a denormalized repr

Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Billing cycles are configured per customer segment (e.g., residential customers billed monthly, large commercial quarterly). This link enables segment-level billing operations management and ensures n

Indicates whether invoices are generated in advance of the service period (prepaid model) or after service delivery (arrears model). True for advance billing (invoice before service), False for arrears billing (invoice after service).

Comma-separated list of day thresholds for AR aging bucket calculations (e.g., 30,60,90,120 for standard 30-day buckets). Used to categorize outstanding receivables by age for collections prioritization and financial reporting.
reference: GAAP Financial Reporting Standards, SAP FI/CO AR Aging

description: Indicates whether accounts in this billing cycle automatically renew at the end of each period without requiring manual intervention. True for ongoing subscription services, False for term-limited contracts requiring explicit renewal.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: Revenue model applied in this billing cycle. Subscription for fixed recurring fees (monthly container rental), transactional for per-event charges (per-haul roll-off), usage-based for metered consumption (tonnage-based tipping fees), hybrid for combination of fixed and variable charges.
Day of the month when the billing period closes and invoices are generated (1-31). For monthly cycles, this is the cutoff day. For bi-monthly or quarterly, this represents the anchor day.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: Defines how invoices are consolidated for multi-site or hierarchical accounts. Account-level generates one invoice per account, site-level generates separate invoices per service location, parent-account rolls up all child accounts, consolidated combines all services into single invoice.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: Timestamp when this billing cycle record was first created in the system. Audit trail for configuration management and SOX compliance.

Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this billing cycle. Typically USD for US operations, CAD for Canadian operations. Supports multi-currency billing for international accounts.
Short alphanumeric code identifying the billing cycle (e.g., MC01 for Monthly Cycle 01, BC15 for Bi-Monthly Cycle 15). Used as business identifier in Oracle Revenue Management.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: Detailed description of the billing cycle purpose, scope, and applicable customer segments.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: Descriptive name of the billing cycle for business user identification (e.g., Monthly Residential East, Quarterly Commercial West).
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: Current operational status of the billing cycle. Active for cycles currently in use, inactive for cycles not yet activated, suspended for temporarily disabled cycles, archived for historical cycles no longer in use but retained for audit purposes.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: Indicates whether accounts in this billing cycle are eligible for early payment discounts, volume discounts, or promotional pricing. True enables discount application rules, False excludes cycle from discount programs.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: Indicates whether automated dunning (collections reminder) process is enabled for overdue accounts in this billing cycle. True triggers escalating reminder notices and collection actions, False requires manual collections management.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: Number of days from invoice date within which payment must be received to qualify for early payment discount. Typically 10 days for 2/10 net-30 terms.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: Percentage discount offered for payment within specified early payment window (e.g., 2% discount if paid within 10 days). Common for commercial accounts to incentivize faster payment and improve cash flow.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: Date when this billing cycle configuration expires and can no longer be assigned to new accounts. Null for open-ended cycles. Used for phasing out legacy billing structures.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: Date when this billing cycle configuration becomes effective and can be assigned to customer accounts. Supports future-dated cycle changes for rate updates or term modifications.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: Recurrence pattern for invoice generation. Monthly for standard residential/commercial, bi-monthly for certain municipal contracts, quarterly for large commercial accounts, annual for prepaid contracts, per-haul for roll-off containers, on-demand for special services.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

business_glossary_term: General Ledger (GL) Accounts Receivable (AR) Account Code
description: Default GL account code for posting accounts receivable from invoices in this billing cycle. Maps to SAP FI/CO chart of accounts for balance sheet reporting.
reference: SAP FI/CO Chart of Accounts, GAAP Financial Reporting

description: Default GL account code for posting revenue from invoices in this billing cycle. Maps to SAP FI/CO chart of accounts for financial reporting and revenue recognition.
reference: SAP FI/CO Chart of Accounts, GAAP Financial Reporting

description: Number of days after payment due date before late fees are assessed or collection actions begin. Typically 5-10 days to accommodate payment processing delays.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: Primary method for delivering invoices to customers in this billing cycle. Email for electronic delivery, postal-mail for paper invoices, portal for customer self-service access, EDI for electronic data interchange with large commercial accounts, both for dual delivery.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: The date when invoices for this billing cycle are generated and made available to customers. Typically 1-3 days after billing period closes.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: Timestamp when this billing cycle record was last updated. Tracks configuration changes for audit and compliance purposes.
Fixed dollar amount charged as late fee regardless of balance, used as alternative or supplement to percentage-based late fees. Common for residential accounts ($5-$25 flat fee).
Percentage of outstanding balance charged as late fee when payment is overdue beyond grace period. Typically 1.5% to 3% per month, subject to state usury laws.
Indicates whether late fees are automatically triggered for overdue invoices in this billing cycle. True enables automatic late fee assessment, False requires manual review.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: Number of days after invoice generation date when payment is due. Typically 15, 30, or 45 days representing net-15, net-30, or net-45 payment terms.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: Standard payment terms applied to invoices in this billing cycle. Net-30 is most common for commercial accounts, net-15 for residential, net-45 or net-60 for large municipal contracts, due-on-receipt for certain transactional services, prepaid for annual contracts.

Indicates whether charges are prorated for partial billing periods when accounts are activated or terminated mid-cycle. True enables daily proration calculations, False bills full period regardless of start/end dates.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: Accounting method for recognizing revenue from invoices in this billing cycle per GAAP/IFRS standards. Point-in-time for transactional services (per-haul), over-time for subscription services (monthly collection), milestone-based for project-based contracts.

Indicates whether monthly account statements are generated in addition to individual invoices. True for accounts requiring consolidated statements showing all activity, False for invoice-only delivery.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud Data Model

description: Method for calculating sales tax or VAT on invoices in this billing cycle. Standard applies jurisdiction-based tax rates, exempt for tax-exempt customers (municipalities, non-profits), reverse-charge for certain B2B transactions, mixed for accounts with both taxable and exempt services.
reference: State and Local Tax Compliance Requirements

description: Maximum dollar amount that can be written off as bad debt without requiring executive approval. Balances below this threshold can be written off by collections team, amounts above require CFO or controller authorization per SOX controls.

Detailed narrative explanation of why the adjustment was necessary. Provides business context for audit, compliance, and customer service purposes.
Amount recovered from a previously written-off balance. Tracks bad debt recoveries for financial reporting and collections performance metrics.

Classification of the billing run execution mode. Scheduled runs are automated per billing cycle calendar; manual runs are operator-initiated; correction runs address prior errors; supplemental runs handle mid-cycle adjustments; final runs close the cycle; interim runs are preliminary.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Cloud User Guide

description: Name of the system that executed the billing run (e.g., Oracle Revenue Management, SAP SD). Used for multi-system environments and data lineage.
Date and time when the billing run execution began. Used for performance monitoring and SLA tracking.
Count of distinct customer accounts included in this billing run. Used for reconciliation and completeness validation.
Sum of all billing adjustments (credits, discounts, dispute resolutions) applied in this run, in USD. Negative values represent credits to customers.
Sum of all invoice amounts generated by this billing run, in USD. Represents gross revenue recognized for the billing period before payments and adjustments.
Count of invoices created by this billing run. May differ from accounts processed if some accounts have zero balance or are on hold.
Sum of all recurring service charges (container rentals, subscription fees) included in this run, in USD. Excludes usage-based charges and taxes.
Sum of all tax charges included in the invoices generated by this run, in USD. Includes sales tax, environmental fees, and regulatory surcharges.
Sum of all usage-based charges (tonnage, pickups, tipping fees) included in this run, in USD. Excludes recurring service charges and taxes.
Count of customer accounts processed that resulted in zero invoice amount due to no usage or offsetting credits.
Unique identifier for the accounts receivable account. Primary key for the AR sub-ledger account representing the financial relationship with a customer.

Foreign key linking to billing.billing_account. Business justification: AR account is the sub-ledger representation of a billing account's receivables. While ar_account.customer_account_id provides customer hierarchy linkage, a direct FK to billing_account is essential fo
Reference to the active payment plan agreement if the customer is on a payment plan. Null if no payment plan exists.
Date when the AR account was closed or terminated. Null for active accounts. Used for churn analysis and account lifecycle tracking.
Date when the AR account was first established for the customer. Used to calculate customer tenure and lifetime value.

Current lifecycle status of the AR account. Active accounts are in good standing; suspended accounts have service holds; closed accounts are no longer active; write-off accounts have been deemed uncollectible; bankruptcy accounts are in legal proceedings.
Outstanding balance for invoices aged 0 to 30 days past due date. Current receivables within normal payment terms. Expressed in USD.
Outstanding balance for invoices aged 31 to 60 days past due date. Moderately overdue receivables requiring follow-up. Expressed in USD.
Outstanding balance for invoices aged 61 to 90 days past due date. Significantly overdue receivables requiring escalated collection efforts. Expressed in USD.

Outstanding balance for invoices aged more than 90 days past due date. Severely overdue receivables at high risk of uncollectibility, may require legal action or write-off consideration. Expressed in USD.

Business identifier for the AR account, typically the customer account number used in billing and collections. May align with SAP FI AR account number or Oracle Revenue Management customer account number.
reference: SAP FI-AR Account Master, Oracle Revenue Management

description: Indicates whether the customer is enrolled in automatic payment processing. True means payments are automatically processed on due date; false means manual payment required.
Remaining credit available to the customer, calculated as credit limit minus current balance. Expressed in USD.
Amount reserved for potential uncollectible receivables based on aging analysis and credit risk assessment. Used for allowance for doubtful accounts calculation. Expressed in USD.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for invoicing and payments. Typically USD for US operations but may vary for international customers.

Frequency at which the customer is billed for services. Monthly is standard residential; quarterly/annual are common for commercial; on-demand is for transactional services; custom is negotiated frequency.

Current stage in the collections process. Current indicates no collection activity; reminder/notices indicate escalating communication; collections-agency indicates third-party involvement; legal-action indicates court proceedings; payment-plan indicates negotiated repayment; settled indicates resolved. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: current|reminder_sent|first_notice|second_notice|final_notice|collections_agency|legal_action|payment_plan|settled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Timestamp when this AR account record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.
Indicates whether the account is on credit hold. True means services are suspended due to credit issues; false means account is in good standing.

Reason code for why the account is on credit hold. Over-limit indicates balance exceeds credit limit; past-due indicates overdue invoices; bankruptcy indicates legal proceedings; fraud indicates suspected fraudulent activity; dispute indicates billing dispute; manual-hold indicates administrative hold; none indicates no hold. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: over_limit|past_due|bankruptcy|fraud|dispute|manual_hold|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Maximum amount of credit extended to the customer. Services may be suspended if current balance exceeds this limit. Expressed in USD.

Current credit standing of the account. Approved accounts can receive services; on-hold accounts have credit restrictions; under-review accounts are being evaluated; restricted accounts have limited credit; revoked accounts have no credit privileges.
Total outstanding balance owed by the customer across all invoices. Represents the sum of all unpaid charges, fees, and adjustments. Expressed in USD.
Average number of days it takes the customer to pay invoices. Key metric for assessing payment behavior and credit risk. Calculated as (current balance / average daily sales).
Indicates whether there are active billing disputes on the account. True means one or more invoices are disputed; false means no disputes.
Total amount of charges currently under dispute by the customer. Expressed in USD.

Current collection escalation level for overdue accounts. 0 indicates no dunning; 1-3 indicate progressive collection notice levels; higher numbers indicate more aggressive collection actions including legal proceedings.
Amount of the most recent invoice issued to the customer. Expressed in USD.
