description: Date of the most recent invoice issued to the customer. Used to track billing activity and calculate days since last invoice.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management, SAP SD Billing

description: Amount of the most recent payment received from the customer. Expressed in USD.
Date of the most recent payment received from the customer. Used to track payment history and calculate days since last payment.
Timestamp when this AR account record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Indicates whether the customer has opted for electronic invoicing instead of paper statements. True means electronic delivery only; false means paper statements are sent.

Primary payment method used by the customer. ACH is Automated Clearing House electronic transfer; credit-card is card payment; check is paper check; wire-transfer is bank wire; cash is physical currency; auto-pay is automatic recurring payment; other is alternative methods. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ach|credit_card|check|wire_transfer|cash|auto_pay|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Indicates whether the customer is on a negotiated payment plan for overdue balances. True means payment plan is active; false means no payment plan.

Standard payment terms for this AR account. Net-15/30/45/60 indicates number of days from invoice date; due-on-receipt requires immediate payment; prepaid requires payment before service; custom indicates negotiated terms. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: net_15|net_30|net_45|net_60|due_on_receipt|prepaid|custom — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

description: The SAP FI customer account number in the accounts receivable sub-ledger. Used for integration and reconciliation with the SAP S/4HANA FI module.

Indicates whether the customer is exempt from sales tax on waste management services. True means tax-exempt status verified; false means standard tax applies.

description: Total amount of billing adjustments (credits, write-offs, discounts) applied to the customer account in the current fiscal year. Expressed in USD.
Total amount invoiced to the customer in the current fiscal year. Used for revenue analysis and customer value assessment. Expressed in USD.
Total amount of payments received from the customer in the current fiscal year. Used for cash flow analysis and payment behavior assessment. Expressed in USD.
Total amount of receivables written off as uncollectible in the current fiscal year. Used for bad debt expense tracking and financial reporting. Expressed in USD.
Unique identifier for the payment transaction. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to billing.ar_account. Business justification: Payments directly affect AR account balances and aging buckets. While payment.customer_account_id provides customer context and payment.invoice_id links to specific invoices, a direct FK to ar_account
Identifier of the customer who made the payment. Links to the customer master record.
Identifier of the primary invoice this payment is applied against. Null for prepayments or unapplied payments.

Total amount of the payment received from the customer, in the payment currency.
Portion of the payment amount that has been applied to one or more invoices.
Identifier of the accounts receivable batch in which this payment was processed, used for AR reconciliation and audit.
Bank account number from which the payment was drawn (for ACH, EFT, wire transfers). Masked or tokenized for security.
Identifier of the bank deposit batch in which this payment was deposited, used for cash management and reconciliation.
Bank routing number (ABA number) for ACH, EFT, or wire transfer payments.

The interface or channel through which the payment was submitted (customer portal, mail, phone, in-person, bank transfer, auto-pay, third-party processor). The date the payment cleared the bank and funds became available.
Timestamp when the payment record was first created in the system.
Last four digits of the credit or debit card used for payment, for reference and reconciliation purposes.
Type of credit or debit card used for payment (Visa, MasterCard, American Express, Discover, other).
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the payment amount (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).
The date the payment was deposited into the company bank account.
The accounting date on which the payment was posted to the general ledger for financial reporting purposes.
The accounting period (e.g., 2024-01, Q1-2024) in which the payment was posted to the general ledger.
Identifier of the lockbox batch for payments received via bank lockbox service, used for automated payment processing and reconciliation.
User ID or system identifier of the person or process that last modified the payment record.
Timestamp when the payment record was last modified.
Free-text notes or comments related to the payment transaction, including customer instructions, special handling, or internal annotations.
Fee charged to the customer for a returned payment due to non-sufficient funds.
The date the payment was received or recorded in the system.
Business-facing unique reference number for the payment transaction, used for customer communication and reconciliation.

Classification of the payment transaction: standard payment against invoice, prepayment, advance deposit, retainer, refund, or credit memo application.

Name of the third-party payment processor or gateway used to process the payment (e.g., Stripe, PayPal, Authorize.Net).

Unique identifier for the payment application record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to billing.ar_account. Business justification: A payment application reduces the balance on a specific AR account. While payment already links to ar_account, the payment_application record (which captures the exact allocation of funds to invoices)
Reference to the customer account for which this payment application is processed. Denormalized for reporting performance and customer AR analysis.
Reference to the invoice receiving payment application. Links to the invoice record in the billing system.
Reference to the payment transaction being applied to invoices. Links to the payment record in the payment processing system.
The fiscal period identifier (e.g., 2024-Q1, 2024-01) to which this payment application is assigned for financial reporting and period close processes.
The amount of billing adjustment applied concurrently with this payment application. Used for dispute resolutions, service credits, and billing corrections.
The date when the payment was applied to the invoice. This is the business event date used for accounting period assignment and Accounts Receivable (AR) aging calculations.
Business identifier for the payment application transaction. Used for tracking and reference in customer service and accounting operations.

The system or process that originated this payment application. Manual applications are entered by AR staff; automatic applications are system-matched; lockbox applications come from bank lockbox processing; customer portal applications are self-service; batch imports are from external systems; API applications are from integrated systems.

Current lifecycle status of the payment application. Pending applications are awaiting approval; applied applications are finalized; reversed applications have been undone; voided applications are cancelled; adjusted applications have been modified post-application.
Precise date and time when the payment application was processed in the system. Used for audit trail and transaction sequencing.

Classification of how the payment is being applied. Standard applications reduce open AR balance; prepayments are applied to future invoices; credit memo offsets apply credits; dispute settlements resolve billing disputes; overpayment allocations handle excess payments; refund reversals correct previously issued refunds.
The monetary amount from the payment that was applied to this specific invoice. Supports partial payment scenarios where a single payment is split across multiple invoices.
Date and time when this payment application was approved. Null for auto-approved applications. Used for workflow tracking and compliance reporting.
Free-text notes or comments about this payment application. Used to document special circumstances, customer requests, dispute resolutions, or manual intervention reasons.
Date and time when this payment application record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the applied amount. Supports multi-currency billing scenarios for international commercial accounts.
The amount of early payment discount or settlement discount applied with this payment application. Common in commercial accounts with negotiated payment terms.
The date by which payment must be received to qualify for early payment discount. Used to validate discount eligibility at application time.
The currency exchange rate used to convert payment amount to invoice currency, if cross-currency application. Null for same-currency applications.
The date for which the exchange rate was determined. Used for foreign exchange gain/loss calculations and audit trail.

The type of exchange rate used for cross-currency applications. Spot rates are market rates at transaction time; corporate rates are company-defined standard rates; user-defined rates are manually entered.
The accounting period date when this payment application is posted to the General Ledger (GL). May differ from application_date due to period close rules and accounting calendar constraints.
The outstanding balance of the invoice immediately after this payment application was processed. Used for audit trail and to determine if invoice is fully paid.
The outstanding balance of the invoice immediately before this payment application was processed. Used for audit trail and reconciliation.
Indicates whether this payment application involves currency conversion (payment currency differs from invoice currency). Used for foreign exchange gain/loss tracking.

Indicates whether this payment application represents an on-account credit (payment received without a specific invoice to apply to). True for prepayments and overpayments held for future application.
Date and time when this payment application record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
The automated matching rule that was used to apply this payment to the invoice (e.g., exact amount match, invoice number match, customer account match). Used for automatic cash application processes.
The initial applied amount before any subsequent adjustments or reversals. Used to track the history of changes to the payment application.
The payment instrument used for this payment (e.g., check, ACH, credit card, wire transfer, cash). Denormalized from payment record for application-level reporting.
External reference number from the payment transaction (e.g., check number, ACH trace number, credit card authorization code). Denormalized for reconciliation and customer service.
The portion of the original payment that remains unapplied after this application. Used for tracking partial payment scenarios and multi-invoice applications.

description: The date when this payment application was reversed, if applicable. Null for active applications. Used to track corrections and adjustments in AR management.
Standardized code indicating why the payment application was reversed. Examples include payment error, incorrect invoice, customer dispute, NSF (Non-Sufficient Funds), or administrative correction.
Detailed explanation of why the payment application was reversed. Provides context for audit and customer service inquiries.
The amount of invoice balance written off as uncollectible at the time of this payment application. Used in partial payment scenarios where remaining balance is deemed uncollectible.
Unique identifier for the payment plan arrangement. Primary key.
Reference to the customer account enrolled in this payment plan.

Foreign key linking to contract.billing_term. Business justification: Payment plans are negotiated deviations from standard contract billing terms. Referencing the original billing_term being modified supports collections management, dunning configuration, and contract

Foreign key linking to contract.contract. Business justification: Payment plans are negotiated as part of contract terms. Collections and credit teams need to trace payment arrangements back to originating agreement for covenant compliance, early termination penalty
Date the payment plan was approved by management. Null if no approval was required.
Indicates whether this payment plan required management approval before activation. Typically true for large balances, extended terms, or settlement plans.
Indicates whether automatic payment processing is enabled for this plan. When true, installments are automatically charged to the customers payment method on file on each due date.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management - Auto Pay Configuration

description: Court-assigned bankruptcy case number if this payment plan is part of a bankruptcy arrangement. Null for non-bankruptcy plans.

Type of bankruptcy filing if this payment plan is part of a bankruptcy arrangement. Chapter 7 is liquidation; Chapter 11 is business reorganization; Chapter 13 is individual debt adjustment. Null for non-bankruptcy plans.
Date and time when this payment plan record was first created in the system.
Number of missed installments allowed before the plan is automatically moved to defaulted status. Typically 2 for standard plans, may be higher for hardship programs.
Initial payment required at plan setup before installment schedule begins. May be zero for certain hardship programs.
Date the down payment was received and processed. Null if no down payment was required or if down payment is still outstanding.
The portion of the original balance that was enrolled into this payment plan. May be less than original balance if customer made partial payment or if certain charges were excluded from the plan.
Scheduled due date for the last installment payment. Represents the planned completion date of the payment plan.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management - Final Payment Due Date

description: Scheduled due date for the first installment payment. Subsequent installments are calculated based on this date and the installment frequency.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management - First Payment Due Date

description: Number of days after installment due date before payment is considered missed. Standard grace period is typically 10 days.
Standard amount due for each installment payment. Calculated as (enrolled_balance_amount - down_payment_amount) / total_installment_count. Final installment may differ to account for rounding.
Cadence at which installment payments are scheduled. Monthly is most common; weekly and biweekly are used for residential hardship programs; quarterly may be used for large commercial accounts.
Total interest charges accrued on this payment plan to date. Zero for interest-free plans.

Annual interest rate applied to the enrolled balance, if any. Most residential hardship plans have zero interest; commercial workout agreements may include interest. Expressed as percentage (e.g., 5.00 for 5%).
Standard late fee charged for each missed installment payment. May be waived for hardship programs.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management - Late Fee Configuration

description: Number of scheduled installments that were not paid by their due date. Used to determine default status and trigger collections escalation.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management - Missed Payment Count

description: Date and time when this payment plan record was last modified. Updated whenever plan status, payment amounts, or other attributes change.
Free-text notes capturing special terms, customer circumstances, negotiation details, or other relevant information about this payment plan.
Total outstanding accounts receivable (AR) balance at the time the payment plan was established. Represents the full amount owed before any plan payments were made.

Actual date the payment plan was completed, defaulted, or cancelled. Null for active or suspended plans. For completed plans, this is the date the final payment was received; for defaulted or cancelled plans, this is the date the plan was terminated.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management - Arrangement End Date

description: Externally visible unique business identifier for the payment plan, formatted as PP-NNNNNNNN.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management - Payment Arrangement Number

description: Effective date when the payment plan became active and the installment schedule began. Typically the date the plan was approved and down payment (if required) was received.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management - Arrangement Start Date

description: Current lifecycle state of the payment plan. Draft indicates plan is being set up but not yet active; active means plan is in effect and installments are being processed; completed indicates all installments paid successfully; defaulted means customer missed installments per default criteria; cancelled indicates plan was terminated before completion; suspended means plan is temporarily on hold pending resolution of dispute or other issue.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management - Payment Arrangement Status

description: Classification of the payment plan based on the business program and customer segment. Residential hardship programs support customers facing temporary financial difficulty; commercial workout agreements are negotiated arrangements for business accounts; standard installment plans are routine payment schedules; seasonal deferral plans accommodate seasonal business fluctuations; bankruptcy arrangements comply with court-ordered payment terms; settlement plans represent negotiated reduced-balance agreements.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management - Payment Arrangement Type

description: Current outstanding balance remaining on the payment plan after all payments received to date. Calculated as enrolled_balance_amount minus sum of all installment payments received.
Dollar amount of discount applied to the original balance as part of a settlement agreement. Zero for standard payment plans.
Percentage discount applied to the original balance as part of a settlement agreement. Zero for standard payment plans. Expressed as percentage (e.g., 20.00 for 20% discount).
Total number of scheduled installment payments in this plan. Typically ranges from 3 to 24 installments depending on plan type and balance amount.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management - Number of Installments

description: Cumulative sum of all late fees assessed on this payment plan due to missed installments.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management - Late Fee Total

description: Cumulative sum of all payments received against this payment plan, including down payment and all installment payments to date.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management - Total Payments Received

description: The legal mechanism by which consent was obtained. 'express_written' is required for TCPA-regulated SMS and autodialed calls. 'double_opt_in' provides the strongest evidence of consent for email marketing. Drives compliance reporting and legal defensibility.
reference: TCPA (47 U.S.C. § 227); CAN-SPAM Act; FCC TCPA Regulations

description: The system or touchpoint through which consent was captured. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_portal|salesforce_crm|paper_form|ivr|mobile_app|service_call|email_signup|contract_signature|third_party — promote to reference product]
reference: TCPA; CAN-SPAM Act; CCPA; Salesforce CRM; Waste Logics Customer Portal

description: Current regulatory consent status for the specified channel and notification type. 'granted' indicates valid consent is on file; 'revoked' indicates consent was withdrawn; 'pending' indicates awaiting confirmation (double opt-in); 'expired' indicates consent has lapsed per retention policy; 'not_required' applies to transactional communications exempt from consent requirements.
reference: TCPA; CAN-SPAM Act; CCPA Section 1798.120; GDPR Article 7

description: The exact date and time when the customer or contact granted, revoked, or updated consent for this channel and notification type. Critical audit field for TCPA and CAN-SPAM compliance. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.
reference: TCPA; CAN-SPAM Act; CCPA; GDPR Article 7(1)

description: The customer or contact's preferred frequency for non-transactional outreach (e.g., promotional offers, NPS surveys, service updates). Transactional and compliance-driven communications (e.g., billing alerts, regulatory notices) are not subject to this frequency cap.
reference: CAN-SPAM Act; Salesforce CRM Marketing Cloud Frequency Management

description: The date and time when this communication preference record was first created in the system. Immutable audit field used for compliance reporting, data lineage, and preference history analysis.
reference: SOX Compliance; TCPA Audit Requirements; ISO/IEC 27001

description: Specific suppression flag for telephone-based outreach (voice calls). Distinct from the master do_not_contact flag to allow granular channel-level suppression. Compliance with the National Do Not Call Registry and TCPA requirements.
reference: TCPA; National Do Not Call Registry (FTC); 47 CFR Part 64

description: Master suppression flag indicating the customer or contact must not be contacted through any channel. Overrides all individual channel and notification preferences. Must be honored across all outbound communication systems including Salesforce CRM, Oracle Revenue Management, and Waste Logics portal.
reference: TCPA; CAN-SPAM Act; CCPA Section 1798.120; National Do Not Call Registry

description: Specific suppression flag for email-based outreach. Distinct from the master do_not_contact flag to allow granular channel-level suppression. Compliance with CAN-SPAM Act unsubscribe requirements.
Specific suppression flag for physical postal mail outreach. Allows customers to opt out of paper-based communications such as billing statements, promotional mailers, and service notices.
reference: USPS Direct Mail Regulations; CAN-SPAM Act

description: Specific suppression flag for SMS/text message outreach. Required for TCPA compliance as SMS requires prior express written consent. Distinct from the master do_not_contact flag.

The date from which this communication preference record is effective and should be applied to outbound communications. Supports temporal preference management and scheduled preference changes (e.g., a customer requesting preferences to take effect at the start of next billing cycle).
reference: Salesforce CRM; Waste Logics Customer Portal; TCPA Compliance

description: The date until which this communication preference record is effective. Null indicates the preference is open-ended with no planned expiry. Used for temporary preference overrides (e.g., vacation holds, seasonal suppression) and consent expiry management.
reference: Salesforce CRM; Waste Logics Customer Portal; TCPA Compliance

description: Indicates whether this communication preference record is currently active and in effect. Set to False when a preference is superseded by a newer record or when the contact is deactivated, enabling soft-delete and historical audit without data loss.
reference: Salesforce CRM; Waste Logics Customer Portal

description: The customer or contact's preferred language for all communications, stored as an IETF BCP 47 language tag (e.g., 'en', 'es', 'zh-CN', 'fr'). Drives language selection in templated outbound communications across email, SMS, IVR, and portal notifications.
reference: IETF BCP 47 Language Tags; ISO 639-1 Language Codes

description: The identifier of the user, system, or agent that last modified this preference record. May be a Salesforce CRM user ID, a Waste Logics portal session identifier, or a system process name (e.g., 'BULK_IMPORT', 'IVR_SYSTEM'). Supports audit trail and compliance investigation.
reference: SOX Compliance; TCPA Audit Requirements; Salesforce CRM Audit Trail

description: The category of notification for which this preference record captures opt-in or opt-out status. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: billing_alert|service_reminder|outage_notification|promotional_offer|nps_survey|collection_schedule|account_update|regulatory_notice|payment_confirmation|route_change — promote to reference product]
reference: CAN-SPAM Act; TCPA; Oracle Revenue Management; Salesforce CRM

business_glossary_term: Net Promoter Score (NPS) Survey Opt-In Flag
description: Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive Net Promoter Score (NPS) and customer satisfaction surveys. Supports the customer experience measurement program and drives service improvement analytics.
reference: Salesforce CRM; Net Promoter Score (NPS) Methodology; CAN-SPAM Act

description: Current opt-in or opt-out status of the customer or contact for the specified channel and notification type combination. 'pending' indicates consent has been requested but not yet confirmed (e.g., double opt-in flow). 'not_set' indicates no preference has been recorded.
reference: TCPA; CAN-SPAM Act; GDPR Article 7; CCPA Section 1798.120

description: The channel or mechanism through which the customer submitted their opt-out request. Supports compliance audit trails and process improvement analysis (e.g., identifying which unsubscribe mechanisms are most used). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email_unsubscribe|sms_stop|ivr|customer_portal|agent_request|written_request|regulatory_order — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]

The reason provided by the customer or contact for opting out of a communication channel or notification type. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: too_frequent|not_relevant|prefer_other_channel|unsubscribe_link|customer_request|complaint|regulatory_request|deceased — promote to reference product]
reference: CAN-SPAM Act; TCPA; Salesforce CRM Case Management

description: The exact date and time when the customer or contact submitted an opt-out request for this channel or notification type. CAN-SPAM requires opt-out requests to be honored within 10 business days; this timestamp is the compliance clock start.

Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive service disruption and outage notifications such as missed collection alerts, route delays, weather-related service suspensions, and facility closures.
reference: AMCS Platform; Locus Dispatch; Salesforce CRM Service Cloud

description: Indicates whether the customer has enabled in-portal notifications within the Waste Logics customer portal. When True, notifications are surfaced within the portal dashboard in addition to or instead of external channels.
reference: Waste Logics Customer Portal; Salesforce CRM

description: Categorizes the nature of this preference record: channel (preferred delivery channel), notification (opt-in/out for a notification category), language (preferred language), frequency (preferred contact cadence), or do_not_contact (suppression flag). Enables structured querying of preference records.
reference: TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act); CAN-SPAM Act; Salesforce CRM Marketing Cloud

description: The end of the customer's preferred time window for receiving phone or SMS outreach, stored in HH:MM 24-hour format in the customer's local time zone. TCPA restricts calls to 8 AM–9 PM local time; this field captures the customer's narrower preference within that window.

The start of the customer's preferred time window for receiving phone or SMS outreach, stored in HH:MM 24-hour format in the customer's local time zone. Used by outbound dialer and SMS scheduling systems to respect contact time preferences.

Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive promotional communications including new service offers, recycling program promotions, and sustainability initiative campaigns. Subject to CAN-SPAM and TCPA consent requirements.
reference: CAN-SPAM Act; TCPA; Salesforce CRM Marketing Cloud

description: Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive regulatory and compliance-related notices such as EPA-mandated service change notifications, RCRA hazardous waste handling updates, and local solid waste authority announcements. Note: some regulatory notices may be mandatory regardless of opt-in status.
reference: EPA Regulations; RCRA; Clean Air Act; Clean Water Act; Local Solid Waste Authorities

description: Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive service reminders such as upcoming collection day notifications, container set-out reminders, and scheduled service confirmations. Supports AMCS Platform and Locus Dispatch customer notification workflows.
reference: AMCS Platform; Locus Dispatch; Waste Logics Customer Portal

description: The originating system from which this preference record was captured or last updated. Enables data lineage tracking and conflict resolution when preferences are updated across multiple systems of record (Salesforce CRM, Waste Logics portal, Oracle Revenue Management). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: salesforce_crm|waste_logics|oracle_revenue_mgmt|ivr|mobile_app|paper_form|bulk_import|agent_entry — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: Salesforce CRM; Waste Logics Customer Portal; Oracle Revenue Management; Data Governance Policy

description: Indicates whether this contact has been added to a global suppression list, preventing all marketing and non-transactional outreach regardless of individual channel preferences. Populated from CAN-SPAM unsubscribe lists, TCPA litigation holds, or internal suppression decisions.
reference: CAN-SPAM Act; TCPA; Salesforce CRM Marketing Cloud Suppression Lists

description: The reason this contact was added to the suppression list. 'bounce' indicates a hard email bounce; 'complaint' indicates a spam complaint; 'litigation_hold' indicates legal proceedings; 'regulatory' indicates a government-mandated suppression order. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: unsubscribe|complaint|bounce|litigation_hold|regulatory|deceased|fraud — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: CAN-SPAM Act; TCPA; FTC Guidelines; Internal Legal Hold Policy

description: The IANA time zone identifier for the customer's or contact's local time zone (e.g., 'America/Chicago', 'America/Los_Angeles'). Used in conjunction with preferred_contact_time_start and preferred_contact_time_end to schedule compliant outreach.
reference: IANA Time Zone Database; TCPA Compliance

description: The date and time when this communication preference record was most recently modified. Updated on every change to any preference field. Used for change detection, synchronization with downstream systems, and compliance audit trails.
reference: SOX Compliance; TCPA Audit Requirements; Salesforce CRM; Waste Logics Customer Portal

description: Unique surrogate identifier for each contact record in the Waste Management customer domain. Primary key for the contact data product.
Reference to the parent customer account this contact is associated with. Links the contact to the customer master record in the customer domain.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object — AccountId field

description: Foreign key linking to customer.customer_segment. Business justification: Normalize denormalized account_segment STRING field (semantic equivalent to customer_segment) to FK reference. Contacts should derive segment classification from their associated customer account via

Current lifecycle status of the contact record. Active contacts receive communications and can authorize service changes. Inactive contacts are retained for historical reference. Do_not_contact enforces opt-out compliance. Deceased and unverified statuses support data quality management.
reference: GDPR Article 17 (Right to Erasure); CCPA Section 1798.105; CAN-SPAM Act

description: Date and time when the contact record was first created in the Waste Management data platform (Silver layer). Represents the record audit creation timestamp for data lineage and compliance purposes. Formatted as yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
reference: ISO/IEC 11179; SOX Section 404 Audit Trail Requirements; GDPR Article 30 (Records of Processing)

description: Organizational department or business unit the contact belongs to within their company (e.g., Facilities, Finance, Operations, Environmental Health & Safety). Supports routing of service and billing inquiries to the correct team.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object — Department field

description: Indicates whether the contact is registered on the National Do Not Call Registry or has explicitly requested no outbound telemarketing calls. True = do not call for marketing purposes. Enforced by the Oracle Revenue Management and Salesforce CRM outbound dialing workflows.
reference: FTC National Do Not Call Registry; TCPA; CCPA Section 1798.120

description: Indicates whether the contact has consented to receive marketing and promotional email communications from Waste Management. True = opted in; False = opted out or never consented. Distinct from transactional/service emails which are sent regardless of this flag.
reference: CAN-SPAM Act; GDPR Article 7; CCPA Section 1798.120

description: Fax number for the contact. Used for transmission of formal documents such as service agreements, hazardous waste manifests, and regulatory compliance correspondence where fax remains a legally accepted delivery method.
reference: GDPR Article 4; DOT Hazardous Materials Regulations; RCRA Manifest Requirements

description: Given (first) name of the individual contact person. Used for personalized communications, billing correspondence, and service notifications.

Indicates whether this contact is designated to receive billing-related communications including invoices, payment reminders, Accounts Receivable (AR) notices, and rate change notifications. Supports Oracle Revenue Management billing workflow routing.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management — Contact Roles; GAAP/IFRS Billing Standards

description: Indicates whether this contact should be notified in emergency situations such as hazardous waste spills, service safety incidents, or after-hours urgent service needs. Emergency contacts are prioritized in incident escalation workflows managed through Enviance EHS.
reference: OSHA 29 CFR 1910.38 Emergency Action Plans; RCRA Emergency Response Requirements; Enviance EHS

description: Indicates whether this contact is designated as the primary point of contact for the customer account. Each customer account should have exactly one primary contact. Used to determine the default recipient for account-level communications when no specific role routing applies.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Role — Primary flag; Waste Logics Contact Management

description: Indicates whether this contact is designated to receive service-related communications including collection schedule notifications, service disruption alerts, Work Order (WO) confirmations, and container delivery/pickup notices.

description: Professional title or position of the contact within their organization (e.g., Facilities Manager, Environmental Compliance Officer, Accounts Payable Specialist). Particularly relevant for commercial and municipal accounts.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object — Title field

description: Family (last) name of the individual contact person. Combined with first_name to form the full display name for correspondence and account management.

First line of the contact's mailing address for physical correspondence including paper billing statements, regulatory notices, and formal contract documents. May differ from the service address on the customer account.

description: Second line of the contact's mailing address (suite, apartment, floor, unit number, or care-of designation). Supplements mailing_address_line1 for complete address formatting.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA Section 1798.140; USPS Addressing Standards

description: City component of the contact's mailing address. Used for physical mail delivery and geographic segmentation of contact communications.

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the contact's mailing address (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX). Supports international commercial and industrial accounts and determines applicable privacy regulation framework.

description: ZIP or ZIP+4 postal code for the contact's mailing address. Used for mail delivery, geographic market analysis, and routing of state-specific regulatory communications.

description: Two-letter US state or territory abbreviation for the contact's mailing address (e.g., TX, CA, FL). Follows USPS state code standards. Used for state-level regulatory compliance routing and tax jurisdiction determination.
reference: USPS State Abbreviations; State Environmental Quality Departments; CCPA (California)

description: Date and time of the contact's most recent login to the Waste Logics customer portal. Used to identify inactive portal users, drive re-engagement campaigns, and support portal access audits. Null if the contact has never logged in.
reference: Waste Logics Customer Portal — Session Audit Log; SOX IT General Controls

description: Contact's stated preference for how Waste Management should reach them. Drives communication channel selection for service notifications, billing statements, and marketing communications. reference: GDPR Article 7 (Consent); CCPA Section 1798.120; CAN-SPAM Act; TCPA

description: Contact's preferred time window for receiving outbound calls or communications from Waste Management. Supports compliance with TCPA restrictions and improves customer satisfaction by respecting contact preferences.
reference: TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act); CCPA Section 1798.120

description: ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) representing the contact's preferred language for communications (e.g., en, es, fr, zh-CN). Drives localization of billing statements, service notifications, and customer portal content.

description: Main telephone number for reaching the contact. Formatted per E.164 international standard. Used for service scheduling confirmations, billing inquiries, and emergency notifications. Sourced from Salesforce CRM Contact Phone field.

reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Role; Waste Logics Contact Management

description: External system identifier for this contact record in Salesforce CRM. Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing between the Silver layer and the Salesforce Contact object.

description: Indicates whether the contact has provided express written consent to receive SMS/text message communications from Waste Management, including service reminders, route change alerts, and promotional messages. Required for TCPA compliance.

description: Originating operational system from which this contact record was sourced. Supports data lineage tracking, conflict resolution during master data management, and audit traceability. description: Honorific or salutation prefix for the contact (e.g., Mr., Ms., Dr.). Used in formal written communications and billing correspondence.

reference: ISO/IEC 11179; SOX Section 404 Audit Trail Requirements; GDPR Article 30

description: Date on which the contact's identity and contact information was most recently verified. Used to enforce re-verification schedules and identify stale contact records requiring data quality remediation.

Identity and contact information verification status for this contact record. Verified = contact details confirmed via outbound validation or portal registration. Unverified = data entered but not yet confirmed. Pending = verification in progress. Failed = verification attempt unsuccessful. Supports data quality management and fraud prevention.
reference: GDPR Article 5 (Data Accuracy); CCPA Section 1798.100; SOX Internal Controls

description: Identifier for the corresponding user account in the Waste Logics customer portal. Populated when the contact has registered for self-service portal access. Null if no portal account exists.

description: Telephone extension number associated with the work phone for the contact. Required for large commercial and municipal accounts where contacts are reached via a main switchboard.
reference: Salesforce CRM Contact Object — Phone Extension

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the customer account record in the Waste Management data platform. Primary key for the customer domain master entity.

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulated_facility. Business justification: Large industrial/commercial customers are often themselves regulated facility operators (transfer stations, treatment facilities). WM account managers need to link the customer account to its EPA/stat

Legal or trade name of the customer account. For residential accounts this is the householder full name; for commercial and municipal accounts this is the registered business or entity name. Used for billing, contracts, and correspondence.
reference: GDPR Article 4; CCPA §1798.140; Salesforce CRM Account Name Field

description: Externally-known, human-readable account number assigned to the customer by Waste Management. Used on invoices, service agreements, and customer communications. Sourced from Oracle Revenue Management and Salesforce CRM.

description: Current lifecycle state of the customer account. Prospect = pre-service onboarding; Active = services being rendered; Suspended = temporarily halted (e.g., non-payment, seasonal hold); Inactive = no current services but record retained; Closed = account permanently terminated and archived.

Drives service priority, dedicated account management assignment, and pricing concessions. Strategic tier typically reserved for large municipal contracts and national commercial accounts.

description: High-level classification of the customer account indicating the nature of the customer relationship. Drives service offerings, pricing tiers, regulatory requirements, and reporting segmentation. Residential = B2C households; Commercial = B2B businesses; Industrial = large-volume generators; Municipal/Government = public sector contracts.
reference: NWRA Industry Classification; SWANA Member Segmentation Guidelines

description: Indicates whether the customer account is enrolled in automatic payment processing. When True, invoices are automatically charged to the customer's payment instrument on the due date via Oracle Revenue Management.

description: Primary street address line for invoice and billing correspondence delivery. Populated only when billing address differs from service address. Used by Oracle Revenue Management for invoice generation and AR collections.

description: Current credit standing of the customer account as assessed by Waste Management's accounts receivable (AR) and collections team. Good = no outstanding issues; Watch = elevated risk monitoring; Hold = service may be suspended pending payment; Collections = account referred to collections; Write_off = balance deemed uncollectable. description: North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) or Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) code identifying the primary industry of the commercial or industrial customer. description: Timestamp of the most recent update to the customer account record in the data platform. Used for change data capture (CDC), data synchronization with source systems, and audit trail compliance.

description: Indicates whether the customer has consented to receive marketing and promotional communications from Waste Management, including service upgrade offers, sustainability program invitations, and recycling education materials. Managed per CAN-SPAM and CCPA consent requirements.

reference: Bain & Company NPS Methodology; Salesforce CRM Survey Integration; SWANA Customer Satisfaction Benchmarks

description: Date on which the most recent NPS survey response was recorded for this customer account. Used to determine survey recency and schedule follow-up surveys per the customer satisfaction program cadence.
reference: Bain & Company NPS Methodology; Salesforce CRM Survey Date

description: Indicates whether the customer has opted into electronic (paperless) billing. When True, invoices are delivered via email or the Waste Logics customer portal rather than physical mail. Supports Waste Management's sustainability initiatives.
reference: Waste Logics Customer Portal; Oracle Revenue Management Billing Preferences

description: Contractual payment terms governing when invoices are due for this customer account. Net_15/30/45/60 = days from invoice date; Prepaid = payment required before service; COD = cash on delivery. Sourced from Oracle Revenue Management and SAP S/4HANA SD module.

description: Indicates whether the customer account has active access to the Waste Logics self-service customer portal for online service scheduling, container management, invoice viewing, and service history review.

description: Customer's preferred language for communications, invoices, and service notifications. Stored as IETF BCP 47 language tag (e.g., en-US, es-MX). Used to route multilingual customer service interactions and generate localized documents.

description: Customer's preferred method for remitting payment on invoices. reference: Oracle Revenue Management Payment Configuration; PCI DSS v4.0; NACHA ACH Rules

description: Full name of the primary point of contact for the customer account. For residential accounts this is the account holder; for commercial accounts this is the designated account manager or facility contact. Used for service communications, billing inquiries, and contract management.

description: Primary email address for the customer account used for billing notifications, service alerts, and marketing communications. Sourced from Salesforce CRM and Waste Logics customer portal. Subject to opt-in/opt-out preferences.

Primary telephone number for the customer account used for service scheduling, driver communication, and customer service interactions. Stored in E.164 international format. Sourced from Salesforce CRM and Waste Logics.

Integration key linking this customer account to the corresponding Account record in Salesforce CRM. Used for bi-directional synchronization of customer profile, service requests, and contract data between the data platform and Salesforce.

description: Customer master number assigned in SAP S/4HANA (SD and FI/CO modules). Used for order-to-cash processing, accounts receivable management, and financial reporting. Links this customer account to SAP billing documents and general ledger (GL) postings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master; SAP FI/CO Business Partner; GAAP/IFRS Financial Reporting

description: Date on which the customer account was most recently placed in suspended status, typically due to non-payment or service dispute. Null for accounts not currently or previously suspended. Used for AR collections workflow and service reinstatement tracking.

description: Indicates whether the customer account holds a valid tax exemption certificate, exempting them from applicable sales or service taxes on waste management invoices. description: GPS longitude coordinate of the container's physical placement location at the service address. Enables precise geolocation for driver navigation, container audits, and lost container recovery. Sourced from Geotab Fleet Telematics or AMCS Platform.

description: Indicates whether this generator is registered as a universal waste handler under 40 CFR Part 273, managing batteries, pesticides, mercury-containing equipment, or lamps under the streamlined universal waste rules rather than full RCRA hazardous waste requirements.
reference: RCRA 40 CFR Part 273; EPA Universal Waste Rule

description: Unique identifier for the service frequency plan configuration. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: RCRA and state regulations mandate minimum pickup frequencies for hazardous waste generators. Frequency plans must reference the regulatory requirement that sets the minimum service interval, enabling
The date when this frequency plan was approved for operational use by authorized management. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
The username or identifier of the manager or administrator who approved this frequency plan for operational use. Required for plans in active status.
Indicates whether contracts using this frequency plan automatically renew at the end of the commitment period. True if auto-renewal is enabled, False if explicit renewal is required.
Defines how often customers are billed for this frequency plan: per individual pickup, weekly, monthly, quarterly, or annually. Used by billing systems to generate invoices.
The number of days advance notice required for customers to cancel or modify service under this frequency plan. Used for contract enforcement and scheduling adjustments.
The date and time when this frequency plan record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
The date when this frequency plan is no longer available for new service contracts. Existing contracts may continue beyond this date. Null indicates no planned end date. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
The date when this frequency plan becomes available for use in new service contracts and billing configurations. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.
Indicates whether customers can request additional pickups beyond the standard frequency under this plan. True if extra pickups are permitted (typically with additional charges), False otherwise.

Current lifecycle status of the frequency plan: active (available for new contracts), inactive (not available but existing contracts continue), deprecated (being phased out), pending approval (awaiting management approval), or suspended (temporarily unavailable).
The base frequency pattern for service pickups: daily, weekly, bi-weekly (every two weeks), monthly, on-call (customer-initiated), or event-based (scheduled events).
Indicates whether this frequency plan includes service pickups on Fridays. True if Friday is an eligible service day, False otherwise.

Defines how service is adjusted when a scheduled pickup falls on a recognized holiday: no service provided, shifted to next business day, shifted to prior business day, service on same day regardless, or customer chooses adjustment method.
The date and time when this frequency plan record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.
The maximum number of months a customer can commit to this frequency plan in a single contract term. Null indicates no maximum limit.
The maximum number of scheduled pickups a customer can skip in a 12-month period under this frequency plan. Null indicates no limit or skip_allowed_flag is False.
The minimum number of months a customer must commit to this frequency plan when entering a service contract. Used for contract term enforcement and early termination calculations.
Indicates whether this frequency plan includes service pickups on Mondays. True if Monday is an eligible service day, False otherwise.
Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or operational considerations related to this frequency plan. Used for internal documentation and knowledge transfer.
The time unit that defines the frequency period: day, week, month, quarter, or year. Used in conjunction with pickups_per_period to calculate service frequency.

The number of pickups scheduled within the defined frequency period. For weekly service this is typically 1, for bi-weekly this is 1 per two-week period, for daily this is 5-7 depending on operating days.
Business identifier code for the frequency plan used in billing, contracts, and operational systems. Externally visible unique code.
Detailed description of the frequency plan including service scope, pickup schedule details, and any special conditions or restrictions.
Human-readable name of the frequency plan (e.g., 'Weekly Residential Pickup', 'Bi-Weekly Commercial Service', 'Daily MSW Collection').
Indicates whether billing can be prorated for partial periods when service starts or ends mid-cycle. True if proration is allowed, False if full-period billing applies.
Indicates whether this frequency plan includes service pickups on Saturdays. True if Saturday is an eligible service day, False otherwise.

Classification of the service type this frequency plan applies to: residential curbside, commercial dumpster, industrial hauling, municipal contracts, roll-off container rental, or recycling programs.
The latest time of day (HH:MM format, 24-hour) that service pickups must be completed for this frequency plan. Used for route scheduling and SLA compliance monitoring.
The earliest time of day (HH:MM format, 24-hour) that service pickups can begin for this frequency plan. Used for route scheduling and customer communication.
Indicates whether customers can request to skip scheduled pickups under this frequency plan (e.g., for vacation holds). True if skips are permitted, False otherwise.
Indicates whether this frequency plan includes service pickups on Sundays. True if Sunday is an eligible service day, False otherwise.
Indicates whether this frequency plan includes service pickups on Thursdays. True if Thursday is an eligible service day, False otherwise.
Indicates whether this frequency plan includes service pickups on Tuesdays. True if Tuesday is an eligible service day, False otherwise.
Version number of this frequency plan configuration. Incremented with each modification to support change history and rollback capabilities.
Indicates whether this frequency plan includes service pickups on Wednesdays. True if Wednesday is an eligible service day, False otherwise.
Unique identifier for the service rate schedule record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to service.service_area. Business justification: Rate schedule applies to a specific service area. The service_rate_schedule table has a STRING column 'service_area_code' that should be replaced with a proper FK to service_area.service_area_id. This

Foreign key linking to recycling.commodity. Business justification: Recycling service rates often include revenue-share or commodity-indexed pricing tied to specific recyclable commodities (OCC, PET, aluminum). Required for dynamic pricing models and customer contract

Foreign key linking to service.container_type. Business justification: Rate schedule applies to a specific container size/type. The service_rate_schedule table has a STRING column 'container_size' that should be replaced with a proper FK to container_type.container_type_

Foreign key linking to service.frequency_plan. Business justification: Rate schedule is associated with a frequency plan. The service_rate_schedule table has a STRING column 'service_frequency' that should be replaced with a proper FK to frequency_plan.frequency_plan_id.

Reference to the specific waste management service offering (residential pickup, commercial dumpster, roll-off rental, recycling program, hazardous waste collection, WTE program) for which this rate schedule applies.
Reference to the newer rate schedule that replaces this one. Null if this is the current active version.

Foreign key linking to service.waste_stream. Business justification: Rate schedules in waste management are fundamentally waste-stream-specific: MSW tipping fees, recycling commodity adjustments, C&D surcharges, and hazardous waste handling fees all differ by waste str
Date when this rate schedule was approved by pricing authority or regulatory body. Required before status can be set to active.
Name or identifier of the pricing manager or regulatory authority who approved this rate schedule.
Core service charge amount before any surcharges, fees, or taxes. Represents the fundamental pricing for the service offering.

Unit of measure for the base rate amount. Defines how the service is priced (monthly subscription, per-pickup, tonnage-based, volume-based). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: per_month|per_pickup|per_ton|per_yard|per_gallon|per_event|per_container — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Surcharge applied when recycling or waste streams contain contamination above acceptable thresholds. Covers additional sorting and disposal costs.
Maximum allowable contamination percentage in recycling or waste stream before contamination surcharge applies. Typically 5-10% for recycling programs.
Timestamp when this rate schedule record was first created in the system.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this rate schedule. Typically USD for US operations.
Customer segment classification for which this rate schedule applies. Determines eligibility and pricing tier structure.
Date when this rate schedule expires and is no longer applicable for new contracts. Nullable for open-ended schedules.
Date when this rate schedule becomes active and applicable for billing. Supports time-bound rate versioning for annual adjustments.
Method used to calculate environmental recovery fee component. Fixed amount or percentage of base rate.
Fee charged to recover costs of environmental compliance, monitoring, and sustainability programs. May be fixed or calculated as percentage of base rate.
Rate multiplier applied to the difference between current DOE diesel price and threshold to calculate surcharge. Used when fuel_surcharge_method is index_linked.
DOE diesel price per gallon threshold above which index-linked fuel surcharge applies. Used when fuel_surcharge_method is index_linked.
Fixed fuel surcharge amount when method is fixed_amount, or base amount for percentage calculation. Null when method is index_linked or none.
Method used to calculate fuel surcharge component. Index-linked typically references DOE diesel price index with threshold and adjustment formula.
Percentage of base rate applied as fuel surcharge when method is percentage. Null for other methods.
Timestamp when this rate schedule record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.
Minimum monthly or per-service charge regardless of usage or volume. Ensures cost recovery for low-volume customers.
Free-text notes documenting special conditions, exceptions, promotional terms, or other relevant information about this rate schedule.
Fee charged per unit (ton or pound) when container or load weight exceeds the standard weight limit included in base rate.
Unique business identifier for the rate schedule used in billing systems and customer contracts. Typically follows format SERVICE-SEGMENT-GEO-VERSION.
reference: Oracle Revenue Management Rate Code Standard

description: Human-readable descriptive name of the rate schedule for business user identification and reporting purposes.
Current lifecycle status of the rate schedule. Active schedules are available for new contracts; expired or superseded schedules are retained for historical billing.
Version number for this rate schedule. Increments with each revision to support time-bound rate versioning for annual adjustments.
Fee charged to cover costs of regulatory compliance including RCRA, CERCLA, state and local permit requirements, and reporting obligations.
Applicable sales tax rate as decimal for this service area and customer segment. Null if tax_inclusive_flag is true or service is tax-exempt.
Maximum weight in tons or pounds included in the base rate before overweight fees apply. Varies by service type and container size.
Indicates whether the base rate and fees include applicable taxes (true) or taxes are calculated separately (false).
Method for calculating and applying tipping fee charges. Fixed per ton, actual cost pass-through, included in base rate, or not applicable.
Landfill or disposal facility tipping fee per ton passed through to customer. Reflects actual disposal costs at destination facility.
Unit of measure for weight-based pricing components including standard weight limit and overweight fees.
Unique identifier for the SLA definition record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to service.line. Business justification: SLA definitions in waste management are established at two levels: (1) per specific offering (already modeled via sla_definition.service_offering_id → offering) and (2) per service line (e.g., all res

Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_requirement. Business justification: Certain SLA response times (e.g., emergency hazardous spill response, missed hazardous waste pickup resolution) are directly mandated by regulatory requirements. Compliance officers must trace SLA def
Reference to the service offering to which this SLA applies (e.g., residential pickup, commercial dumpster, roll-off rental).
Indicates whether SLA breach credits are automatically applied to customer accounts (true) or require manual approval (false).
Maximum time in hours to resolve a customer complaint or service issue to customer satisfaction.
Maximum number of business days to deliver a container after order placement (e.g., 2 days for roll-off, 1 day for commercial dumpster).
Maximum number of business days to pick up a container after customer request (e.g., 1 day for standard service, same-day for premium).
Timestamp when this SLA definition record was first created in the system.
Date when this SLA definition expires or is no longer applicable. Null for open-ended SLAs.
Date when this SLA definition becomes effective and applicable to contracts.
Time in hours after which an unresolved service issue is automatically escalated to management or specialized team.
List of conditions or circumstances under which the SLA does not apply (e.g., weather emergencies, road closures, customer access issues).
Fixed dollar amount credited or refunded for late container delivery beyond committed lead time.
Percentage of service charge credited or refunded for late container delivery beyond committed lead time.
Description of how SLA compliance is measured and calculated (e.g., GPS timestamp validation, driver confirmation, customer acknowledgment).
Fixed dollar amount credited to customer account for each missed pickup that breaches SLA.
Percentage of service charge credited to customer account for each missed pickup that breaches SLA (alternative to fixed amount).
User ID or name of the person who last modified this SLA definition record.
Timestamp when this SLA definition record was last modified.
Indicates whether customers receive automated notifications for SLA breaches or service delays (true/false).
Target percentage for on-time service delivery (e.g., 98.5% for premium tier). Measured as percentage of scheduled pickups completed within service window.
Frequency at which SLA performance reports are generated and shared with customers or stakeholders.
Maximum time in hours to resolve a service issue or complaint (e.g., 48 hours for missed pickup resolution).
Maximum time in hours to respond to a service request or missed pickup complaint (e.g., 24 hours for standard residential).
End time of the committed service window for pickup or delivery (HH:MM format, e.g., 17:00 for 5 PM end).
Start time of the committed service window for pickup or delivery (HH:MM format, e.g., 07:00 for 7 AM start).
Allowable grace period in minutes beyond the service window end time before SLA breach is recorded (e.g., 30 minutes).
Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the SLA definition for system integration and reporting.
Detailed description of the SLA terms, commitments, and conditions. Includes any special provisions or exclusions.
Current lifecycle status of the SLA definition (active, inactive, draft, retired).
Business-friendly name of the SLA definition (e.g., 'Standard Residential Pickup SLA', 'Premium Commercial Service SLA').
Classification of the SLA tier (standard, premium, enterprise, custom).
User ID or name of the person who created this SLA definition record.

Approval status of the service bundle in the product governance workflow.
Name or identifier of the person who approved this service bundle for catalog inclusion.
Date when the service bundle was approved for inclusion in the active catalog.
Indicates whether contracts using this bundle automatically renew at the end of the term (True) or require explicit renewal (False).
Base price of the service bundle before any discounts, surcharges, or taxes. Expressed in USD.

Indicates whether management approval is required before this service can be sold to customers (True) or not (False). Typically True for high-value or specialized services.

description: Unit of measure for the base rate: per month, per pickup, per ton, per cubic yard, per gallon, or per event.

description: RCRA hazardous waste code(s) applicable to this service, if regulated under RCRA (e.g., D001 for ignitable waste, F001-F005 for spent solvents). Null for non-RCRA services.

Regulatory status of the service under federal and state environmental law: non-regulated, RCRA-regulated, Treatment Storage and Disposal Facility (TSDF), universal waste, or hazardous materials (Hazmat).

description: SAP SD condition type code mapped to this service code for pricing and billing integration in SAP S/4HANA.
High-level categorization of the service by customer segment (residential, commercial, industrial, municipal, special event, temporary).

Standardized alphanumeric code uniquely identifying a specific waste management service offering. Used across billing, collection, contract, and compliance systems as the universal service identifier.

Human-readable name of the service offering (e.g., Residential Weekly Curbside Collection, Commercial Front-Load Service, Roll-Off Container Rental).

Current lifecycle status of the service code: active (available for sale), inactive (temporarily unavailable), suspended (on hold), discontinued (no longer offered), or pending approval (awaiting activation).
Classification of the service by operational type: collection (pickup), recycling (MRF processing), disposal (landfill), hazardous waste handling, waste-to-energy (WTE), or composting.
Indicates whether this service is part of a sustainability or environmental stewardship program (True) or a standard service (False). Used for ESG reporting and marketing.
Unique identifier for the service line. Primary key for the service line entity.
Reference to a parent service line if this service line is a sub-category or specialization. Enables hierarchical service line taxonomy. Null for top-level service lines.

Standard base price or starting price point for this service line in USD. Actual customer pricing may vary based on contract terms, volume commitments, and market conditions. Used for financial planning and margin analysis.
Primary business unit or customer segment this service line serves. Aligns with organizational structure and market segmentation strategy.
Indicates whether this service line generates activities eligible for carbon offset credits or renewable energy certificates. True for LFG-to-energy, RNG production, and WTE operations.

description: Timestamp when this service line record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.

Indicates whether customers can self-service this service line through the online customer portal (Waste Logics). True for standard services with online scheduling and account management; false for specialized services requiring direct sales engagement.
Numeric sort order for displaying service lines in customer-facing interfaces, reports, and catalogs. Lower numbers appear first.

description: Minimum CDL class and endorsements required for drivers operating this service line. Class A for combination vehicles, Class B for heavy straight trucks, HAZMAT endorsement for hazardous waste transport.

description: Date when this service line was discontinued or sunset. Null for active service lines. Used for historical reporting and contract transition planning.

description: Primary fleet vehicle types required to deliver this service line. description: Total number of violations or notices of non-compliance issued against this permit since its effective date. Used to track compliance performance and regulatory risk.
reference: EPA Enforcement and Compliance History Online (ECHO), State Environmental Quality Department Violation Tracking

description: Unique identifier for the permit condition. Primary key.

The time period over which the limit is calculated or averaged (e.g., hourly, daily, monthly, annual, 30-day rolling). Null for instantaneous limits.

description: Description of any phased compliance schedule or milestone dates specified in the condition (e.g., interim limits, construction deadlines, operational milestones).
reference: EPA Compliance Schedule Guidance, RCRA Corrective Action

description: Current compliance status of this condition based on the most recent monitoring or inspection. Updated as monitoring data is received.

Full text description of the permit condition as written in the permit document. Specifies the enforceable requirement in detail.

Classification of the permit condition by regulatory purpose. Determines the nature of the enforceable requirement. description: The date on which this permit condition becomes enforceable. May differ from the permit effective date if the condition has a delayed or phased implementation.
The regulatory enforcement priority level assigned to this condition. High-priority conditions receive heightened scrutiny and faster enforcement response.

description: The date on which this condition ceases to be enforceable. Null if the condition remains in effect for the life of the permit.

Indicates whether this condition is designated as critical or high-priority for compliance tracking. Violations of critical conditions may trigger immediate enforcement action.
The date of the most recent compliance verification, monitoring event, or inspection for this condition.
The date of the most recent violation or exceedance of this condition. Null if no violations have occurred.
Specifies whether the limit is a maximum allowable value, minimum required value, range, or time-averaged value. Defines how compliance is measured.
The unit of measure for the limit value (e.g., ppm, mg/L, tons per day, decibels, cubic meters per hour). Required when limit_value is populated.

description: The numeric threshold, limit, or target value specified in the condition (e.g., emission rate, discharge concentration, operational parameter). Null if the condition is qualitative.

description: The user or system account that last modified this permit condition record. Audit trail field.

The required method, protocol, or technology for monitoring compliance with this condition (e.g., EPA Method 9, continuous emission monitoring system, grab sampling, visual inspection).

description: The scheduled date for the next compliance verification, monitoring event, or inspection for this condition. Used for proactive compliance management.
Free-text field for additional context, clarifications, or operational notes related to this permit condition. Used for internal compliance management.

description: Number of days after the monitoring period or event by which the report must be submitted. Null if no specific deadline is defined.
